Grease Muffin Pan. Put butter or margarine in a 4-quart microwave-able bowl sprayed with no-stick spray on HIGH 3 seconds or until melted. Add marshmallows, Stir to coat. Microwave 1-1/2 minutes or until marshmallows are melted and smooth, stirring after 45 seconds. Add cereal. Stir with wooden spoon to coat well.

item.title: Never-fail Pie Crust
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5 cups flour
  • 2 tbsp white sugar
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 2 tbsp vinegar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 lb lard

Instructions

Sift dry ingredients. Add lard and cut into the flour until lard is in very small pieces. In an 8 oz cup, beat egg. Add vinegar and fill cup with cold water. Combine wet and dry mixtures. Use plenty of flour when rolling pie crust. Store unused pie crust in covered container in the refrigerator or separate into smaller batches and freeze.

item.title: New Potatoes with Herbs – (aboriginal Cooking – Gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb tiny new potatoes (about 20)
  • 1 Tbs chopped chives
  • 1 Tbs chopped fresh tarragon/basil
  • 1 Tbs lemon juice
  • 2 Tbs olive oil or vegetable oil
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

In saucepan, boil unpeeled potatoes until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain. Add tarragon, chives, lemon juice and oil. Add salt and pepper to taste.

Makes 4 servings.

item.title: No-bake Peanut Butter Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter or margarine
  • ½ cup lightly-packed brown sugar
  • ½ cup corn syrup.

Instructions

Mix and bring to a boil.

Add: 1 cup peanut butter chocolate chips. Melt over medium heat til smooth. Remove from heat and add 6 cups corn flakes. Mix well.

Pour into a greased 9 x 13 pan. Melt rest of chipits and add some chocolate chipits to make a cup. Spread on squares.

item.title: Oatmeal Pancakes – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups rolled oats
  • 2 cups 2% milk
  • ½ cup white flour
  • ½ cup whole wheat flour
  • 1-1/2 Tbsp brown sugar
  • 1-1/2 Tbsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 3 eggs (beaten)
  • ¼ cup melted butter/margarine/Crisco

Instructions

Mix oats and milk. Set aside until milk is absorbed. Flour mixture: combine flour, sugar, baking powder, salt and cinnamon. Mix well. Add eggs and melted butter to oats mixture. Beat well. Add oat mixture to flour mixture, mixing until smooth. Pour batter by ¼ cupfuls onto preheated lightly greased griddle. Bake until fluffy and bubbles break on surface. Pour natural maple syrup over and enjoy. Makes 12 – 15 pancakes.

item.title: Onion Sauteed Potatoes
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Preparation: 5 minutes
  • Cook: 20 minutes
  • 3 Tbsp Margarine or butter
  • 4 large potatoes, cut into chunks
  • 1 pouch Loney’s onion soup mix
  • 1/3 cup water
  • 2 Tbsp fresh parsley, chopped
  • 1 Tbsp lemon juice
  • 1 tsp lemon rind, grated
  • ½ tsp dried rosemary leaves
  • ¼ tsp black pepper
  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil

Instructions

In large, nonstick skillet, melt margarine or butter. Add potatoes; cook, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender and golden. In small bowl, combine onion soup mix, water, parsley, lemon juice, lemon rind, rosemary and pepper. Add to potatoes; stir and cook over high heat until po0tatoes are coated and liquid evaporated. Stir in oil and cook until potatoes are crisp.

item.title: Patty Bartlett’s Noodle Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 85-gr pkg Ramen noodles
  • 3 Tbsp vinegar
  • (the ones w/the flavour packets)
  • 1 Tbsp sugar
  • 1 cup almonds
  • 2 cups cooked chicken
  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 2 green onions, chopped
  • ½ cup oil
  • 1 small cabbage, shredded

Instructions

Drop noodles into boiling water for 1 to 1-1/2 minutes. Drain and cool. Saute almonds in butter until toasted. Toss all ingredients together.

Sounds weird; tastes good!

item.title: Peanut Butter “sucre a La Crème” – Lucia Beaudoin
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup margarine
  • ½ cup corn syrup or honey
  • ¾ cup peanut butter
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ tsp salat
  • 1 lb or 3-3/4 cups icing sugar

Instructions

Mix first 5 ingredients and gradually add icing sugar. Spread in greased or buttered 8 x 8” pan, cut and refrigerate.

I haven’t tried this recipe yet but Lucia assures me it’s a prize-winner. And no cooking!

item.title: Peanut Butter Balls - (gail Leblanc – Metis Cookhouse)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup peanut butter
  • 1 cup Rice Krispies
  • 1 Tbsp butter or margarine
  • 1 cup icing sugar
  • ½ cup nuts (optional)
  • 1 cup coconut so roll balls into

Instructions

Cream peanut butter, icing sugar and butter. Add Rice Krispies and nuts. Shape into balls and roll in coconut.

item.title: Peanut Butter Chocolate Balls – Isabelle Rainville
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Mix ½” slice off 1# butter
  • 1 cup icing sugar
  • ¾ cup peanut butter
  • ¾ cup chopped walnuts.

Instructions

Chill for some time; then make small balls. Return to freezer.

MELT: 4 squares semi-sweet chocolate and ½ block Para wax in double boiler.

DIP: chocolate balls in mixture and cool.

item.title: Peanut Butter Cups (native Cuisine)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup margarine, melted
  • 4 cups icing sugar

Instructions

1-1/2 cups peanut butter 1 cup graham wafer crumbs

Topping: 1 pkg semi-sweet chocolate chips

Mix the above ingredients together. Mixture will be rather dry. Spray cookie sheet with Pam, then press onto the cookie sheet. Melt 1 pkg of semi-sweet chocolate chips and spread on top. Chill for 10 minutes. Remove and cut into squares. Chill for about 2 hours before removing from cookie sheet.

item.title: Peanut Butter Marshmallow Goodies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup peanut butter
  • ½ cup butter of margarine
  • 12-oz pkg butterscotch chips
  • ½ bag coloured mini marshmallows

Instructions

Melt first 3 ingredients. Cool and add marshmallows. Place in a lightly buttered 9”x 9”pan. Chill in refrigerator. Cut into 1 inch squares.

item.title: Peanut Crackers
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup maple syrup
  • ½ cup peanut butter
  • ¼ cup sugar
  • 3 cups Kellogg’s corn flakes
  • ¼ cup brown sugar

Instructions

Combine first 3 ingredients and bring to a boil over moderate heat. Stir in peanut butter, Remove from heat and stir in corn flakes. Using a heaped tablespoon, drop onto greased cookie sheet. Chill until firm.

item.title: Pearl’s Cucumber Relish
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Peel and remove seeds from cucumbers if desired and put through food chopper. Should make about 12 – 13 cups of cukes and onions mixed.
  • Add 3 tbsp salt and let stand for 4 hours.
  • Press water out – Mix 3 cups white sugar, ½ to 2/3 cup flour, ½ tsp cayenne pepper, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp dry mustard, 1-1/2 cups water, 2 cups vinegar and heat mixture to a boil. Boil on low for 10 minutes.

Instructions

NOTE: ½ tsp cayenne makes it pretty peppy! But Good!

item.title: Pickled Beans (nugget Cook Book)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6-qt basket string beans (to make 16 cups) cut into 1” pieces
  • 3 cups vinegar
  • 3 cups brown sugar
  • ½ cup dry mustard
  • ½ cup flour (1/4 cup for thinner sauce)
  • 1-1/2 tsp turmeric
  • 1 tsp celery seed
  • 1 tsp salt

Instructions

Wash, cut and cook beans in boiling salted water for 15 minutes. Drain.

Make a sauce of the other ingredients and add cooked beans. Cook for 5 minutes. Bottle and seal.

item.title: Pickled Eggs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups white vinegar
  • 1-1/2 cups water
  • 1-1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp pepper berries
  • ½ tsp celery seed

Instructions

Boil vinegar solution for 10 minutes and cool. Hard-boil 1 dozen eggs, dash into cold water to cool rapidly and remove shells. Pack eggs in quart jars. Pour cold vinegar solution over to cover, then seal.

Let stand at least 2 days before using. These keep well at room temperature for a few days but should be refrigerated if kept more than 1 week.

item.title: Pizza Sauce
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tin 213-ml tomato sauce
  • ½ tsp dried basil
  • ½ tsp dried oregano
  • ½ tsp minced garlic
  • ¼ tsp dried thyme
  • Pinch of rosemary
  • Pinch of dried dill

Instructions

In a bowl or a jar, combine all ingredients.

item.title: Plain Dandelion Wine
item.description:
Instructions

1ST WEEK 4 quarts of dandelion flowers, cleaned of ALL their green 4 quarts of boiling water

2ND WEEK 4 oranges cut in ¼” slices 4 lemons cut in ¼” slices 1 cup of white raisins, finely chopped 6 cups of cane sugar 1 package of dry granulated yeast

Put dandelion blossoms in canner kettle and pour the boiling water over them. Let sand in a warm place for one week. Stir twice a day if possible.

item.title: Popular Brownie Recipe
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • ½ cup (8 tbsp) cocoa
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup oil
  • 4 eggs
  • ¼ cup cold water
  • ½ cup (or more) chopped walnuts

Instructions

Put all ingredients in beater bowl in order given ending with walnuts and beat on low speed or by hand until smooth. Do not beat too long. Grease a 9 x 13 pan. Smooth batter in it and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes – no longer. Ice if desired.

item.title: Pork Cassoulet (from Metis Newspaper)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Cook Time:- 1 Hr. & 15 Min.
  • Prep. Time:- 10 Min.
  • 2 lbs. Pork loin or sirloin roast, boneless
  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • Salt & Pepper
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 2 garlic cloves, minces
  • 3 cups cranberry juice cocktail
  • 2 – 19 oz/540 ml) cans white kidney beans
  • 2 Tbsp tomato paste
  • 1 Tbsp dry mustard
  • ½ tsp thyme
  • 1 bay leaf
  • fresh parsley, chopped
  • 1 cup fresh or frozen cranberries, chopped (optional)

Instructions

Cut pork into ¾” cubes. Heat oil in large Dutch oven over medium heat. Season Pork cubes with salt and pepper and brown. Transfer to a plate and set aside. Brown onions and garlic until soft, then add cranberry cocktail. Stir to scrape up browned bits; bring to a boil and simmer for 5 – 7 minutes to reduce liquid.

item.title: Potato Pizza (serves 4)
item.description:
Instructions

This recipe is for microwave ovens but could be prepared in the same manner and used in a conventional oven.

1-1/2 cups mashed potatoes ½ cup chopped green pepper ¼ cup dry bread crumbs ¼ lb bacon slices, chopped 1 tsp dried mixed Italian herbs 1 14-oz tin tomatoes, drained Salt and pepper 1-1/2 cups grated cheese 1 small tin anchovy fillets(optional) Few ripe olives (optional)

Mix together the mashed potato, bread crumbs and herbs. Season lightly and press into a round shallow glass pan. Mix together the onion, green peppers, bacon and cook in the microwave for 5 minutes (until vegetables are softened and bacon is lightly cooked). Arrange this mixture on the potato base. Top with drained tomatoes and grated cheese. Arrange the anchovy fillets in a lattice pattern on top and cook in microwave for 4 minutes or until heated thoroughly and the cheese has melted. Place an olive in each square of the pizza before serving.

item.title: Prize Butter Tarts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup butter
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 2 tbsp milk

Instructions

Melt butter, mix all ingredients and put in tart shells. Bake at 350 degrees until pastry is browned – approx. 30 minutes.

item.title: Pudding-cake (kraft Winter 2006)
item.description:
Instructions

Prep. Time: 15 min. Total: 1 hour 35 Min.

1 pkg (2-layer size) yellow cake mix 2 cups cold milk 1-1/4 cup water 2 pkg (4-serving size each) Jell-o Lemon 1/3 cup sugar (optional) Instant Pudding 2 Tbsp icing sugar (optional)

Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Prepare cake mix as directed on package. Pour batter into greased 13 x 9-inch baking dish. Pour milk and water into large bowl. Add dry pudding mixes and granulated sugar, if using. Beat with wire whisk 2 minutes or until well blended. Pour over cake batter in dish. Place baking dish on baking sheet to catch dripping, as mixture does bubble. Bake 55 min. to 1 hour or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool in pan 20 minutes. (Mixture will thicken slightly as it cool.). Sprinkle with icing sugar, if using. Spoon onto serving dishes. Serve warm; garnish with raspberries, if desired. Makes 16 servings. Serving suggestions: There’s no need to reheat leftovers – this cake is also delicious as a cold treat. The lemon filling transforms from saucy and gooey to thick and custard-like. Yummy!

item.title: Pumpkin Bread – Gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3-1/2 cups flour
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp cloves
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 2-2/3 cups sugar
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp baking soda
  • 2 cups canned pumpkin
  • 2.3 cup cooking oil
  • 4 eggs – beaten
  • 2/3 cups water
  • 1 cup walnuts, optional

Instructions

Mix all dry ingredients. Add all wet ingredients and mix well together. Pour into 2 greased 2-pound load pans. Bake approx. 1 hour at 350 degrees F.

item.title: Pumpkin Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • l tbsp pumpkin pie spice
  • l tbsp all-purpose flour
  • l/2 tsp salt
  • 2 eggs
  • l-l/2 cups pumpkin
  • l-l/2 cups milk (can be part cream)

Instructions

Mix sugar, spice, flour and salt. Beat eggs until light and fluffy. Stir in pumpkin. Add dry ingredients, mixing well. Gradually stir in milk. Blend thoroughly. Bake in pie pastry in a 375 degree oven for 45 – 55 minutes.

item.title: Quiche Crust (rice)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • l-l/2 cups cooked brown rice
  • 2 tbsp butter, softened
  • 2 tbsp chopped onion
  • l/4 tsp basil
  • l egg, slightly beaten

Instructions

Combine ingredients. Press into bottom and sides of greased 9” pie plate. Use any quiche filling. Tuna tastes especially good in this crust.

item.title: Quick Chocolate Mousse - Cw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 14-oz Eagle Brand
  • 1 pkg (4-oz) instant chocolate pudding mix
  • 1 cup cold water
  • Additional whipped cream
  • 1 cup whipping cream, whipped

Instructions

In a large mixing bowl, combine milk, pudding mix and water. Beat until well mixed. Chill 5 minutes. Fold in whipped cream. Spoon into individual serving dishes and garnish with additional whipped cream.

Yield: 4 – 6 servings

item.title: Quick Sweet and Sour Sauce
item.description:
Instructions

For hamburger, pork, chicken, etc.

2 oz catsup 6 oz sugar 8 oz water 2 tsp corn starch 2 oz vinegar

Boil first four ingredients until clear. Mix cornstarch in 2 oz water. Add to sauce to thicken.

item.title: Quick Vegetable Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 quart chicken broth or stock
  • 1 16-oz can tomatoes
  • ½ cup chopped onion
  • ½ cup chopped cabbage
  • 1 10-oz pkg frozen mixed veggies,
  • ½ tsp dried basil
  • thawed
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • Dash sugar
  • ¼ cup uncooked elbow macaroni
  • 1 cup leftover cubed cooked chicken
  • or roast beef

Instructions

In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, bring broth, tomatoes, onion, cabbage, vegetables and seasonings to a boil. Add macaroni and simmer 10 minutes or until tender. Add meat and heat through.

item.title: Raspberry Chocolate Squares
item.description:
Instructions

These decadent treats have soft coconutty centers, a touch of raspberry and a luscious layer of chocolate on top.

1-1/4 cups all-purpose flour 1/3 cup granulated sugar ½ cup butter 1 egg yolk 1/3 cup raspberry jam 1 can (300 ml) sweetened condensed milk 2 cups sweetened flaked coconut 5 oz semisweet chocolate 1 tbsp butter

In bowl, mix together flour and sugar, using pastry blender or 2 knives, cut in butter until mixture is roughly the size of peas. Stir in egg yolk, mix thoroughly.

item.title: Raspberry Vinaigrette (community Voices)
item.description:
Instructions

INGREDIENTS 1 cup virgin olive oil ½ cup raspberry vinegar 2 tsp dry mustard 3 Tbsp dried , finely crushed onion flakes 4 Tbsp liquid honey (more or less to suit taste) Fresh herbs to taste (basil, dill, mint or your choice) Salt & pepper to taste

DIRECTIONS Put ingredients in a bottle and shake well before using. Store dressing in fridge.

This vinaigrette is wonderful sprinkled on green salads or dribbled over steamed vegetables.

item.title: Raspberry Vinegar
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pint raspberries
  • 2 cups champagne vinegar
  • ¼ cup sugar

Instructions

Set aside ¼ cup berries. Place remaining in medium bowl. With fork, crush berries slightly. In medium saucepan, bring to a boil vinegar and sugar. Pour over crushed berries, cover and steep 48 hours. Place whole berries in sterilized bottles. Strain vinegar through cheesecloth, pour into bottles.

item.title: Raspberry Vinegar #2 (community Voices)
item.description:
Instructions

If you like your summer salads light and refreshing, all you need to do s splash on a dash of raspberry vinegar. Sprinkle it straight from the bottle or use it as a base for Raspberry Vinaigrette.

INGREDIENTS: 5 cups apple cider vinegar (or red wine vinegar) 5 handfuls of raspberries, washed

DIRECTIONS: Put berries into bottle or jar. Heat vinegar until boiling point. Pour over raspberries, leaving about one inch head space. Cork or cap and set in a cool dark place for several days before using.

item.title: Ratatouille
item.description:
Ingredients

  • About ½ cup olive oil
  • 2 large onions, sliced
  • 2 large cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 medium eggplant, cut in ½” cubes
  • 6 medium zucchini, sliced thick
  • 2 green or red peppers, seeded,cut in chunks
  • About 2 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp basil leaves
  • ½ cup minced parsley
  • 4 large tomatoes, cut in chunks

Instructions

Heat ¼ cup of the oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Add onions and garlic and cook, stirring until onions are limp but not browned. Stir in the eggplant, zucchini, peppers, 2 tsp salt, basil and minced parsley. Add a little of the oil as needed to keep the vegetables from sticking. Cover pan and cook over moderate heat (about 30 minutes). Stir occasionally, using a large spatula and turning the vegetables to help preserve their shape. If mixture becomes quite soupy during this time, remove cover to allow some of the moisture to escape.

item.title: Rhubarb Cream Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups sugar
  • 4 tbsp flour
  • ¼ tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • 2 beaten eggs
  • 3 cups cut-up rhubarb
  • 1 unbaked pie shell

Instructions

Blend sugar, flour, nutmeg and butter. Add beaten eggs. Beat until smooth. Pour over rhubarb in a 9-inch pastry lined pan. Top with pastry if desired. Bake 10 minutes at 450 degrees, then reduce heat to 350 degrees and bake for 30 minutes.

NOTE: This is the original recipe which I copied. However, I bake this pie for about one hour at 375 degrees.

item.title: Rhubarb Crisp
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups rhubarb
  • ½ cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp cinnamon

Instructions

Mix and place in bottom of buttered dish.

1 cup rolled oats ½ cup margarine ½ cup flour 1 cup brown sugar

Mix till crumbly and place on top of rhubarb.

Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.

Yum, Yum delicious!

item.title: Rhubarb Dessert
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 1 cup flour
  • 1-1/2 cup white sugar
  • 3-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • 2 tbsp shortening
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ cup milk

Instructions

Mix the above ingredients and put in 13 x 9 inch pan. Cover with 4 or 5 cups cut rhubarb. Sprinkle with 1 cup sugar and pour 2 cups boiling water over top. Bake at 350 to 400 degrees F. for 30 minutes (or until top is golden brown). Serve with whipped cream.

item.title: Ribbon Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup melted butter or margarine
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup brown sugar, packed
  • 2 cups quick cooking oatmeal
  • ½ tsp vanilla

Instructions

Mix last four ingredients, add butter and mix. Place on cookie sheet – spread out so sheet is evenly. Bake for approximately 5 minutes at 350 degrees – or until mixture is bubbling. Remove and let sit for 5 minutes. Cut in squares.

item.title: Rice and Zucchini Casserole
item.description:
Instructions

Excellent with beef, turkey or ham. Can be reheated many times.

2 cups cooked rice 6 medium zucchini, sliced 2 large sliced onions ½ lb sharp cheese, grated 2 cans mushroom soup

Cook onion and zucchini over low heat until soft with butter of margarine in fry pan (cover and stir occasionally). Put a layer of rice, layer of vegetable mixture, some of the cheese and part of mushroom soup. Repeat in layers. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes, or until bubbly and top is browned. Serves 12

item.title: Rice Krispie Squares (large Quantity)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter or margarine
  • 10 cups miniature marshmallows
  • 3 tsp vanilla extract (optional)
  • or 80 regular (2 250g pkgs)
  • 11 cups Rice Krispies

Instructions

In large saucepan over low heat, melt margarine. Add marshmallows. Stir until melted and well blended. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla. Add cereal, stirring until coated. Using lightly-buttered spatula, press into buttered 13 x 9 pan. Cool.

item.title: Rice Krispies Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup margarine or butter
  • 5 cups miniature marshmallows
  • (or 40 regular marshmallows)
  • (250 gram pkg)
  • l/2 tsp vanilla
  • 6 cups rice crispies

Instructions

In large saucepan over low heat, melt margarine. Add marshmallows, stir until melted and well blended. Remove from heat Stir in vanilla. Add cereal, stirring until well coated. Using lightly buttered spatula, press into buttered 13 x 9 “pan. Cool.. Cut into squares. YIELD; 24 – 2”squares.

item.title: Rice Pudding, Mom’s Creamy – (hazelwood Cookbook)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup cooked rice
  • 1 Tbsp butter
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 eggs
  • ½ cup raisins
  • 1/3 cup sugar

Instructions

Combine rice, milk, eggs and sugar in double boiler. Heat until mixture coats metal spoon. Add remaining ingredients and let sit for 30 minutes.

item.title: Richard Belanger’s Sugar Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 250-gr whipping cream
  • ¼ - ½ cup of Carnation milk
  • 1 full cup brown sugar
  • ¼ cup flour
  • Vanilla and nutmeg

Instructions

Mix all together at minimum speed and then at full speed until well blended.

Pour into uncooked pie shell and bake at 350 until “you decide it’s cooked”.

P.S. I have not made this yet but I understand it’s a prize winner. When I do make this pie, I will time it and enter it on this sheet.

item.title: Richard’s Excellent Fudge
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb butter
  • 1 lg tin Carnation (whole milk)
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 kg brown sugar
  • 1 kg icing sugar

Instructions

Mix well, Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring a lot. Boil for no more than 12 minutes. Take off heat. Add 1 kg icing sugar. Mix well and quickly pour into a well-buttered 9 x 13 pan INSTRUCTIONS AS WRITTEN BY RICHARD BELANGER:- In a big pot on stove, let melt 1 lb of butter. When melted, add 1 big can of Carnation and a little bit of vanilla.

item.title: Rocky Road Fudge
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups (12-oz pkg) chocolate chips
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 can Eagle Brand milk
  • 2 cups peanuts
  • 2 tbsp margarine
  • 1 10-oz pkg mini marshmallows

Instructions

MELT: Chips with Eagle Brand and margarine over low heat, or in top of a double boiler. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla and nuts. Cool to lukewarm.

Place marshmallows in a large bowl; fold in chocolate mixture. Spread in a waxed paper-lined 9”x 13”pan. Chill until firm, about 2 hours. Remove from pan and peel off waxed paper. Cut into squares.

item.title: Roly’s Barley Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs soup bones
  • 8 cups water (to start with)
  • 1 cup barley
  • ¾ tsp savory
  • 3 large carrots, diced
  • Pinch garlic salt
  • 3 medium onions, diced
  • ½ tsp oregano
  • 4 stalks celery with leaves, diced
  • 28-oz tin stewed tomatoes
  • 2 tsp parsley
  • Salt & Pepper to taste

Instructions

Boil water and bones for about ½ hour. Add barley and spices; let boil for about 1 hour. Add more water, if needed. Add vegetables and tomatoes; let cook until vegetables are ready. Sit and enjoy.

item.title: Russian Tea
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups orange Tang
  • ½ cup dry lemonade mix
  • 1 cup instant tea
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 2 tsp cloves

item.title: Ruth’s Marinated Cucumbers and Onions – (gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 1 or 2 Tbsp sugar
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp celery seed
  • 1 large cucumber, thinly slice (about 3 cups)
  • ½ cup thinly sliced onions

Instructions

Combine marinade ingredients. Add cucumber and onions and toss to coat. Cover and chill in fridge for four hours or up to five days. Stir occasionally. Enjoy!

item.title: Ruth’s Tea Biscuits - (gail Leblanc – Metis Cookhouse)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • ¼ cup sugar
  • 1 Tbsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ¼ cup oil or shortening
  • ¾ cup milk

Instructions

Mix flour, sugar, baking powder and salt. Cut in the oil; add milk and stir with a fork to make a soft dough.

Gather into a ball and knead several times until smooth. Roll out to ½” thick and cut with a small round cookie cutter ( about 2” diameter).

item.title: Salad Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 can Eagle Brand milk
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 can vinegar
  • salt and pepper
  • 1 tsp dry mustard

Instructions

Mix 2 eggs, add Eagle Brand milk and dry mustard; mix thoroughly. Then add salt and pepper to taste. Mix thoroughly and add 1 can vinegar. This is an excellent dressing for potato salad and will keep in the fridge for quite a while.

item.title: Salmon Relish Sandwich Filling
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tin 3-3/4 oz salmon
  • 2 tbsp sweet green relish
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • 1 tbsp mayo

Instructions

HARD-COOKED EGG SANDWISH FILLING

4 hard-cooked eggs ½ cup mayo ½ tsp salt 2 tbsp green or red hot dog relish 1 tsp chopped green onion

item.title: Salmon Steak “en Papillote” – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • For oven or BBQ
  • MARINADE:
  • 1 Tbsp brown sugar
  • ½ tsp chopped dill
  • 1 Tbsp Dijon mustard
  • Juice of half a lemon
  • Salt & pepper to taste
  • Half a lemon, thinly sliced
  • 2 Tbsp soft margarine
  • Aluminum foil
  • 1/3 cup dry white or red wine

Instructions

Stir together brown sugar, dill, mustard, lemon juice, salt and pepper Brush over salmon and top each steak with lemon slices Brush margarine on half of two sheets of foil. On each sheet place 2 salmon steaks side by side and baste with wine. Wrap up and seal like a turnover. Cook either in your oven or on the BBQ at 375 F. for approx 15 minutes.

item.title: Scalloped Potatoes – (elsie Smiley)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 raw potatoes
  • 3 Tbsp flour
  • 1 can cream of mushroom soup or
  • 2 to 4 Tbsp butter
  • cream of chicken soup
  • 2 cans of milk
  • 1 onion
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Scrub, peel and thinly slice the potatoes. Place half of them in a thickly buttered baking dish, salt and pepper, and sprinkle with the flour. Add soup and onions, butter and the rest of the potatoes, salt and pepper and butter.

item.title: Scalloped Potatoes, Cheezy
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cans cream of celery soup
  • 2 medium cooking onions, chopped
  • ½ cup milk
  • potatoes to fill a good-sized casserole dish
  • Approx. l lb medium cheddar cheese
  • Salt – approximately 1 tsp.
  • Pepper to taste

Instructions

Peel and wash potatoes. Place alternate slices of potatoes and finely chopped onions in casserole. In a separate bowl, combine soup, milk, salt and pepper. Pour over potatoes. Grate cheese and sprinkle approximately ½ between layers. Top with remaining cheese and small bite-size pieces of butter, a dash paprika. Bake in a moderate oven at 350 degrees, covered, in casserole dish, for approximately 1 hour. Then remove cover and bake an additional ½ hour.

item.title: Seven-up Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 can 7-Up
  • ½ bag marshmallows
  • 1 large pkg lime Jello
  • 1 19-oz can crushed pineapple
  • 8 oz pkg cream cheese
  • 1 pkg Dream Whip

Instructions

Dissolve over low heat marshmallows in one can of 7-Up. Add lime Jello and stir until dissolved. Add cream cheese and beat until smooth. Add pineapple. When partially set, beat Cream Whip and fold in. Mold and chill.

item.title: Shining Tree Corn Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour
  • 1 cup cornmeal
  • 3 Tbsp white sugar
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 beaten egg
  • ¼ cup oil

Instructions

Mix. Put in hot greased frying pan. Lower heat and bake 20 – 25 minutes.

item.title: Slu8h
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tin Honeydew Melon or Strawberry Daiquiri
  • 1 tin frozen orange juice
  • 2 cups cold tea
  • 12 oz Lemon Gin

Instructions

Mix. Freeze. Scoop out and mix with Gingerale, 7-up or Sprite.

item.title: Sonia’s Absolutely Fabulous Dessert
item.description:
Ingredients

  • (1 cup chopped walnuts or pecans
  • 1 Tbsp sugar )
  • ( 1 cup flour
  • ½ cup butter ) 1st Part
  • 1 large tub Cool Whip
  • 1 cup icing sugar
  • 8 oz (250 gr) cream cheese
  • 1 small pkg instant chocolate pudding
  • 1 small pkg instant vanilla pudding
  • 4 cups milk (Separated)
  • 1 Jersey milk chocolate bar

Instructions

Cream together first four ingredients: press in a 9” x 13” pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes. Cool well.

item.title: Sour Cream Apple Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup dairy sour cream
  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • 3 tbsp all-purpose flour
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 egg
  • 4 cups peeled, cored & diced tart apples
  • 1 9”unbaked pastry shell
  • 1/3 cup packed brown sugar
  • ½ cup all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup butter

Instructions

Beat together cream, sugar, flour, salt, vanilla and egg. Add apples. Pour into unbaked pastry shell. Bake in 375 degree oven for 25 minutes.

item.title: Sour Cream Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup thick sour cream
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 cup raisin
  • 1-1/2 tbsp flour
  • 2 eggs, separated
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • Few grains nutmeg
  • Few grains salt

Instructions

Mix together raisins, sugar, flour, salt and spices. Add to sour cream. Mix and add well beaten egg yolks. Whip egg whites until stiff; fold into mixture. Pour mixture into an unbaked pie shell. Bake in a moderate oven – 350 degrees – until filling is firm enough not to stick to a knife inserted into center of pie- about 50 minutes.

item.title: Southern Pecan Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • 1/3 cup melted butter or margarine
  • 2/3 cups sugar
  • 1 cup pecan halves
  • 1 cup dark corn syrup
  • 9-inch unbaked pastry shell

Instructions

Beat eggs thoroughly with sugar, dash of salt, corn syrup and melted butter. Add pecans. Pour into unbaked pastry shell. Bake in moderate oven at 350 degrees for 50 minutes or until knife inserted halfway between outside and center of filling comes out clean. Cool.

item.title: Sparkling Strawberry Pie - Cw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 pkg (3 oz ea.) raspsberry or strawberry-
  • 2 cups boiling water
  • flavoured Jello
  • 3-4 cups frozen whole strawberries
  • Whipped topping
  • 1 prepared pie crust

Instructions

In a mixing bow, dissolve gelatin in water. Add frozen berries and allow to set a few minutes to thicken. When partially set, spoon into pie shell. Refrigerate. Garnish with whipped topping.

item.title: Speedy Bean Salad - Cw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 2/3 cup vinegar
  • 1/3 cup vegetable oil
  • 1 tsp salt
  • Dash pepper
  • 1 15-1/2 oz kidney beans, rinsed &
  • 1 15-oz garbanzo beans, rinsed &
  • drained
  • drained
  • ½ medium onion, chopped
  • 1 15-oz great northern beans, rinsed
  • ½ cup chopped green pepper
  • & drained

Instructions

In a bowl, combine sugar, vinegar, oil, salt and pepper. Stir in all remaining ingredients. Let marinate until serving time.

Yield: 6 servings

item.title: Speedy Chocolate Fudge
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ (12 oz pkg) semi-sweet chocolate chips
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 – 15 oz tin sweetened condensed milk
  • ½ cup chopped nuts
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Melt chocolate over low heat stirring. Remove from heat and stir in sweetened condensed milk, salt, vanilla and nuts. Pour into a waxed paper lined 8”square pan. Spread evenly smoothing surface. Refrigerate until firm, about 2 hours. Turn candy out on board and peel off paper. With a sharp knife, cut fudge into desired size pieces.

item.title: Spiced Pickled Beets (purity Cook Book)
item.description:
Instructions

Select small young beets 1” to 1-1/2” in diameter. Scrub thoroughly leaving root and 1” to 2” of stem. Cook until just tender. Dip in cold water and remove skins.

One 6-quart basket will yield about 12 cups of prepared beets. For this quantity, prepare the following syrup.

Combine in saucepan 2 cups vinegar (can be cider if desired) 2 cups sugar 2 cups water 1 Tbsp salt 1 tsp ground allspice

item.title: Spinach Salad W/strawberry & Poppy Seed Dressing
item.description:
Instructions

To prepare dressing, whisk together 1/3 cup mayonnaise 2 Tbsp milk 2 Tbsp sugar 1 Tbsp white wine vinegar 1 Tbsp poppy seed 1/8 tsp salt

Combine in a bowl 8 cups baby spinach or torn romaine lettuce 2 cups sliced strawberries ¼ cup slivered almonds, toasted

Toss with dressing to coat. Serve immediately. Serves 8.

item.title: Sucre a La Crème
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups brown sugar
  • 1-1/2 cups white sugar
  • 1 cup Carnation Evaporated Milk – undiluted

Instructions

Bring the Carnation milk and the sugars to a boil. Boil to 240 F., stirring continually till a soft ball stage is reached. (Dropping a few drops in cold water.) Let mixture cool slightly, then stir till mixture thickens – this happens quickly. Pour mixture in a buttered 8” square pan.

Cut into squares before it is completely cooled. ½ to ¾ cup of chopped nuts may be added before cooling.

item.title: Sugar Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup evaporated milk or cream
  • 2 eggs
  • 4 Tbsp flour
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Beat eggs with milk. Add remaining ingredients and blend well. Pour into an unbaked 9-inch pie shell. Bake at 350 degrees for approximately 40 minutes

item.title: Sugared Nuts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb each cashews, whole almonds and pecan halves
  • ½ cup unsalted butter or margarine, melted
  • 2 egg whites, lightly beaten
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 tbsp ground cinnamon

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350 degrees. In large bowl, combine nuts. In medium bowl, whisk together remaining ingredients, pour over nuts. Mix, spread on baking sheet. Bake 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until crisp. Cool before packing.

Makes 3 pounds.

item.title: Sunchokes (jerusalem Artichoke) – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • These knotty tubers are the roots of a variety of the sunflower plant and were discovered by Champlain in 1605 in the gardens of the Indians in Cape Cod. They can be served raw or cooked. They are superb in salads and add an interesting touch to soups when thin slices are floated on top.

Instructions

They have a delicate, nutty flavour when served raw. They are an excellent diabetic food since they are high in food value and in the production of levulose sugars, which persons with diabetes can eat.

item.title: Super Chocolate Fudge
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pkg (350 gr) semi-sweet chocolate chips
  • 1 can Eagle Brand milk
  • 1-1/4 cup icing sugar
  • pinch of salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup chopped nuts

Instructions

In heavy saucepan, over low heat and melt chocolate chips with Eagle Brand. Stir in remaining ingredients. Spread evenly in waxed paper-lined 8” square pan. Chill 2 to 3 hours or until firm. Turn fudge onto cutting board. Peel off paper. Cut into squares. Store loosely covered at room temperature. Makes bout ¾ pounds.

item.title: Swedish Nuts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ lbs (1-1/2 cups) blanches almonds
  • ½ lb (2 cups) walnut halves
  • 1 cup sugar
  • dash salt
  • 2 stiffly beaten egg whites
  • ½ cup butter

Instructions

Toast nuts (325 degrees) until light brown. Fold sugar and dash of salt in egg whites. Beat until stiff, fold in nuts.

Melt butter on cookie sheet. Spread nut mixture in pan, baking at 300 degrees for about 30 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes until no butter remains. Cool. Makes about 4 cups.

item.title: Swedish Strips
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shortening
  • l cup brown sugar
  • l egg
  • l tsp vanilla
  • l/4 tsp salt
  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • l/2 cup jam
  • 2 tbsp icing sugar
  • l/2 cup nuts, finely chopped

Instructions

Cream shortening and sugar until light and fluffy. Add egg, vanilla and salt and mix well. Add flour and combine. Place cookie batter on slightly greased cookie sheet. Spread and flatten until l/4”thick.

Baking time: l5 minutes at 350 degrees.

item.title: Sweet & Sour Polish Dill Pickles –(louise Chalifoux)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Brine: 4 cups vinegar
  • Onions
  • 3 cups water
  • Garlic
  • 3 cups sugar
  • Dill
  • ½ cup pickling salt
  • Green Cucumbers

Instructions

Slice onions (about 2 medium ones to 1 quart) and put 1 piece of garlic and dill in bottom of quart sealers. Then slice green cucumbers (about ¼” thick) into sealers. Bring brine (vinegar, water, sugar, salt) to a boil. Pour over cucumbers in jar and seal.

Enough for 5 quarts.

item.title: Sweet Marie Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Melt over heat:
  • ½ cup corn syrup
  • ½ cup peanut butter
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • Add:
  • 2 cups rice krispies
  • 1 tbsp melted butter
  • ½ cup peanuts

Instructions

Spread in 8”x 8”pan (slightly buttered). Ice with chocolate icing or melt semi-sweet chocolate chips and pour over bars.

item.title: Tarragon Roasted Chicken – (aboriginal Cooking –gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 1 3-lb chicken
  • 2 tsp dried tarragon
  • ¼ cup white wine or apple juice
  • 1 Tbs vegetable oil

Instructions

Cut 1 garlic clove in half. Rub cut sides on outside of chicken. Place garlic in chicken cavity. Sprinkle half of the tarragon in chicken cavity. Tie chicken with string and place in roasting pan.

Mince remaining garlic and combine with remaining tarragon, wine (or apple juice) and oil. Drizzle over chicken and roast in 350 F/180 C oven, basting frequently, for 1-1/4 hours or until juices run clear when chicken is pierced with a fork.

item.title: Tarte Au Sucre (pauline Dupuis)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups brown sugar
  • 2 eggs, beaten
  • 2 tbsp margarine
  • 3 tbsp flour
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1-1/2 cups milk

Instructions

Mix together all ingredients and pour into a large uncooked pie shell or two small ones. Bake at 375 degrees for approx. 50 minutes.

item.title: The Ultimate Tourtiere
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 lb ground pork (lean or medium)
  • ¾ cup water
  • ¾ cup finely chopped onion
  • ¼ cup finely chopped celery
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • ¾ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp dried savory
  • ¼ tsp each thyme, pepper and ground cloves
  • 1 bay leaf
  • ½ cup mashed potato
  • Pastry for double crust 9”pie

Instructions

In large heavy saucepan, combine pork, water, onions, celery and garlic. Cook over medium heat until bubbling, stirring to break up meat. Add salt, savory, thyme, pepper, cloves and bay leaf. Reduce heat, cover and simmer, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes. Remove bay leaf. Stir in mashed potato, mixing well. Taste and add more salt and pepper if needed. Let cool, stirring occasionally (mixture will thicken as it cools). Line pie plate with pastry. Fill with meat mixture. Cover with remaining pastry, seal and crimp edges, cut a few steam vents. Bake in a 425 degree oven for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 375 degrees and bake for 20 to 25 minutes longer or until golden brown.

item.title: Three Sisters Soup – (from Take-5 Workshop by Lise Malette)
item.description:
Instructions

Many Aboriginal people know corn, beans & squash as the “Three Sisters”. It is believed that these crops are watched over and kept safe by the spirits of the Three Sisters who are considered to be special gifts from the Creator and should be planted together, eaten together and celebrated together.

1 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 bay leaf l lb beef or wild meat 1 tsp dried basil 1 onion, chopped 6 cups beef stock * 1 cup carrots, diced 1 – 28 oz tin tomatoes 1 cup celery or fennel, chopped 3 cups chopped cabbage 1 cup acorn, butternut or hubbard 1 cup frozen corn squash, cubed 1 – 19 oz tin kidney beans, drained

item.title: To Whip Evaporated Milk
item.description:
Instructions

Place unopened can of evaporated milk in freezing compartment for 1 hour. Or pack in ice and salt. To whip, bowl and beaters must be ice cold.

item.title: Toffee Chocolate Chunk Almond Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ½ tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 cup Skor chips
  • ¾ cup chocolate chips
  • 1/3 cup sliced natural almonds
  • 1/3 cup unsalted butter, melted
  • ½ cup firmly packed light brown sugar
  • 1 tsp almond extract
  • ½ tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 large egg

Instructions

Preheat over to 350 F. and coat an 8” pan with non-stick spray, or line with non-stick aluminum foil. Whisk flour, baking powder and salt together in a small bowl. Toss together toffee, chocolate chips and almonds in a small bowl. Put one cup of mixture into another small bowl and ½ cup into a third bowl. Whisk together melted butter, brown sugar and extracts in a medium bowl (or pot you melted butter in) until blended. Whisk in egg until well combined. Stir in flour mixture until a few streaks of it remain, then stir in first bowlful of toffee mixture until evenly combined. Spread in an even layer in baking pan and sprinkle top evenly with reserved 1 cup of toffee mixture. Bake until light golden brown around edges but soft in center, 30 minutes; a toothpick inserted in center should come out with wet batter attached. Sprinkle remaining ½ cup of toffee mixture evenly over top. Bake until toothpick inserted in center comes out with a few moist crumbs attached, about 5 more minutes. Place pan on rack to cool completely. Cut into 16 bars. NOTE: Grease pan really well as this recipe tends to really stick, especially around the edges.

item.title: Toffee Frost-ease
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup butter
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup walnuts
  • 1 cup chocolate chips

Instructions

Cream butter, sugar and vanilla. Stir in flour & nuts and mix well.

Pat into an ungreased 9 x 13 pan. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 15 – 20 minutes, until lightly browned.

Remove from oven and sprinkle chocolate chips evenly over top. After about 5 minutes they’ll be soft – then spread evenly.

item.title: Tomato Casserole - (cowboy Grub)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 slices buttered bread
  • 1 28-oz tin tomatoes
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 to 3 tbsp brown sugar
  • ½ tsp dried basil
  • 2 tbsp butter
  • 2 tbsp or more chopped onion (or green onions)

Instructions

Cut the buttered slices of bread into 1”squares and dry them out in the oven at 250 degrees for about ¾ hr. Put half in bottom of large casserole (about 2-quart size). Melt butter in a saucepan and lightly sauté onions. Pour over bread cubes. Mix tomatoes, sugar, salt and basil and pour over bread. Top with rest of bread cubes, pushing them down a little. Bake uncovered at 350 degrees for 45 – 60 minutes.

item.title: Top Hat Triple-layer Bars
item.description:
Instructions

The tuxedo of dessert bars, this dressed up sweet will become a holiday tradition at your house.

½ cup butter, melted 1-1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs 1 200-gr pkg flaked sweetened coconut 1 tin (300 ml) sweetened condensed milk 1 350-gr pkg semisweet chocolate chips ½ cup smooth peanut butter

Combine butter with cracker crumbs in bowl; press evenly over bottom of 13 x 9 cake pan or glass baking dish lined with parchment paper. Top evenly with coconut, then condensed milk.

item.title: Tourtiere
item.description:
Instructions

If you don’t have your own favorite meat pie, here is a recipe you might like to consider

Pastry for a 2-crust 9”pie.

Filling: 1 Tbsp shortening 2 – 3 Tbsp boiling water 1 small onion, chopped fine Salt and pepper ½ lb lean veal, minced Celery leaves or parsley snipped ½ lb minced pork Enough crumbs to thicken the meat

Method: Saute the onion in melted shortening until limp. Add pork, veal and water. Simmer 35 minutes or just until meat becomes very tender. Add salt, pepper, celery or parsley and crumbs. Bake at 375 degrees for about 45 – 50 minutes or until crust is nicely browned.

item.title: Uncle Paul’s Pumpkin Bars – (creation Unique)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 eggs
  • 1-2/3 cups granulated sugar
  • 1 cup cooking oil
  • 16 oz. Can pumpkin
  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 2 tsp baking powder, ground cinnamon
  • 1 tsp salt, baking soda

Instructions

In mixing bowl, beat together eggs, sugar, oil and pumpkin till light and fluffy. Stir together baking powder, cinnamon, salt and soda. Add to pumpkin mixture and mix thoroughly. Spread batter in 2 lightly greased 8” x8” pans.

Bake in 350 F oven for 25 minutes. Cool. Frost bars with your favourite cream cheese frosting.

item.title: Vegetable Burger Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb ground beef
  • ½ cup dry onion soup mix
  • 1 can stewed tomatoes
  • 1 tsp white sugar
  • 8-oz can tomato sauce
  • 1 cup diced potatoes
  • 2 cups water
  • 1 tbsp celery salt
  • 10-oz pkg frozen mixed vegetables

Instructions

In Dutch oven or large pan, brown ground beef. Drain off fat. Stir in tomatoes, tomato sauce, water, onion soup mix, vegetables, sugar and celery salt. Bring to boil; reduce heat, cover and simmer ½ hour. Add diced potatoes; simmer 20 minutes longer.

item.title: Wedges (potatoes)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 cups flour
  • 1 tsp curry powder
  • 3 tbsp salt
  • ½ tsp poultry seasoning
  • 1 tbsp pepper
  • 1/16 tsp cloves
  • 1 tbsp garlic powder
  • 1/16 tsp turmeric
  • 1 tbsp onion salt
  • ½ tsp dry mustard
  • 1 tsp mixed spice

Instructions

Mix well and store in a jar. For potatoes, beat 2 eggs with 1 tbsp of milk. Dip potatoes in egg mixture, roll in the prepared flour, then fry in oil until golden.

item.title: Wendy Coleslaw Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups finely shredded cabbage
  • 1 cup finely shredded carrot
  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • ½ cup milk
  • ½ cup minced onion
  • ¼ cup sugar
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 drops hot pepper sauce (optional)
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • ½ tsp celery salt

Instructions

Toss cabbage and carrot. Mix all other ingredients in a separate bowl. Toss with large fork until well coated. Refrigerate for at least 1-1/2 hr. before serving.

Makes one quart of coleslaw.

item.title: Whole Wheat Blueberry Biscuits
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 cup whole wheat flour
  • 1 Tbsp granulated sugar
  • 1 Tbsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp baking soda
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ cup soft margarine
  • 1 cup blueberries
  • 1 cup buttermilk or sour milk
  • In bowl, combine all-purpose and whole wheat flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Using fingers, rub in margarine until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
  • Stir in blueberries; add buttermilk and mix lightly. Drop by spoonfuls into 10 mounds. Bake in 425 degree (220 C) oven for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden.
  • Makes 10 biscuits (5 g fat per biscuit)

item.title: Wild Bluebarry Smoothies (community Voices)
item.description:
Instructions

INGREDIENTS 2 handfuls of wild blueberries 2 single serving containers of about 6 oz of yogurt (vanilla or plain) 1 Tbsp of honey if plain yogurt is used 3 – 4 ice cubes

DIRECTIONS Blend well at high speed. Top with loose Wild Blueberries. Serve immediately. Serves 2.

item.title: Wild Rice & Cheese Casserole – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup wild rice
  • 3 cups water
  • ½ cup chopped onion
  • 1 cup grated cheese
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 3 cups sliced mushroom
  • 3 Tbsp butter or margarine
  • 1 – 19 oz tin stewed tomatoes

Instructions

Wash rice under cold water. Put the rice into 3 cups of water and boil for 5 minutes. Let soak in the same water for 1 hour. Cook rice in boiling water for an additional 20 minutes.

item.title: Wild Rice Blueberry Pudding - (tracy Bald)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups cooked wild rice
  • 2 cups cream or 2% evaporated milk
  • 6 eggs
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 cup blueberries
  • ¾ cup maple syrup

Instructions

Whisk together the eggs, milk, vanilla and maple syrup. Over medium heat, stir together the wild rice and blueberries in a large sauce pan. Gradually add in milk mixture and stir well. Simmer over medium low heat for 30 minutes, stirring frequently until pudding thickens. Pour into bowls and cool slightly before serving. Serves 8

item.title: Wild Rice Stuffing for Game Birds – (by Gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup wild rice
  • 2 chopped onions
  • ¼ cup margarine or butter, melted
  • ½ cup blanched almonds
  • 1 cup sultana raisins
  • 2 tsp poultry seasoing
  • Salt & pepper to taste

Instructions

Wash rice; soak overnight; in the morning drain; cook in boiling salted water until tender. Drain.

Saute onions in hot margarine or butter until golden brown. Add almonds, raisins, poultry seasoning, salt and pepper.

Lightly stuff fowl or game. Cover bird with oiled, heavy brown paper or foil paper, with no water, no cover and no basting, roast in 325 F. oven until done.

item.title: Winter Chili Sauce – Using Canned Tomatoes
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 28-0z cans best quality tomatoes (Drained 2 6-oz cans tomato paste
  • 2 green peppers
  • 4 medium cooking onions
  • 6 lg outer celery stalks w/leaves
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 2 tsp salt
  • ¾ cup vinegar
  • Spices tied in spice bag

Instructions

Spices tied in cheesecloth bag with long string: 12 whole cloves, 1 tsp mustard seed, 1 tsp celery seed, 3” cinnamon stick, ¼ tsp cayenne pepper (OR 2 Tbsp mixed pickling spices). Into rather heavy kettle measure the tomatoes from which excess juice has been drained to shorten boiling period. (Use juice for some other purpose.) Add the peppers, onions and celery that have been put through medium coarse knife of grinder. You will have to reverse the winding handle several times to unpack the celery strands that gather behind the knife. OR chop finely by hand on board. Add vinegar and salt and sugar and spices tied in bag. Bring to boil and stir very frequently to prevent sticking. Boil gently ½ hour. Add tomato paste and boil ½ hour longer. Remove spice bag. Pour into 3 sterilized pint jars and seal.

item.title: Zesty Party Crunch
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¾ cup margarine
  • 1-1/2 tsp Italian seasoning
  • 1 16-oz pkg Quaker Oat Square Cereal 1-1/2 cups thin pretzel sticks
  • ½ cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • 1-1/2 cups peanuts

Instructions

Heat oven to 325 degrees. In 13 x 9” baking pan, heat margarine in oven until melted. Remove pan from oven. Stir in cheese and seasoning.

Add cereal, pretzels and peanuts, stirring until well coated. Bake 20 minutes, stirring occasionally during baking.

item.title: Zucchini-tomato-onion Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups thinly sliced zucchini
  • 1 tomato, sliced thin
  • 1 medium onion, thinly sliced
  • 3 tbsp grated Parmesan cheese
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

In greased 9 x 5 x 3 baking dish, layer half the zucchini, tomato and onions. Sprinkle with half the cheese; salt and pepper to taste. Repeat.

Bake uncovered in a 350 degree oven for 40 minutes. Cover and bake 20 minutes longer.

Makes 4 servings.

item.title: Zesty Asian Chicken Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Isabelle Dorval

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup rice vinegar
  • 8 tsp reduced sodium soy sauce
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 2 tbsp cilantro, chopped
  • 1 tbsp fresh ginger, minced
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 1/2 tsp sesame oil
  • 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
  • 4-3 oz boned and skinned chicken breast halves
  • 2 cups broccoli florets
  • 2 carrots, cut into 1 1/2 inch long matchsticks
  • 1 cup (6 ozs.) pitted prunes, snipped into halves
  • 3 green onions, sliced
  • 4 lettuce leaves

Instructions

To make dressing/marinade, in medium bowl whisk vinegar, soy, cilantro, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and pepper flakes. Remove 1/4 cup of mixture into a shallow 8 inch baking dish. Place chicken breasts in baking dish. Turn to coat both sides. Let chicken marinate for 30 minutes.

item.title: Tomato Soup Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Emily and Robert Desbois

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 1 lemon Jello

  • 1 can tomato soup

  • 1 small cream cheese

  • 3 sticks celery, chopped

  • 1 medium onion, chopped

  • ¾ cup almonds, chopped

  • 1 cup Miracle Whip

Instructions

Heat Jello, soup and cream cheese until melted. Add other ingredients. Put green peppers on top if desired.

Place in fridge. Make a day ahead.

item.title: Spinach Salad with Strawberries
item.description:
Submitted by Patti and Darren Henri

Cousins of the Bride

  • Spinach, washed and torn
  • 1 pint of fresh strawberries, washed and sliced
  • ½ cup slivered almonds

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ to ½ cup of sugar
  • 2 tbsp sesame seeds
  • 1 tbsp poppy seeds
  • ¼ tsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 1 ½ tsp chopped onion
  • ¼ tsp paprika
  • ½ cup vegetable oil
  • ¼ cup cider vinegar

Instructions

Make the dressing the day before. Pour over salad, toss and serve.

item.title: Salade d'Amour
item.description:
Submitted by Rachel and Wayne Pigeau

Godparents/Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

  • 2 cups cooked rice, cooled
  • 1 ½ cup fresh, chopped spinach
  • 3 stalks celery, cut diagonally
  • ¼ cup raisins
  • ½ cup green peppers, chopped
  • ½ cup red peppers, chopped
  • 3 sprigs fresh parsley
  • ½ cup green onions, chopped
  • 1 cup fresh, sliced mushrooms
  • 2 cups fresh (not canned) bean sprouts
  • ½ cup cashews

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup soya sauce
  • ½ cup oil
  • 2 garlic buds (or more if you like)

Instructions

Put dressing in a jar and add raisins. Let stand overnight in refrigerator. Mix all ingredients together and pour dressing. Let sit in refrigerator for one hour before serving.

item.title: Rice Greek Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Lynn Sayer

Cousin of the Bride

  • 2 cups cooked and cooled, Uncle Ben’s Converted Brand rice
  • 3/4 cup English cucumber, cut into slices
  • 3/4 cup tomatoes, diced
  • 3/4 cup green and red peppers, diced
  • 1/3 cup red onion, diced
  • 1 cup Feta cheese, cut into large dice
  • 1/3 cup Kalamata olives, pitted

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup olive oil
  • 1/4 cup red wine vinegar
  • 1 garlic, minced (about 1 tsp)
  • 1 tsp rubbed oregano
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1/2 tsp pepper

Instructions

In a large bowl, combine cooked rice with all diced vegetables, diced cheese and olives.

item.title: Party Lime Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Deanna and Larry Shank

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 can crushed pineapple
  • 1 small package of lime Jello
  • 1 cup whipping cream
  • 1 cup chopped celery
  • 6 ozs cream cheese, room temperature
  • 1 cup chopped nuts

Instructions

Heat pineapple. Dissolve lime Jello in pineapple. Stir well. Set in refrigerator until partially set.

Whip cream. Beat in cream cheese. Add nuts and celery. Add cream mixture to pineapple mixture.

item.title: Pasta Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Billy Bell

Groom

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup oil
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 2 ½ tbsp sugar
  • ¼ cup concentrated orange juice
  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 1 tsp crushed red pepper
  • ½ tsp basil
  • black pepper
  • salt

Instructions

Cook choice of pasta, drain and add the dressing. Add crushed feta cheese, parmesan cheese, grapes (red and green), red and green peppers, olives and celery.

Chill and serve.

item.title: Lovely Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Ellie and Mike Bell

Cousins of the Groom

  • 1 green pepper
  • 1 red pepper
  • 2 green onions
  • 2 celery stalks
  • ¼ lb mushrooms
  • ¼ lb spinach (1 bag)
  • 1 cup cooked rice
  • 4 ozs cashew nuts, optional
  • 1 tbsp fresh parsley

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ½ cup cooking oil
  • ¼ cup soya sauce
  • 1 garlic clove, minced

Instructions

Marinate dressing for one hour before using. Add to above salad 5-10 minutes before serving.

item.title: Good Coleslaw
item.description:
Submitted by Bev Bell and Lorne Mick

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

  • 1 medium cabbage
  • 1 medium onion
  • 2 grated carrots
  • 1/2 green pepper, optional
  • 1/2 red pepper, optional
  • 1 stick of celery, optional

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup oil
  • 1 cup white vinegar
  • 2/3 cup sugar
  • 2 tsp salt
  • celery salt, a little honey and chopped garlic are optional

Instructions

Chop, shred, grate, hack the veggies into a bowl. Bring oil, vinegar, sugar and salt to a boil, stirring to dissolve. Cool a little and pour over veggies. Tip the bowl to allow dressing to run over cabbage, etc.

item.title: Easy Taco Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Sandra and Steve Pigeau

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 lb ground beef, browned
  • 1 large bag of Doritos, crushed into small pieces
  • 2 cups grated cheddar cheese
  • 1 medium onion, diced
  • 1 green pepper, diced
  • 1 tomato, diced
  • 1 small head of lettuce, chopped
  • 1 bottle of Kraft Catalina dressing

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together. If done ahead, do not add the Doritos or dressing until served.

item.title: Broccoli Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Tracey and Pierre Beaudry

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Bride

  • 1/2 cup raisins
  • 1/2 cup shelled sunflower seeds
  • 1 medium red onion, chopped
  • 1/2 lb crisp, crumbled bacon
  • 3 heads broccoli florets

Combine the above ingredients in a large bowl.

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup Miracle Whip
  • 2 tsp rice vinegar
  • 1/3 cup white sugar

Instructions

Combine and add to broccoli mixture. Stir well and chill.

item.title: Best Damn Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Julie and Dave Pigeau

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 head of lettuce, chopped
  • 3 ribs of celery, chopped
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • 2 cups partially thawed peas
  • 2 cups Hellman’s (must be Hellman’s mayonnaise)
  • 2 tbsp white sugar
  • ¼ cup parmesan cheese
  • bacon bits

Instructions

In a rectangle pan, layer all ingredients beginning with lettuce, celery, onions, and peas. Spread mayonnaise over top. Sprinkle with sugar, then parmesan cheese. Put in the fridge for 24 hours. Sprinkle with bacon bits just before serving.

item.title: "Cook Up" Rice
item.description:
Submitted by Naz and Brian Lloyd

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 3 ½ lbs of skinless chicken (cut up 2 whole chickens)
  • 1 ½ cup rice
  • 1-540 mL can black-eyed peas
  • 1-400 mL can coconut milk
  • 3 tbsp butter
  • 3 tbsp soya sauce
  • 2 tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 tsp basil
  • 3 cloves of garlic, minced
  • 1 large onion, minced
  • pinch of black pepper
  • salt to taste

Instructions

Wash chicken and cut into pieces (leave bones in). Place chicken in a large bowl. Blend in onion, garlic, soya sauce, basil, pepper and salt.

item.title: Sausage and Rice
item.description:
Submitted by Lori Girard

Friend of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 can of tomatoes, or stewed tomatoes
  • one onion, cut in long slivers
  • 1 pkg beef bouillon
  • ¾ cup water
  • basil, garlic, salt and pepper

Instructions

Fry onion. Add other ingredients. Thicken sauce with corn starch. Fry breakfast sausage. Slice sausage and add to sauce.

Pour sauce with sausage over white rice.

item.title: Baked Rice
item.description:
Submitted by Linda Desrochers

Cousin of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 2 cups of Uncle Ben’s converted rice
  • 4 cups water
  • ½ cup soya sauce
  • ½ cup oil
  • 1 pkg onion soup mix
  • 1 can drained mushrooms
  • 1 can drained peas

Instructions

Mix all ingredients in roast pan. Place in oven for one hour at 350 degrees.

item.title: Poor Man's Pasta
item.description:
Submitted by Derek Forgie

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • Macaroni
  • Cheese Dust

Instructions

Fill a metal container with a water-like substance. Find a way to make it hot enough for the water to make a boiling noise. Place several noodles into the noisy water.

Stir the noodles with a pointy or rounded stick until they become limpanized. Drain the leftover water into a plant that looks cold and thirsty.

item.title: Penné with Peppers, Mushrooms, and Sausage
item.description:
Submitted by Marleen and Bill Tregenza

Godparents of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 6 red bell peppers
  • 14 ozs fresh mushrooms
  • 8 ozs sausage, remove from casing
  • 2 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp oregano
  • 1 tbsp thyme
  • 2 garlic cloves
  • ½ cup fresh parsley
  • grated parmesan cheese
  • salt and pepper

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 degrees. Cut peppers into ½ inch diagonal pieces. Place in a 9x13 inch baking dish. Drizzle with 1 tbsp of olive oil, salt and pepper to taste. Bake until charred (45 minutes), stir occasionally. Remove.

item.title: Penné with Italian Sausage, Tomato, and Herbs
item.description:
Submitted by Sue and Don Lloyd

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 4 hot or medium Italian sausages
  • 2 cups penné or corkscrew-shaped pasta
  • 1 tsp vegetable oil
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 3 gloves garlic, minced
  • 1-28 oz can of tomatoes, coarsely chopped
  • 2 tsp dried basil
  • 1 tsp dried oregano
  • ½ cup fresh parsley, chopped
  • pepper
  • 2 tbsp freshly grated parmesan cheese
  • 2-3 pinches of crushed red pepper

Instructions

In skillet, cook sausage over medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes. Drain well. Cut into thin, round slices. Cook pasta until tender but firm. Drain and return to pot.

item.title: Chicken Tetrazzini
item.description:
Submitted by Christine Shank

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 boiling fowl (5 ½ - 6 lbs)
  • a few stalks of celery, chopped
  • 3 small onions, chopped
  • 1 quart milk
  • ¼ lb butter
  • 4 ozs hard flour
  • 2 cups chicken stock
  • 1 lb spaghetti
  • 1 lb mushrooms
  • ½ cup parmesan cheese
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Boil fowl. Take meat from bones and put stalks of celery, onions and salt and pepper to taste into chicken stock. Simmer.

item.title: Angel Hair Pasta with Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Allison and Dave Rudback

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp olive oil, divided
  • 2 boneless chicken breasts, cubed
  • 1 carrot, sliced diagonally
  • 1 cup broccoli florets
  • 2 cloves of garlic, minced
  • 12 oz angel hair pasta
  • 2/3 cup chicken broth
  • 1/4 cup parmesan cheese
  • 1 tsp dried basil

Instructions

Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a medium sized skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and cook until cooked through, about 5 minutes. Remove from skillet and drain on paper towel.

item.title: Sugar Pie
item.description:
Submitted by Heather Bell

Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ cup brown sugar
  • 1 ½ tbsp flour

Instructions

Place the above ingredients in a saucepan. Add 1 cup of canned milk. Cook over low heat, stirring until dissolved. Remove from heat. Slowly add 1 beaten egg, stirring constantly. Pour into an 8 inch, unbaked pie shell. Bake at 400 degrees for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 300 degrees and continue baking for 30 minutes, or until thick.

item.title: Pizza Dough
item.description:
Submitted by Paulette and Mike Shank

Cousins of the Bride

In a large bowl, combine:

  • 1 ½ cup warm water
  • 1 tbsp yeast

Let sit for 10-15 minutes. Mix well, then add:

Ingredients

  • 1 tbsp oil
  • 2 tbsp brown sugar
  • 3 cups flour (+1 cup if needed)
  • 1 tsp salt

Instructions

Mix well and knead to form a ball. Cover and let rise in oven (heat off) for 1 hour.

item.title: Never Fail Pastry
item.description:
Submitted by Yolande and Dave Worthington

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 5 cups flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ¾ tsp salt
  • 3 tbsp brown sugar
  • 1 lb shortening
  • 1 egg, slightly beaten
  • cold water
  • 2 tbsp vinegar

Instructions

Sift flour, baking powder and salt together. Add brown sugar. Cut in shortening until fine as meal. Slightly beat egg in measuring cup. Add cold water until egg and water mixture measures ¾ cup. Add vinegar. Drizzle egg mixture over flour mixture and stir with a fork until it is well mixed.

item.title: Meat Pies
item.description:
Submitted by Del Gosson

Aunt of the Bride

Filling:

  • 3 lbs of beef, or cheaper cut of steak
  • 2 lbs of pork, remove fat
  • 2 double chicken breasts, cooked for about 20 minutes
  • 3 large onions
  • 1 tbsp salt
  • 1 tsp pepper
  • ½ cup flour

Pie Crust:

Ingredients

  • 1 lb of shortening

Instructions

Follow pastry recipe on shortening box. Add 2 extra cups of flour and enough water to hold the pastry together.

item.title: Flapper Pie Special
item.description:
Submitted by Gisele and Ronnie Roy

Cousins of the Bride

  • 18-20 graham wafers
  • ¼ cup butter, melted
  • ¼ cup shortening, melted
  • ¼ tsp cinnamon

Crush and roll wafers fine. Blend in butter, shortening and cinnamon. Line bottom and sides of a 9 inch pie plate. Reserve 1/8 cup for topping.

Filling:

Ingredients

  • 4 eggs
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 ½ tbsp cornstarch
  • 2 cups milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Separate eggs. Beat yolks until lemony in color. Add sugar and cornstarch and blend well.

item.title: Deluxe Pumpkin Cream Cheese Pie
item.description:
Submitted by Charleen and Jim Martin

Cousins of the Bride

Requires 2 uncooked pie shells.

  • 1 large pkg cream cheese
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Beat the above ingredients together and spread in bottom of pie shells.

Pumpkin Layer:

Ingredients

  • 2 cups pumpkin, canned
  • 2 beaten eggs
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 cup milk
  • ½ tsp ginger

Instructions

Mix ingredients and spread carefully over cream cheese layer in pie shells.

item.title: Apple Pie
item.description:
Submitted by Yolande and Dave Worthington

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 4 VERY large Granny Smith apples (6 cups)
  • 2/3 to 1 cup white sugar, depending on tartness of apples
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1/8 tsp salt
  • 1 tbsp cornstarch

Instructions

Prepare the “Never Fail Pastry” on page 92. Use 2 portions.

Mix sugar, cinnamon, salt and cornstarch together. Peel and thinly slice the apples. Toll out one portion of the pastry and line the bottom of a 9 inch pie pan. Place alternate layers of apples and sugar mixture in the pie pan. Roll out the second portion of pastry, cut vents and place top crust in position. Seal edge and flute.

item.title: Swiss Steak Slow Cooker
item.description:
Submitted by Murdena and Robert Burton

Cousins of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1-2 steaks
  • 1-14 oz can of tomatoes
  • 1 can mushroom pieces
  • 1 can beef consommé
  • 1 small onion, chopped
  • salt, pepper and garlic salt to taste

Instructions

Brown steak and onion. Season with salts and add remaining ingredients in slow cooker.

Simmer for 2-3 hours.

item.title: Sweet 'n' Sour Meatloaf
item.description:
Submitted by Jeannine and Elmer Bell

Parents of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 small onion, minced
  • ¼ cup crushed crackers (or bread crumbs)
  • 1-8 oz tin tomato sauce
  • ¼ cup brown sugar, packed
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 1 tsp prepared mustard
  • 1 egg
  • 2 lbs ground chuck
  • 1 ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper

Instructions

Mix tomato sauce with sugar, vinegar and mustard until sugar is dissolved. Put ½ cup of sauce mixture aside.

item.title: Sweet and Sour Meatballs
item.description:
Submitted by Billy Bell

Groom

  • 2 lbs hamburg
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 cup bread crumbs
  • 2 eggs

Knead all ingredients together. Form meatballs and place on a cookie sheet. Cook at 350 degrees until brown.

Sauce:

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ cup brown sugar
  • 1 tsp dry mustard
  • ¾ cup ketchup
  • ¾ cup water
  • ¾ cup vinegar

Instructions

Mix ingredients together in a sauce pan. Bring to a boil. Mix

item.title: Stuffed Pork Chops
item.description:
Submitted by Kara MacDonald

Friend of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp butter
  • ½ pkg mushrooms, diced
  • 1 red pepper
  • 1 envelope of Lipton onion soup mix
  • 1 cup bread crumbs
  • ½ tsp pepper
  • 4 thick pork chops

Instructions

Combine vegetables and butter in sauce pan and cook until mushrooms are done. Add the soup mix, bread crumbs and pepper. Allow the mixture to cool.

Cut the pork chops on the side and make a pocket on the inside of the pork chop. Do not remove excess fat from the pork chops. Stuff the pockets with the above mixture.

item.title: Roast Shoulder of Pork With Stuffing
item.description:
Submitted by Pauline and Jim Cunningham

Friends of the Groom’s Family (Uncle Jim)

Stuffing:

Ingredients

  • 1/4 – 1/2 cup salt pork, diced
  • 5 apples, diced
  • 1/2 cup celery, chopped
  • 1/3 cup sugar
  • 1/2 cup onions, chopped
  • 2 cups dried bread crumbs
  • 1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped

Instructions

Fry salt pork until crisp. Remove from skillet. Cook celery, onion and parsley in fat for 5 minutes. Remove from skillet. Place apples in skillet and sprinkle with sugar. Cook covered until tender. Remove cover and continue to cook until juice evaporates. Add bread crumbs and other ingredients and mix well.

item.title: Roasted Bull Elephant
item.description:
Submitted by Lorraine and Frank Nault

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 1 elephant roast
  • ½ boxcar of bread crumbs
  • 3 crates of cut celery
  • 3 mailbags of chopped garlic cloves
  • 2 trash cans of scalding hot water
  • 50 lbs diced hot peppers
  • 1 keg salt
  • 2 oil drums of butter

Instructions

Blast off the rear end of one elephant to make large opening for stuffing. Mix ½ boxcar of bread crumbs, 3 mailbags of chopped garlic, 3 crates of cut celery and 2 trash cans of scalding hot water to moisten.

item.title: Rich's Sweet Balls
item.description:
Submitted by Anya and Rich Pagé

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 pkg of ground beef
  • ½ cup or so of bread crumbs
  • 1 egg
  • salt and pepper
  • 2 green onions, chopped
  • 1 tsp parsley flakes
  • 2 cloves of garlic, chopped
  • butter or oil to grease frying pan
  • 1 jar of honey garlic sauce (available in Chinese food section
  • of grocery store)

Instructions

In a large bowl, mix everything except honey garlic sauce and roll lovingly into balls. The balls like to be squeezed and played with so don’t hesitate to give them your full attention. If you drop a ball on the floor, throw it out, no one likes hairy balls.

item.title: Pork With Broccoli Stir Fry
item.description:
Submitted by Tina Sasyniuk and Kevin Long

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • ¾ lb lean, boneless pork
  • 1 tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce
  • 1 tbsp cooking sherry
  • 1 bunch broccoli
  • 2 tbsp vegetable oil
  • 2 tbsp minced garlic cloves
  • 2 tbsp minced gingerroot
  • ¼ cup water
  • 2 tbsp hoisin sauce (optional) 25ml

Instructions

Slice pork thinly across the grain.

In a bowl, stir together cornstarch, soy sauce, and sherry. Add pork and stir to coat well. Separate broccoli into florets, peel stalks, then cut in 1 ½ inch pieces.

item.title: Poor Man's Lobster
item.description:
Submitted by Shelley Bzdell and Marc Robidas — Friends of the Groom

Ingredients

  • Fish filet
  • 7UP (enough to boil in)
  • Garlic butter

Instructions

Boil fish in 7UP until the filet floats.

Fry in garlic butter.

Enjoy the fine taste of lobster!

item.title: No Peek Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Lacey Pigeau

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ cups white rice
  • 1-10 oz can condensed celery soup
  • 1-10 oz can condensed mushroom soup
  • 1 pkg dried onion soup
  • 1 cup milk
  • 4-5 skinless chicken breasts

Instructions

Mix soup, milk and rice. Place mixture in a casserole dish. Lay chicken on top. Cover with foil and cook at 325 degrees for 2 hours. Do not peek!

item.title: Manilla Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Stephanie Lemaire and John Breton

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 6 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • 1 cup onion, finely chopped
  • 1/3 cup white vinegar
  • 1/3 cup soya sauce
  • 1/2 cup water
  • 2 tbsp granulated sugar
  • 1/8 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

Arrange chicken breasts in a single layer in a large saucepan. Mix remaining 8 ingredients in a small bowl. Pour over chicken. Cover. Bring to a boil. Lower heat and simmer for

item.title: Gord's Homemade Jerky
item.description:
Submitted by Patsy and Gord Bell

Sister-in-law and Brother of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ kg meat (any kind, no fat)
  • 1 bottle soya sauce
  • ½ bottle Worcestershire sauce
  • ½ - 1 cup of brown sugar
  • a few cloves of garlic

Instructions

Cut meat into 1/8-1/4 inch thick strips. Make sure there is no fat on the meat. Cut with the grain for chewier jerky.

Mix the rest of the ingredients and taste to your liking. Add meat strips, cover and refrigerate for at least 24 hours.

item.title: Dan's Chili
item.description:
Submitted by Linda and Mike Gosson

Parents of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 cup onions, chopped
  • 1 cup celery, chopped
  • 1 can kidney beans
  • 1 tbsp chili powder
  • 1 ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tbsp vinegar
  • 1 cup green peppers
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 1 large can tomatoes (I use stewed whole tomatoes)
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • ½ - 1 tsp crushed red pepper

Instructions

Brown beef, onion, celery, and green peppers. Add remaining ingredients and simmer for 1 hour stirring frequently.

item.title: Crock Pot Beans
item.description:
Submitted by Tom Alexander

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 3 cups beans
  • 6 cups water
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 onion, chopped
  • ½ lb green bacon or regular bacon (I use a chunk of salt pork)
  • ½ cup molasses
  • 1 cup ketchup
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • 2 tsp dry mustard

Instructions

Place everything in the crock pot and let cook on low for 15-20 hours or on high for 5-7 hours. (7 hours seemed to work best)

item.title: Chicken Parmesan
item.description:
Submitted by Charlene and Leonard Gillis

Cousins of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • ¼ cup parmesan cheese
  • 2 cups tomato sauce (or leftover spaghetti sauce)
  • ½ - 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese

Instructions

Put ½ cup of sauce in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish. Place the chicken on top of the sauce and pour remaining sauce over chicken.

Sprinkle parmesan cheese over the sauce and chicken and place in a preheated 350 degree oven. Bake for about 1 ½ hours or until chicken is tender when poked with a fork.

item.title: Chicken à la King
item.description:
Submitted by Rachelle and Tom Pigeau — Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 cups chopped, cooked chicken breast
  • Frozen vegetables (optional)
  • 1 can cream of chicken soup
  • 1/2 can milk (or water)
  • 1/2 cup mozzarella cheese, shredded
  • 1 pouch Stove Top stuffing mix
  • 1 cup water

Instructions

Layer chicken and frozen vegetables in a medium baking dish.

Mix cream of chicken soup with 1/2 can of milk (or water) and pour over top of chicken and vegetables.

item.title: Becky's Sweet and Sour Chicken Wings
item.description:
Submitted by Becky and Darren Shackleton

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 large package of wings (about 25)
  • ¾ cup of soya sauce
  • ½ cup ketchup
  • ½ cup water
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • 2 tsp flour

Instructions

Take a large glass casserole dish and mix soya sauce, ketchup, water and brown sugar. Add the flour and mix well. Add the wings in the dish and place in oven for 40 minutes at 400 degrees.

item.title: Bacon Mushroom Swiss Meatloaf
item.description:
Submitted by Darcy Lloyd

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 12 ozs of bacon, diced
  • 4 medium white mushrooms, chopped
  • 1 small onion, finely chopped
  • 1 ½ lbs extra lean ground beef
  • 1 egg
  • ¼ cup evaporated milk or half and half
  • 6 ozs shredded swiss cheese
  • ½ cup corn flake crumbs

Instructions

In a large skillet, cook bacon until crisp but not burnt. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel to drain and cool. Remove all but a tablespoon of the bacon fat from the skillet and sauté the mushrooms and onions until tender. Allow to cool slightly.

item.title: Yuck-a-Flux (The Party Drink)
item.description:
Submitted by Kathy and Sean Demarce

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Take one clean industrial size olive or pickle jar (4 litre size would be perfect) with a lid.

Ingredients

  • 3 lemons
  • 3 limes
  • 3 oranges

Instructions

Cut the fruit into quarters. Squeeze the juice into the jar and place the rinds into the jar as well. Add some different fruit like whole strawberries, quartered kiwi (2-3) is good. We recommend NOT using grapefruit, or bananas because they can get mushy. Add 1 bottle of flavoured Vodka (750ml) (Absolut Mandarin, Blueberry, Raspberry, whatever flavour sounds good). Fruit flavoured vodka is the best, although, Vanilla Vodka can be good.

item.title: Porch Climber
item.description:
Submitted by Susy Boulanger and Craig Lavigne

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 big bucket
  • 3 bags of ice
  • 6 cans of concentrated fruit juice
  • 60-100oz of Vodka
  • 24 bottles of beer

Instructions

Pour beer and vodka into bucket. Add ice and concentrated juice. Mix well.

Dunk your cup in and enjoy!

item.title: Jesse's Strawberry Chocolate Shake
item.description:
Submitted by Jesse Beaudry

Nephew of the Bride

Ingredients

  • ½ cup sliced strawberries
  • 2 tbsp white sugar
  • 1 tbsp cocoa
  • 1 cup milk, divided
  • ½ cup cold club soda
  • ice cream or whipped cream
  • fresh strawberries

Instructions

In a blender, combine the sliced strawberries, sugar, cocoa and ½ cup milk. Cover and blend until smooth. Add remaining ½ cup milk and club soda. Cover and blend. Pour into 2 glasses. Garnish with scoop of ice cream or whipped cream and whole strawberries.

item.title: Yummy Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Francine Massicotte

Cousin of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1-8 oz pkg of cream cheese

  • 1 cup sour cream

  • ½ cup mayonnaise

  • 1 cup seafood sauce

  • 2 cups shrimp (optional)

  • ½ cup diced green onions

  • ½ cup diced tomatoes

  • ½ cup green peppers

  • 2 cups grated mozzarella and/or cheddar cheese

Instructions

Mix the first 3 ingredients together and spread it on a pizza pan or large plate. Spread seafood sauce on top and add shrimp (optional). Cover with cheese. Sprinkle with green onions, tomatoes and green peppers.

item.title: Vegetable/Chip Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Brenda and Denis Arsenault

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 cup sour cream
  • 1 cup Hellman’s mayonnaise
  • 1 tsp beau monde spice
  • 1 tsp minced onion
  • 1 tsp accent
  • 1 tsp dill weed
  • 1 tsp parsley
  • ½ tsp garlic salt
  • 2 to 3 drops of Tabasco

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together and serve with vegetables or chips.

item.title: Taco Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Diana Bell

Cousin of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 pkg of cream cheese
  • 1 envelope of taco seasoning
  • 1 jar of salsa
  • 1 medium tomato, diced
