Defrost chopped spinach & squeeze out excess water.

Hollow out center of pumpernickel.

Cut center of pumpernickel into cubes or slices. In a small bowl combine sour cream soup mix and spinach.

Stir until thoroughly mixed. Place in center of pumpernickel

Refrigerate for 1 hour

Include bagels as an option for dipping.

item.title: Pickled Wieners
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb 5” wieners (12 to a pound) OR equivalent in smoked or Polish sausage or pepperoni or knockwurst
  • 1-1/4 cups white vinegar
  • ½ cup water
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 2 tsp pickling spices
  • 1 garlic clove, chopped

Instructions

Cut wieners in thirds crosswise. If other smoked type sausages are used, cut them into large bite-sized pieces. Measure all remaining ingredients into shallow saucepan, bring to a boil, add cut wieners, reduce heat, cover and SIMMER gently 30 minutes. Remove from heat and pour into preheated, broad-mouthed jar with lid. Cool then refrigerate for 3 or 4 days before using. During the marinating period, shake jar to make sure all wiener pieces are covered with marinade.

item.title: Oxo Honey Garlic Chicken Wings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs chicken wings
  • 1/3 cup honey
  • 2 tbsp Oxo chicken bouillon liquid
  • 2 tbsp soya sauce
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • ½ tsp garlic powder
  • ¼ tsp ground ginger

Instructions

Cut wings in two sections and remove tips. In a large baking pan, place wings in a single layer. Bake at 425 degrees for 10 minutes. Remove from oven and raise temperature to 475 degrees. Drain fat from pan.

item.title: Nutty Cheese Ball
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ lb grated med. Cheddar Cheese (about 2 cups)
  • ½ cup milk
  • 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 8 oz softened cream cheese
  • ½ tsp garlic salt
  • 3 oz crumbled blue cheese
  • ¾ cup finely chopped walnuts

Instructions

Mix in a 2-quart bowl, cheeses, milk, Worcestershire sauce and garlic salt. Chill if necessary before shaping into a ball. Roll in walnuts. Chill until firm (about 3 – 5 hours). Serve with crackers.

item.title: Loney's Express Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups sour cream (500 ml)
  • 1 pouch Loney’s Onion Soup Mix
  • 2 Tbsp green pepper, finely chopped
  • ½ tsp Tabasco spicy sauce
  • 2 tsp tomato ketchup
  • ½ tsp chili powder

Instructions

In a medium-sized bowl mix Loney’s Onion Soup Mix with sour cream. Add remaining ingredients and mix thoroughly. Serve immediately with an assortment of raw vegetables.

For a Mexican-style dip, use twice the amount of spicy sauce. Serve with tortilla chips.

item.title: Hidden Valley Bacon & Cheddar Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 (1-oz) packet Original Ranch Party Dip
  • 1 pint sour cream
  • 1/4 cup bacon bits
  • 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese

Instructions

Mix Ranch Party Dip with sour cream.

Add bacon bits and shredded cheddar cheese.

Mix and serve.

item.title: Exquisite Crab Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 8-oz pkg cream cheese
  • 1 can (6 oz) crabmeat
  • 1 tsp horseradish
  • 1 tsp milk
  • 1 tsp minced onion

Instructions

Combine all ingredients. Serve cold or bake for 20 minutes at 350 degrees. Serve with cocktail rye bread.

item.title: Crispy Chicken Wings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs chicken wings
  • ½ cup melted butter or margarine
  • ¼ tsp garlic powder
  • 2 tbsp chopped parsley
  • 1 cup dry bread crumbs
  • ½ cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • ½ tsp salt

Instructions

Cut chicken wings in two pieces, discarding the tips. In a small shallow bowl, combine butter and garlic powder. In another bowl, combine remaining ingredients. Dip chicken into butter mixture, then into crumb mixture. Place on greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350 degrees for 50 – 60 minutes or until done.

item.title: Corey's Cheese Ball
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 250 gr pkg cream cheese
  • ½ jar Cheez Whiz cheese
  • 1 cup shredded cheese
  • 1-1/2 tsp onion flakes
  • 1-1/2 tsp Worcestershire sauce

Instructions

Mix well. Refrigerate till firm. Makes 2 balls. Roll in parsley flakes or crushed walnuts.

item.title: Colette's Crab Meat Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 7-oz tin crab meat
  • 1 pkg. (250 gr) cream cheese
  • 1 tsp lemon juice
  • Sprinkling of parsley

Instructions

Mix all ingredients. Seafood sauce on top for flavor and color (optional)

item.title: Colette's Spinach Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup sour cream
  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • 1 pkg frozen chopped spinach
  • 1 pkg Knorr vegetable soup mix

Instructions

Drain spinach well, then mix all ingredients together. If desired, put dip into pumpernickel bread.

item.title: Chestnut & Bacon Roll-ups
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Quantity desired of bacon strips
  • Quantity desired of water chestnuts
  • Sugar
  • Soya Sauce

Instructions

In one bowl place granulated sugar and in second bowl place soya sauce. Dip chestnuts firs in soya sauce and then into the sugar coat.

Partly cook bacon to remove some of the grease but bacon is still limp. Roll prepared chestnuts in bacon and secure with a toothpick.

Place on a cookie sheet. Just before serving, place in oven or under broiler to crisp bacon.

item.title: Cheese Krispies
item.description:
This recipe came from Pat Johnston, who is Yolande’s Friend

Ingredients

  • 1 cup Butter or Margarine
  • 1 Pkg (1/2 lb.) Imperial Cheese (Red)
  • Dash Cayenne Pepper
  • Pinch of salt
  • ½ tsp Worcestershire Sauce
  • 1-1/2 cups Flour
  • 4 cups Rice Krispies

Instructions

Combine all ingredients by hand or with a pastry blender. Form into small balls. Place on cookie sheet and flatten with fork. Bake 15 – 20 minutes at 350 degrees.

item.title: Bruschetta Fast and Easy
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 28-oz or 796 ml can diced tomatoes
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • 1 Tbsp dried basil
  • 2 Tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1 loaf French bread
  • 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese

Instructions

COMBINE tomatoes, onion and basil in large skillet. BRING to boil, simmer, uncovered, over medium heat until thickened – about 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.

COMBINE oil and garlic powder. SLICE bread in half lengthwise, place cut side up on large baking sheet. PLACE under broiler to toast bread. BRUSH bread with garlic oil. SPREAD tomato mixture evenly over bread; sprinkle with cheese (optional). PLACE under broiler until cheese melts, about 2 minutes.

item.title: Antipasto Roll-ups
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 7 oz tuna or chicken, drained and flakes
  • 1 hard-boiled egg, chopped
  • ¼ cup mayonnaise
  • 1 tsp lemon juice
  • 2 6-oz pkg sliced mozzarella cheese
  • 2 8-oz pkg sliced party salami

Instructions

Mash together tuna or chicken, egg, mayo and lemon juice; set aside.

Cut each cheese slice into 4. Arrange a piece of cheese on each slice of salami, trim cheese to fit.

Spread a tsp. of tuna or chicken mixture on cheese. Roll up and secure with 3 wooden picks. Chill. To serve, cut each roll in 3. Makes 8 dozen appetizers.

item.title: Vegetable Soup
item.description:
• 3 cups water
  • 2 cups tomato juice
  • 1 large onion, diced
  • 1 cup diced carrots
  • 1 cup diced celery
  • 1 cup diced yellow turnip
  • 1 cup diced peeled potato
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1/4 tsp pepper

Combine all 9 ingredients in large saucepan. Bring to a boil on medium. Reduce heat. Cover. Simmer for about 20 minutes until vegetables are tender. Makes about 7 cups.

NOTES:

Ingredients

  • The “20 minute simmer” took longer for me. I like my veggies mushy in stuff like this though.
  • Very good, though. An easy 4 out of 5.

Credit to Company’s Coming - The Rookie Cook cookbook.

item.title: Tasty Cheese Loaf
item.description:
• 1/2 cup margarine (or butter), softened
  • 4 oz. block of cream cheese, softened
  • 1/4 tsp onion powder
  • 1/4 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/8 tsp cayenne powder
  • 1 cup grated Asiago (or Romano) cheese
  • 3 tbsp chopped fresh parsley
  • 1 large French bread loaf, cut into 3/4 inch slices

Beat first 5 ingredients in medium bowl until well combined.

Add Asiago cheese and parsley. Stir.

Spread cheese mixture on both sides of each bread slice. Lay out long piece of foil on counter. Keeping slices in order, reshape loaf towards 1 end of foil. Wrap foil over loaf. Folder ends to seal completely. Bake in 375 degree Fahrenheit oven for 30 minutes until cheese it melted and bread is warmed through. Makes about 15 slices.

item.title: Shortbread Cookies #2
item.description:
• 1 1/4 cups all purpose flour
  • 1/2 cup cornstarch
  • 1/2 cup icing sugar
  • 1 cup butter, softened
  • pinch of salt

Preheat oven to 275 degrees Fahrenheit.

In a medium bowl sift together cornstarch, icing sugar, and flour. Add butter; stir until smooth dough is formed.

Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper. Roll dough into 1-inch balls. Place two inches apart on cookie sheet. Flatten with your fingers or fork. Decorate with seasonal sprinkles.

item.title: Shortbread Cookies #1
item.description:
• 1 cup soft butter
  • 1 1/2 cups flour
  • 1/2 cup cornstarch
  • 1/2 cup icing sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla or almond flavouring

Roll out or cookie press. Decorate with small pieces of red and green cherries. Bake in 325 degree Fahrenheit oven for around 15-20 mins.

NOTES:

Ingredients

  • I used margarine once and butter the next. MUCH better with real butter.
  • 4 out of 5

Credit to Camp Lau-Ren’s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook

item.title: Oven-Baked Chicken
item.description:
• 1/3 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • 3/4 tsp seasoned salt
  • 3/4 tsp salt
  • 1/4 tsp pepper
  • 12 chicken drumsticks, skin removed

Combine first 5 ingredients in large resealable freezer bag. Dampen chicken with water. Add a few pieces to bag. Seal bag. Shake until chicken is well coated. Arrange chicken in single layer on greased baking sheet. Repeat with remaining chicken and spice mixture. Bake in 425 degree Fahrenheit oven for 30 minutes. Turn chicken. Bake for about 15 minutes until juices run clear. Serves 4.

item.title: Oatmeal Raisin Cookies
item.description:
• 3/4 brown sugar
  • 1/2 cup sugar
  • 3/4 cup butter
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 1/2 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1/2 tsp cinnamon
  • 2 cups quick cooking oats
  • 1 cup raisins

Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.

In a medium sized bowl blend together sugars and butter until light and fluffy.

Add eggs and vanilla; mix thoroughly.

In a separate bowl sift together flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon; stir into wet mixture. Add oatmeal and raisins; stir until mixed.

item.title: Lemon Loaf
item.description:
• 1/2 cup butter
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs, beaten
  • 1 1/2 cups flour
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1/2 cup milk
  • grated rind of 1 lemon

Cream butter and sugar. Add rind. Blend in dry ingredients alternately with milk. Bake in greased loaf pan at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 50 minutes.

Mix juice of 1 lemon and 1/4 cup icing sugar and drizzle over top while still hot.

item.title: Loaded "Baked Potato" Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Medium baking potatoes, unpeeled
  • 1 can (10 fl oz.) chicken broth
  • 1 cup milk
  • 3 slices of cooked bacon (can be the ready-to-serve microwave stuff)
  • 3/4 cup of shredded cheddar cheese, divided
  • 2 Tbsp sliced green onions, divided
  • 1/4 cup sour cream

Instructions

  1. Pierce potatoes; microwave on HIGH 5 mins., turning over after 2 1/2 mins.  Cut potatoes in chunks.  Combine potatoes, broth, and milk in large microwaveable bowl.  Microwave on HIGH 6 mins., stirring after 3 min.  Slightly crush potatoes with a potato masher.
  2. Reserve 2 Tbsp. each of the bacon and cheese and 1 Tbsp. of the onions for topping; set aside.   Add remaining bacon, cheese, and onions to soup.
  3. Serve topped with reserved bacon, cheese, onions, and sour cream.

item.title: Honey Garlic Sauce
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3/4 Cup brown sugar
  • 2 Tbsp cornstarch
  • 3/4 Cup water
  • 3 Tbsp soya sauce
  • 1 Tbsp honey
  • 2 Cloves of garlic, chopped very fine

Instructions

Combine all ingredients in a pot. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Real good slathered on chicken wings, ribs, or in a stir fry.

Billy’s Notes - So good! Restaurant quality.

Credit to Camp Lau-Ren’s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook

item.title: Honey Garlic Chicken Wings
item.description:
• 10 to 12 chicken wings, cut into 2 pieces
  • 3/4 cup brown sugar
  • 2 tbsp corn starch
  • 3/4 tbsp soya sauce
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 2 cloves garlic, chopped very fine (or use a garlic press)

Brown chicken wings or spareribs in frying pan. Drain fat and place in casserole dish. In a separate pot, combine sauce ingredients. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Pour sauce over wings in casserole dish. Cook in microwave for 10 to 12 minutes on power level 6 or bake in oven at 325 to 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 to 40 minutes.

item.title: Hamburger Soup (Jenny)
item.description:
• 1.5 lbs. of lean hamburg
  • 3 small onions (chopped fine)

Brown meat and onions, then drain well.

Combine all other ingredients in a large pot with meat and mix well.

Ingredients

  • 1 28 oz. can of tomatoes
  • 3 cans of beef consommé
  • 4 carrots (chopped fine)
  • 1 bay leaf (optional)
  • 1/2 cup of pearl barley
  • pepper to taste
  • 2 cups of water
  • 1 can of tomato soup
  • 3 sticks of celery (chopped fine)
  • 1/2 tsp. of thyme
  • 1/2 tsp. of parsley

Instructions

Simmer covered at least 2 hours or all day if possible.

item.title: Family Favourite Lasagna
item.description:
• 1 lb lean ground beef
  • 2 onions, chopped
  • 2 garlic cloves, minced
  • 1 jar spaghetti sauce
  • 2 tsp dried oregano
  • 1 tsp dried basil
  • Pepper to taste
  • 6 lasagna noodles
  • 2 cups skim or 2% cottage cheese (we use ricotta)
  • 2 cups shredded partly skimmed mozzarella cheese
  • 1 egg, lightly beaten
  • 1/2 cup freshly grated parmesan

In a large nonstick skillet or Dutch oven, cook beef over medium heat for about 5 minutes, or until browned, breaking up with a fork. Pour off fat. Add onions and garlic; cook for 3 to 5 minutes, or until softened. Add spaghetti sauce, oregano and basil; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally for 15 minutes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

item.title: Cinnamon Toast Spread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups margarine
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 2 tbsp cinnamon

Instructions

Stir in a large bowl.  Spread on hot toast.

NOTES:  This really tastes like the store-bought stuff!  I’d give it a 4.5 out of 5.

Credit to Camp Lau-Ren’s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook

item.title: Cream Corn Chowder
item.description:
• 3 bacon slices, diced
  • 1/4 cup finely chopped onion
  • 1/4 cup finely chopped carrot
  • 2 cups milk
  • 1 tbsp chicken bouillon powder
  • 2 tbsp all-purpose flour
  • Pepper, sprinkle
  • 2 cans of cream style corn (10 oz., 284 mL, each)
  • 1/2 cup fresh (or frozen, thawed) kernel corn
  • Chopped fresh chives, for garnish

Fry bacon in large saucepan on medium-high for 4 to 5 minutes until crisp. Remove to paper towel to drain. Drain, leaving 1 tbsp (15 mL) drippings in saucepan.

item.title: Chocolate Chip Banana Muffins
item.description:
Dry Mixture:

  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1/2 cup chocolate chips

Wet Ingredients:

  • 1/2 cup margarine
  • 3/4 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup ripe bananas, mashed

Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and prepare muffin cups. Mix flour, baking soda, and salt. Stir in chocolate chips. Cream together margarine and sugar with electric mixer. Beat in eggs 1 at a time. Mash bananas and add bananas and vanilla to wet ingredients. Add wet ingredients to dry mixture all at once. Stir (do not use mixer) until mixture is moist but not lumpy. Fill muffin cups 3/4 full with batter. Bake 25 minutes at 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Yield: 12 muffins.

item.title: Buffalo Chicken Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 chicken breast
  • 1 brick of cream cheese, softened
  • 1/2 cup of ranch dressing
  • 1/2 cup (or more) of Franks Red Hot
  • 1 cup cheddar cheese, shredded
  • Salt to taste

Instructions

  1. Season the chicken breast with salt, pepper, and chili flakes.  Bake in the oven or a pan.
  2. Shred the chicken and add the Frank’s Red Hot to it.  Mix it up.  (This can be left in the fridge overnight or can go to the next step right away).
  3. In a separate bowl, combine the cream cheese and ranch dressing.  Mix until mostly smooth.
  4. Add in the chicken/hot sauce mixture with a 1/2 cup of the cheddar cheese and mix till combined.
  5. Put the dip in a baking pan (8x8 works well) and cover with the remaining cheese.
  6. Bake at 350 for about 20 mins or until it starts bubbling.

item.title: Beer-Can Chicken
item.description:
• 1 can (12 oz.) beer
  • 1 chicken (3 1/2 to 4 lbs)
  • 2 tbsp or your favourite chicken rub (or Basic Barbecue Rub)

Pop the tab off the beer can. Using a church key-style can opener, make a few more holes in the top of the can. Pour out half the beer into your mouth and enjoy!

Set up the grill for indirect grilling (ie: hot side not where you’re putting the bird) and preheat to medium. If using a charcoal grill, place a large drip pan in the centre.

item.title: Beef Jerky
item.description:
• 1/3 Cup soy sauce

  • 3 Tbs Brown Sugar

  • 2 Tsp Worcestershire Sauce

  • 1/4 Tsp Onion Powder

  • 1/4 Tsp Ground Ginger

  • 1/4 Tsp Garlic Powder

  • 1/4 Tsp Liquid Smoke

  • 1/2 Tsp Table Salt

  • 1 Lb of Lean Beef, fat removed (about 1 inch thick)

Mix first 8 ingredients in bowl. Set aside.

Cut meat across the grain into 1/2 to 1/4 inch (3 to 6 mm) thick slices. Do not cut too thick or center won’t dry. Using partially frozen meat makes this easier to do. Add beef strips to ingredients in bowl. Stir to coat. Cover. Let stand in refrigerator overnight. Draw strips across edge of bowl to remove excess marinade. Place wire cooling tracks in baking pans with sides. Arrange single layer of beef strips over and rearrange pans at half time. Fills 2 tall, narrow jars. Store airtight in cool location or in refrigerator. Freeze for long-term storage. Makes 1/2 pound (225g).

item.title: BBQ Chicken
item.description:
• 2 lb chicken breasts
  • 2 tbsp shortening
  • 1 can tomato soup
  • 1/2 cup chopped onion
  • 1/2 cup chopped celery
  • 2 tbsp brown sugar
  • 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 2 tbsp lemon juice
  • 2 tsp dry mustard

Brown chicken in shortening. Stir in other ingredients. Cover and simmer for 45 minutes until chicken is tender. Serve on a bed of cooked rice.

NOTES:

Ingredients

  • Considering all of the strong flavoured ingredients in this, it didn’t turn out as strong as I thought it would. Still was pretty good though.
  • 4 out of 5

Credit to Camp Lau-Ren’s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook

item.title: Banana Butter
item.description:
• 1 Cup Mashed Banana

  • 1 Cup Canned Crushed Pineapple

  • 2 Tsp. Lemon Juice (Fresh or Bottled)

  • 2 Tbsp. Chopped Maraschino Cherries

  • 3 1/2 Cups Granulated Sugar

  • 3 oz. Liquid Pectin

Measure first 5 ingredients into large saucepan. Heat and stir constantly as you bring to a rolling boil. Boil 1 minute. Remove from heat.

Sitr in pectin. Sitr and skim for 5 minutes so fruit won’t float. Pour into hot sterilized half pint jars to within 1/4 inch (0.5 cm) of top. Place sterilized metal lids on jars and screw metal bands on securely. For added assurance against spoilage, you may choose to process for 5 minutes in a boiling water bath. Makes 4 half pints.

item.title: Basic Waffles
item.description:
From the cookbook that came with our waffle iron.

Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 2 tablespoons granulated sugar
  • 1 tablespoon baking powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1 1/3 cups reduced-fat milk
  • 6 tablespoons of vegetable oil
  • 2 large eggs

Instructions

Place ingredients in large mixing bowl and combine until well blended and smooth. Let batter rest 5 minutes before using. Preheat your Waffle Iron on setting #3 (pretty specific to mine, so use your own judgement).

item.title: Balsamic "Caprese" Rice Bowl
item.description:
Ingredients

Phase 1: For the Steamer

  • 2 cups Long-grain rice (Basmati or Jasmine, rinsed)
  • 3 cups Broth (Chicken or Vegetable) or water
  • 6 cloves Garlic, smashed
  • 2 tbsp Butter or Olive oil
  • 2 pinches of Sea salt

Phase 2: The Fresh Components

  • 3 Roma tomatoes, diced
  • 1 cup Fresh basil, sliced into ribbons
  • 1 cup Shredded cheese (Mozzarella or Italian blend)
  • 4 tbsp Balsamic vinegar (or 1 tbsp Balsamic Glaze)

Instructions

item.title: Bacon and Cheese Quiche
item.description:
• 8 bacon slices, diced
  • 1/2 cup finely chopped onion
  • 1/2 cup grated medium Cheddar (or Swiss) cheese
  • 1 unbaked 9 inch (22 cm) pie shell
  • 3 large eggs
  • 1/2 cup milk
  • 1/2 cup evaporated milk
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • 2 tbsp grated parmesan cheese

Fry bacon and onion in frying pan on medium-high for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until bacon is golden. Drain. Cool.

Sprinkle Cheddar cheese in bottom of pie shell. Sprinkle bacon mixture over cheese.

item.title: Apple Dabble Cake
item.description:
• 2 cups sugar
  • 1 cup vegetable oil
  • 3 eggs
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 3 cups flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 4 cups raw apples, chopped

Mix ingredients by hand in order given. Pour into 13x9 inch pan. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 to 40 minutes.

Combine 1 cup of brown sugar, 1/4 cup milk, 1/4 cup butter. Boil for 4 minutes. Pour over baked cake and return to oven for a few minutes. This is scrumptious!

item.title: Spicy Black Bean Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Medium onion, finely chopped
  • 4 Garlic Cloves, minced
  • 1/2 Tsp Red Pepper Flakes. (or more if you like it with a bit more zing)
  • 45 oz of canned Black Beans (3 15 oz cans)
  • 14 1/2 oz of chicken broth (or vegetable broth)
  • 1 10 oz can of tomatoes with green chilies (such as Rotel Tomatoes)
  • 1 11 oz can of corn

Instructions

  1. Coat bottom of large stockpot with cooking spray. Add onion and garlic and cook, stirring frequently, until onions are soft but not brown, about 5 minutes.
  2. Place one can of beans in blender; add sautéed onion mixture, and red pepper flakes. Cover and blend on high until smooth, about 30 seconds. Pour mixture back into stockpot.
  3. Place second can of beans and broth in blender and puree until smooth; add to stockpot.
  4. Stir third can of beans (do not puree first), chicken stock, tomatoes and green chilies, and corn into stockpot. Bring to a boil, lower heat to medium and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes. Garnish with sour cream.
  5. Yields about 1 1/2 cups of soup per serving.

Billy’s Notes:I couldn’t find 15 oz cans of black beans, so I just dived all my black beans into thirds, for how the recipe plays out.

item.title: Mexican Black Bean and Corn Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 can of black beans, drained and rinsed
  • 1 can of corn, drained
  • 1/2 cup of salsa (no sugar added)
  • 2 Tbsp non-fat Greek yogurt (or sour cream)
  • salt and pepper to taste

Optional ingredients I’ve tried:

  • Cilantro (chopped up) - added a good lemony and more of a sunny vacation-y flavour to it
  • Chopped up jalapenos - little more zing is never a bad idea!

Pour the beans + corn into a bowl.

item.title: Melt In Your Mouth Chicken Breasts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 boneless chicken breasts
  • 1 cup Greek yogurt (or mayo if you’re going a little less healthy)
  • 1/2 cup parmesan cheese
  • 1/2 tsp seasoned salt
  • 1/2 tsp black pepper
  • 1 tsp garlic powder

Instructions

  1. Mix mayonnaise or yogurt, cheese and seasonings.
  2. Spread mixture over chicken breasts and place in baking dish.
  3. Bake at 375°F for 30 minutes.

Credit for this recipe goes to Food.com:

Melt-In-Your-Mouth Chicken Breasts Recipe - Food.comThe mayonnaise make these chicken breast very moist and tender–enjoy!

item.title: Low Fat Sweet and Sour Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

Note:  Make the sauce below for 1 cup of pre-cooked rice.  Just double or triple it for however many cups of rice you have.

Make the rice however you make your rice.  This isn’t a rice recipe - it’s more of a sauce recipe.

  • 2 Tbsp low sodium soya sauce
  • 2 Tbsp rice vinegar
  • 1.5 Tbsp pineapple juice  (NOTE: don’t be a dope like me and buy pineapple juice.  Just use the juice from the can of pineapple, noted below)
  • 1 Tbsp tomato paste
  • 1 Tbsp brown sugar
  • 1 Tsp corn starch
  • 1 Tbsp water (for corn starch slurry)
  • 1 Medium carrot, julienned
  • 1 Medium red bell pepper, cut thinly
  • 1 Medium green bell pepper, cut thinly
  • 1/2 cup of pineapple chunks (optional, but really, there’s pineapple juice in it for cryin’ out loud.  If you don’t like pineapple, don’t make this)

Instructions

  1. Cook your rice ahead of time however you cook rice.
  2. In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, pineapple juice, tomato paste, and brown sugar to create the sweet and sour sauce. Stir until well combined.
  3. In a separate small bowl, dissolve the cornstarch in 1 tablespoon of water to create a slurry. Set aside.
  4. Heat a large nonstick skillet or wok over medium heat. Add the julienned carrots and sliced bell peppers. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until they are slightly softened but still have some crunch.
  5. Pour the sweet and sour sauce into the skillet with the vegetables. Stir well to coat the vegetables evenly.
  6. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. Gradually add the cornstarch slurry while stirring to thicken the sauce. Allow it to cook for 2-3 minutes until the sauce reaches the desired consistency.
  7. If using, gently fold in the pineapple chunks and cook for an additional minute to warm them through.
  8. Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and serve it onto plates or bowls. Spoon the sweet and sour vegetable mixture over the rice.

Credit for this recipe goes to Snap Calorie:

item.title: Greek Yogurt Smoothie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup unsweetened Greek yogurt
  • 3/4 cup unsweetened almond milk
  • 1 tsp stevia (optional)
  • 1 cup of fresh fruit (or drained canned fruit or frozen fruit).  Feel free to mix it up.
  • 1 tbsp chia seeds
  • 2 tbsp oatmeal

Instructions

Chuck it all into a blender.  Drink it.

Weight Watchers Points: 2

Billy’s Notes: The points are pretty much the chia seeds and oatmeal.  If you don’t want the benefits those 2 things bring with them, this is a 0 point smoothie.

item.title: Fruit Protein Pancake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 bananas
  • 2 eggs
  • 3 Tbps rolled oats
  • Olive oil

Instructions

  1. Whisk both eggs in a bowl.
  2. Mash up just 1 of the 3 bananas on a plate, using a fork.  Add to eggs and mix.
  3. Add the rolled oats to the egg/banana mix as well.
  4. Cut up the other 2 bananas in slices.
  5. In a pan (that you can cover), drizzle some olive oil to make sure the bottom is barely covered.
  6. Lay down all of the banana slices evenly around the pan, then pour the egg mixture over them.  Even it out with a spoon or your spatula.
  7. Cover and cook on low for 10 mins.
  8. Flip it over and cook on low for another 5 mins (still covered).  Pro-tip: Saw a neat trick to hold a plate upside down over your pan, tightly, then flip the pan and plate right over - to flip your pancake.  Worked like a charm!
  9. Cut it up and put some syrup or whipped cream or whatever you want to jazz it up!

Weight Watchers Points: 1 point for 1/3 of the pancake.

item.title: Egg Drop Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 cups chicken broth
  • 3 eggs
  • 1/3 cup cold water
  • 3 tbsp corn starch

Instructions

  1. Mix the corn starch slurry (the corn starch and the water) in a bowl
  2. Bring the chicken broth to a simmer in a medium pot
  3. Slowly stir the corn starch slurry into the chicken broth
  4. Let it simmer for around 2-3 mins, stirring occasionally
  5. Whisk the eggs in a separate bowl
  6. Slowly stir the eggs into the soup, while stirring.  The more you stir, the finer the egg “strands” will be.  Think the same technique as making scrambled eggs.  Lots of stirring, little chunks.  Less stirring, big chunks.
  7. Let that simmer for a few mins and enjoy.

Billy’s Note:I put a couple of cups of kimchi in this and it gave it some nice color, texture, and spice.  I’m going to put chicken in it next time to get more protein in it.

item.title: Yankee Pot Roast Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

READY IN:

1hr 45mins

SERVES:

8-10

INGREDIENTS

  • 2 lbsstewing beef, cut in 1 inch cubes (or cut up pot roast)
  • 1 teaspoonsalt
  • 1 teaspoonpepper
  • 1 1⁄2tablespoonsfresh garlic, minced
  • 3 tablespoonsbutter
  • 2 mediumonions, cut in chunks
  • 2 mediumcarrots, cut in thick slices (or I use whole baby carrots)
  • 4 red potatoes, cut in chunks (whatever you consider a decent bite size)
  • 2 stalkscelery, cut in thick slices
  • 1 quartchicken broth(I use the Campbells 900 ml ready-to-serve carton)
  • 1 quartbeef broth
  • 1(156 ml) cantomato paste
  • 2bay leaves
  • 1 teaspoon maggi seasoning sauce or 1 teaspoon bouquet garni liquid seasoning (no clue what this was and didn’t use it)

DIRECTIONS

item.title: Turkey Stuffing Burgers
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 beaten egg
  • 1/2 cup Stove Top stuffing mix
  • 1 Tbsp dried onion
  • 1 Tsp chicken bouillion powder
  • 1/4 cup water
  • a squirt ofWorcestershire sauce (optional)
  • 1 lb ground turkey
  • 1 cup Stove Top stuff mix (crushed fine)

Instructions

  1. In bowl combine egg 1/4 cup water; stir in 1/2 cup stuffing mix, dried onion, a shot of Worcestershire sauce, and bouillon. Let stand for five minutes.
  2. Add turkey and mix well; shape into 1/2 inch patties.  (This made 5 patties for me)
  3. Coat with crushed stuffing mix.  (Make sure you crush it well enough or it won’t stick!)
  4. Cook patties in skillet coated with oil about 10 minutes per side until thoroughly cooked.

Billy’s Notes:These were fantastic!  We’re going to try them with some kind of cranberry mayo or something next time.  Super good!

item.title: The Perfect Basic Burger
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 egg
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon ground black pepper
  • 1 pound ground beef
  • 1/2 cup fine dry bread crumbs

Instructions

  1. Preheat an outdoor grill for high heat and lightly oil grate.
  2. In a medium bowl, whisk together egg, salt and pepper. Place ground beef and bread crumbs into the mixture. With hands or a fork, mix until well blended. Form into 4 patties approximately 3/4 inch thick.
  3. Place patties on the prepared grill. Cover and cook 6 to 8 minutes per side, or to desired doneness.

Billy’s Notes: While I like burgers with a lot of stuff in them, I also like plain jane patties. This did the trick. Just enough extra stuff to make sure they don’t fall apart, these ones are pretty good.

item.title: The Keg Copycat Cheesy Garlic Toast
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Loaf French or ciabatta type bread - sliced in half, horizontally
  • 1/2 Cup butter - softened
  • 1/2 Cup mayonnaise
  • 1/4 Cup cheddar cheese - shredded
  • 1/4 Cup mozzarella cheese - shredded
  • 1 1/2 Tbsp garlic - minced
  • 1/4 Tsp oregano to finish

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven to 425 degrees.
  2. Mix all of the ingredients together, except for the oregano.
  3. Slice a loaf of French bread horizontally and spread 1/2 the mix on the bread.
  4. Bake for 10 mins, or until the cheese is toasted and browned.
  5. Spread the other 1/2 of the mix on the bread and let it melt in there (don’t put it back into the oven!)
  6. Sprinkle with oregano to finish.

Compliments to The Sassy Foodie for this recipe (although I tweaked it and made it better!):

item.title: The Best Lemon Bars
item.description:
Ingredients

Crust:

  • 2 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 cup butter, softened
  • 1/2 cup white sugar

Filling:

  • 1 1/2 cups white sugar
  • 1/4 cup all-purpose flour
  • 4 eggs
  • 2 lemons, juiced

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
  2. To make the crust: Blend 2 cups flour, softened butter, and 1/2 cup sugar in a medium bowl until well combined.
  3. Press crust into the bottom of an ungreased 9x13-inch pan.
  4. Bake crust in the preheated oven until firm and golden, about 15 minutes.
  5. Meanwhile, make the filling: Whisk remaining 1 1/2 cups sugar and 1/4 cup flour in a medium bowl. Whisk in eggs, then lemon juice until smooth.
  6. Pour filling over the baked crust.
  7. Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes. Set the pan aside to cool completely; the bars will firm up as they cool.
  8. When cooled, cut bars into uniform squares.

Credit for this recipe goes toAllrecipes.com:

item.title: The Best Chocolate Chip Cookie Recipe Ever
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cupsalted buttersoftened
  • 1 cupgranulated sugar
  • 1 cuplight brown sugarpacked
  • 2 teaspoonspure vanilla extract
  • 2 large eggs
  • 3 cupsall-purpose flour
  • 1 teaspoonbaking soda
  • ½ teaspoonbaking powder
  • 1 teaspoonsea salt
  • 2 cupschocolate chips(14 oz)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F. Line three baking sheets with parchment paper and set aside.
  2. In a medium bowl mix flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt. Set aside.
  3. Cream together butter and sugars until combined.
  4. Beat in eggs and vanilla until light (about 1 minute).
  5. Mix in the dry ingredients until combined.
  6. Add chocolate chips and mix well.
  7. Roll 2-3 Tablespoons (depending on how large you like your cookies) of dough at a time into balls and place them evenly spaced on your prepared cookie sheets.
  8. Bake in preheated oven for approximately 8-10 minutes. Take them out when they are justbarelystarting to turn brown.
  9. Let them sit on the baking pan for 5 minutes before removing to cooling rack.

Billy’s Notes:  These are fantastic!  Not only are they pretty easy to make (not gummy dough, so easy to roll in balls…heh, heh, balls), but they are moist and taste kickass.  Keep an eye on them in the oven though.  I’d only done 7 mins and they were perfect.

item.title: Teriyaki Chicken Sushi Roll
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Chicken breast
  • Sesame oil
  • Olive oil
  • Sea salt
  • Thick teriyaki sauce
  • Nori sheet
  • 120 g of cooked, seasonedsushi rice
  • Sliced cucumber batons
  • ½ peeled avocado

NOTE: There are a lot of recipes to make the actual sushi rice, but I’ve usedTHIS ONE(made in the Instant Pot). It turns out pretty good.

The instructions for this one are a bit detailed to paste here, so please follow the link below to the original recipe from MakeSushi.com:

item.title: Southwestern Beef Chili with Corn
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 2 carrots, chopped
  • 1 onion, chopped
  • 1 poblano or bell pepper, chopped
  • 1/2 pound ground beef
  • 2 tablespoons tomato paste
  • 2 15-ounce cans black beans, rinsed
  • 1 tablespoon chili powder
  • kosher salt and black pepper
  • 1/2 cup corn kernels (from 1 ear, or frozen and thawed)
  • 1/2 cup grated Cheddar (2 ounces)
  • 2 scallions, sliced

How to Make It

Step 1

Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the carrots, onion, and poblano and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes.

item.title: Spicy Chicken Sushi Roll
item.description:
Ingredients

Spicy Chicken

  • 1 cup shredded chicken (canned or rotisserie)
  • 3 ½ tablespoons mayo, more to taste
  • 1 tablespoon Sriracha

Sushi

  • 2 sheets seaweed, cut in half
  • 1 ½ cups cooked rice (short grain preferred)
  • Rice seasoning
  • 1 Persian cucumber, sliced (or regular cucumbers)
  • 1 large avocado, sliced

NOTE: There are a lot of recipes to make the actual sushi rice, but I’ve usedTHIS ONE(made in the Instant Pot). It turns out pretty good.

item.title: Potato Soup (Outback Steakhouse)
item.description:
Outback Steakhouse Potato Soup

Ingredients

  • Potatoes (4 large or up to 8 small potatoes)
  • Kosher Salt
  • Vegetable Oil
  • 2 sticks of butter
  • 1 Onion, diced
  • 3/4 cup of all purpose flour
  • 48 oz of chicken broth
  • 2 cups water
  • Celery salt to taste
  • 1/2 tsp ground black pepper
  • 1/4 tsp dried basil
  • 1/4 tsp sugar
  • 1 1/2 cups heavy cream
  • 4-6 slices of cooked bacon, chopped
  • Chopped chives (depends on your preference)
  • 2 cups shredded Monterrey Jack / Cheddar cheese blend.

Method

Preheat your oven to 350 degrees

item.title: Pickled Mushrooms
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lb mushrooms
  • 6 garlic cloves
  • 1/2 cupwhite vinegar5% acidity
  • 2 cup water
  • 1 tbspsugar
  • 1 tbspsalt
  • 6bay leaves
  • 8 whole peppercorns
  • 6 tbsp vinegar

Instructions

  1. Clean and wash mushrooms, and put them in a pot. Fill with enough water to cover the ‘shrooms.
  2. Add the 1/2 cup of vinegar to the water, bring it to a boil and cook for 10 mins. (The recipe says 15, but I wanted my ‘shrooms with a bit of body to them, so I’ll try it with 10 mins next time). At 15 mins, they came out more like the consistency of canned mushrooms.
  3. When mushrooms are cooked, drain the liquid.
  4. Now, the marinade: In a separate pot, place the 2 cups of water, salt, sugar, bay leaves, and peppercorns. Bring them to a boil.
  5. Once the sugar and salt have dissolved, add the 6 tbsp of vinegar and remove from the heat and set aside.
  6. Clean and dice the garlic into small pieces. Place the diced garlic on the bottom of the jars.
  7. Fill the jars with the cooked mushrooms.
  8. Pour marinade over the mushrooms. Cover, using lids and store them in the refrigerator. They can be stored in the fridge up to several weeks.

Credit for this recipe goes to Mom’s Dish:

item.title: Pan Seared Steak with Garlic Butter
item.description:
• First start out by letting the steaks rest at room temperature for 30 minutes (this brings them a little closer to room temp to cook more evenly).
  • Heat vegetable oil in a 12-inch cast iron skillet over medium-high heat (you want to let it get hot, preheating should take about 3 minutes if using a gas stove then more for electric.). I like to turn on vent fan above stove.
  • Dab both sides of steak dry with paper towels then season both sides with salt and pepper (some people might recommend to wait until the end on the pepper because it can burn but I’ve never tasted burnt pepper).
  • Place steaks in skillet and using metal tongs press down across top surface of steaks to ensure the entire bottom surface is making direct contact with skillet.
  • Let cook until browned on bottom, about 3 minutes.
  • Flip and continue to cook until steak is about 10 degrees away from desired doneness (see notes below for temps) approximately 3 minutes longer.

Ingredients

  • Reduce heat to medium-low, add garlic butter on top of steak in pats. I used the recipe found here -Garlic Butter Recipe. Using an oven mitt grasp pan and tilt pan so butter pools to one side, spoon butter over steaks and continue to cook until steaks registers temperature of desired doneness, about 1 minute longer.
  • Transfer to a plates, let rest 5 minutes before slicing.

Credit for this method goes to Cooking Classy:

item.title: Olive Oil Pasta
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pound of spaghetti (I just guesstimated and used my own fettucini noodles)

  • 2 tbsp of salt

  • 1/4 cup + 2 tbps of extra-virgin olive oil

  • 8 garlic cloves, minced

  • 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes (less if you want it a bit less spicy - 1/4 tsp was good for the girls)

  • 2 tsp of lemon juice

  • 2 tsp of parsley

  • 1/2 tsp of salt (or more to taste)

item.title: Montreal Bagels
item.description:
Montreal Style Bagels

Dough

  • 1 cup (227g) lukewarm water
  • 2 teaspoons maple syrup
  • 2 teaspoons instant yeast or active dry yeast
  • 3 1/4 to 3 1/2 cups (390g to 418g) King Arthur Unbleached Bread Flour
  • 1 teaspoon salt

Topping

  • 3/4 cup (106g) sesame or poppy seeds

Boiling Liquid

  • 3 quarts (2722g) water
  • 1/3 cup (113g) honey or 1/4 cup (85g) barley malt syrup
  1. For the dough: Combine all of the ingredients, using the lesser amount of flour, and mix until a firm dough forms.
  2. Use the remaining 1/4 cup flour as needed to knead the dough by hand for 10 to 12 minutes, or at medium speed in your mixer with the dough hook for 8 minutes. The dough should be smooth and elastic and not stick to your fingers. When poked with your finger, it should bounce back.
  3. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise for 1 1/2 hours, until almost doubled in bulk.
  4. Line a baking sheet with parchment. Place the sesame (or poppy) seeds in a shallow bowl.
  5. After the first rise, deflate the dough and divide into 8 equal pieces.
  6. Roll each piece into a 6" rope. Wrap the rope around your fingers and overlap the ends. Roll your palm over the seam to seal.
  7. Place them on the baking sheet, cover, and let rise while you set up the boiling liquid and preheat your oven with a baking stone in it to 475°F.
  8. To boil: Fill a large saucepan with 3" of water. Add the honey (or barley malt syrup). Place over medium-high heat and bring the water to a boil.
  9. The bagels are ready to cook when they’ve risen very slightly (the surface should still bounce back when lightly touched).
  10. Reduce the water to a simmer, then slip two or three bagels into it. Cook on one side for a minute, then flip over and cook for another minute.
  11. Remove with a slotted spoon and dredge in the seeds. Flip the baking sheet over and put the parchment on the back; place the boiled bagels on the parchment.
  12. Once all of the bagels are boiled, slide the parchment with the bagels on it onto the baking stone and bake for 18 to 23 minutes, until deep golden brown.
  13. Remove from the oven and cool on a rack before serving.
  14. Store in a paper bag on the counter for one day; or slice and freeze for up to two months for longer storage.

Billy’s Notes:These were actually pretty easy, even though there are a few steps. The one thing worth noting is that I only had them in for around 15 mins, but my oven seems to run hot. Keep an eye on them!!

item.title: Hurry Up Chicken Pot Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2cups chopped cooked chicken breasts
  • 2hard-boiled eggs, sliced
  • 1⁄2cup thinly slicedcarrot
  • 1⁄2cupfrozen green pea
  • 1(10 3/4 ounce) can cream of chicken soup
  • 1cupchicken broth
  • salt and pepper (optional)
  • 11⁄2cups instantbiscuit mix
  • 1cupmilk
  • 1⁄2cup meltedbutter

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
  2. In a greased 2-quart casserole, layer the chicken, eggs, carrots, and peas.
  3. Mix the soup, chicken broth, and season with salt and pepper, if desired; pour over the layers.
  4. Stir together the biscuit mix and milk, and pour this over the casserole.
  5. Drizzle butter over the topping.
  6. Bake until the topping is golden brown, 30 to 40 minutes.

Credit for this recipe goes to Paula Deen:

item.title: Maya's Cookie Brownie Recipe
item.description:
Maya’s Cookie Brownie Recipe (Blondies)

Ingredients

  • ½ cup unsalted butter
  • 2 teaspoons vanilla
  • ¼ teaspoon to ½ salt
  • ½ cup packed brown sugar
  • ½ cup granulated sugar
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ½ cup of chocolate chips

Instructions

Instructions:

  1. Preheat the oven to 350F degrees. Line an 8x8 inch square baking pan with aluminium foil leaving an overhang on each side and spray lightly with non-stick cooking spray. Or line the pan with parchment paper. Set aside.

item.title: Homemade Pasta/Noodles
item.description:
This is a mish-mash of a few different recipes for this I’ve seen on the web, along with a few different techniques. No single one had all the ingredients and info in the same spot, so I figured I’d just write it up myself. If it sucks, I’m sorry. :(

Ingredients

  • 2 cups of flour (I just use regular flour, but the internet nerds say to use semolina and some other weird stuff. Keep it simple!)
  • 2 eggs
  • 1/2 tsp olive oil
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1/2 cup of water (just keep it handy - DO NOT just dump it in!! See the recipe below for the deets)

NOTE: This is a doubled-up recipes that makes around 4 good portions with enough left over for lunch the next day.

item.title: Hearty Chicken and Vegetable Skillet
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 red bell pepper, chopped
  • 1 zucchini, chopped
  • 1 (14.5 ounce) can diced tomatoes, undrained
  • 1/2 cup chicken broth
  • 1 tablespoon tomato paste
  • 1 teaspoon dried Italian seasoning
  • 1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
  • 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley, for garnish

Instructions

  1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
  2. Cook the chicken:Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper. Add them to the hot skillet and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
  3. Sauté the vegetables:Add the chopped onion and red bell pepper to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the chopped zucchini and cook for an additional 3-5 minutes, until slightly tender.
  4. Build the sauce:Stir in the diced tomatoes (with their juice), chicken broth, tomato paste, Italian seasoning, and smoked paprika. Bring the sauce to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
  5. Return the chicken:Add the cooked chicken pieces back into the skillet with the vegetable and tomato sauce. Stir to combine and heat through for a couple of minutes.
  6. Serve:Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.

item.title: Garlic Butter
item.description: Billy’s Notes: I figured I’d mention, before this recipe, that I didn’t make it like the recipe states and it turned out really good. I’m sure the recipe is good, but I wanted garlic butter that I could put in the fridge and use whenever (not a sauce). I mixed all the ingredients together as there recipe states, but then formed it into a log in parchment paper and put it in the fridge to harden. It freezes well and then you can just cut off chunks as you need them

item.title: Easy Moist Banana Blueberry Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 large ripe bananas
  • 3/4 cup of sugar
  • 1 egg, slightly beaten
  • 1/3 cup of melted butter
  • 1 cup of blueberries
  • 1 tsp of baking soda
  • 1 tsp of baking powder
  • 1/2 tsp of salt
  • 1 1/2 cups of flour

Instructions

  • Mash bananas in a large mixing bowl.
  • Add sugar and egg.
  • Add butter and blueberries.
  • Combine dry ingredients and gently stir into banana mixture.
  • Pour into 12 well-greased muffin cups.
  • Bake at 375 degrees for 20 minutes.

Credit for this recipe goes to Food.com:

item.title: Dutch Oven No-Knead Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 1/2 cups warm water (about 100 degrees F)
  • 1 packet yeast (active dry, instant, or quick rise- 2 1/4 teaspoons)
  • 1 ½ tsp salt
  • 3 1/4 cups all-purpose flour

Instructions

  1. In a large bowl(or container), mix together the warm water(1 1/2 cups), yeast(1 packet/2 1/4 teaspoons), and table salt(1 1/2 teaspoon), until yeast and salt are fully or almost dissolved(I like using a whisk for this).
  2. Add the flour(3 1/4 cups)to the bowl all at once and stir together until a sticky dough forms. Don’t worry about mixing it too much, just make sure everything is uniformly wet. It WILL be messy and sticky. You can scrape what’s left on the spoon with a silicone spatula.
  3. Cover the bowl with a kitchen towel(or place the container to the lid on ajar).Leave it for 2-3 hours to rise in a somewhat warm place until it’s doubled in volume.
  4. 30 minutes to an hour before you are ready to bake, preheat your oven to 450 degrees F. Place your Dutch oven in the oven, with the lid ON, while the oven is preheating. Once the oven reaches 450, keep it preheating for another 20 minutes, so the Dutch oven gets good and hot.
  5. Scrape the dough into the edge of a piece of parchment paper dusted with flour. Make it into as much of a “loaf” shape as you can by folding the edges up on top of it(a silicone spatula works well for this).
  6. Then, use the edges of the parchment paper to flip the loaf over so the floured side is on top and the loaf is in the center of the parchment paper. Don’t worry about it looking beautiful or smooth on top. Also doesn’t matter if your dutch oven is round or oval.
  7. Use a very sharp knife or clean razor blade to cut a few slits across the top of the loaf about 1/2" deep, across the entire top. I did this in a cross shape for mine.
  8. Take out the preheated Dutch oven and take off the lid, carefully! Grab the parchment from the sides and place the loaf in your Dutch oven.
  9. Place the cover back on the Dutch ovenand place in the preheated oven on the center rack. Bake for 35 minutes at 450 degrees F. Remove the lid and bake for another 5-10 minutes, until a golden brown color forms- longer if necessary. Remove the loaf from the dutch oven(grabbing the sides of the parchment paper works well here)and place it on a wire rack or cutting board to cool for at least 10 minutes(preferably 30 minutes – an hour, if you have time)before slicing.

Billy’s Notes:This was really good!  Pretty easy too.  Takes some time, but pretty easy to make if you need a declicious and fancy-looking loaf of bread in a pinch.  Like Mom’s recipe, I chose to butter the top of my loaf when I pulled it out of the oven and it softened it up and made it even more delicious-er.

item.title: Crock Pot Venison Stew
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 or 4 lb. venison roast
  • 6 beef bouillon cubes
  • 1 onion, diced
  • 1/2 tsp. garlic powder
  • 5-6 carrots, thick sliced
  • 5-6 med. potatoes, wedged

Instructions

Wash meat, trim fat. Place in crock pot with bouillon cubes, cover with water (about 2 quarts). Add onion and garlic powder. Cook on High overnight about 8 hours. In the A.M. add carrots, potatoes and continue to cook on High 4 to 5 hours until potatoes are tender. Serve as is or may be thickened with cornstarch.

item.title: Dad's Creamy Cucumber Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 large cucumbers, peeled and thinly sliced
  • 1 sweet onion, thinly sliced
  • 1 tablespoon sea salt

Dressing

  • 1 ½ cups mayonnaise, or more to taste
  • 2 tablespoons vinegar
  • 1 tablespoon white sugar
  • 1 teaspoon dried dill weed
  • 1 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1 teaspoon ground black pepper

Instructions

  1. Mix cucumbers, onion, and salt together in a bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and let sit for 15 to 30 minutes.
  2. Turn cucumber mixture into a colander set over a bowl or in a sink. Let drain, stirring occasionally, until most of the liquid and salt has drained, 15 to 30 minutes. Transfer drained cucumber mixture to a large bowl.
  3. Whisk mayonnaise, vinegar, sugar, dill, garlic powder, and pepper for dressing together in a bowl until smooth. Pour over cucumber mixture and stir until coated.
  4. Pour over cucumber mixture and stir until coated.
  5. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours before serving.

Billy’s Notes:  Found this one on the internet and it was really good.  I know I’d tried a few of these but this was easily the best one I’ve tried.

item.title: Crispy Chicken Wings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Dozen chicken wings
  • 1 tbsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 tsps garlic powder
  • Pinch of pepper

Instructions

Adjust oven rack to upper-middle position and preheat oven to 450°F (230°C). Line a rimmed baking sheet with aluminium foil and set a heat-proof wire rack inside.

Pat dry chicken wings liberally with paper towels, squeezing out as much moisture as you can. Transfer them to a large bowl.

In a small bowl, combine the baking powder, garlic powder, salt and pepper together, whisking well to combine, and sprinkle the mixture over the wings. Toss wings through the baking powder mixture until evenly coated.

item.title: Crispy Garlic Baked Potato Wedges
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 large russet potatoes, rinsed
  • 1/4 cup olive oil
  • 1 tablespoon minced garlic, (or 3 teaspoons garlic powder)
  • 1/2 teaspoon onion powder
  • 2 teaspoons salt, (adjust to your tastes)
  • 1 teaspoon paprika
  • 1/2 teaspoon black cracked pepper
  • 2/3 cup finely grated or shredded parmesan cheese, divided
  • 2 tablespoons fresh chopped parsley

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 390°F (200°C). Line 2 large baking sheets with parchment paper; set aside.
  2. Cut each potato in half lengthways, then cut each half in half lengthways again, and then cut each half in half lengthways again until you have 8 wedges. (Make sure they are about the same thickness and size).
  3. In a small jug, combine the oil, garlic, onion powder, salt, paprika annd pepper. Pour the oil mixture over the potatoes and toss together to coat evenly.
  4. Arrange potato wedges in a single layer on a large baking tray, skin-sides-down. Sprinkle with half of the parmesan cheese and extra salt if needed.
  5. Bake for 35 minutes, turning wedges half way through baking time, until golden, crisp and cooked through when tested with a fork.
  6. Sprinkle with parsley and remaining parmesan cheese to serve.

Billy’s Notes: These were great! Some of the best home-made potato things like this I think I’ve tried.

item.title: Creamy Cucumber Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 long English cucumbers
  • Optional: 1/3 cup of sliced onion
  • 1/2 cup of sour cream (or plain Greek yogurt)
  • 3 Tablespoons of Mayonnaise
  • 1/4 Tsp of fresh dill (chopped)
  • 3 Tablespoons of white vinegar
  • 1/2 Teaspoon of white sugar
  • Salt to Taste

Instructions

  1. Peel the cucumbers and cut in half lengthwise. Scrape out the seeds if desired and slice into ¼″ slices.
  2. Combine all dressing ingredients and toss with cucumbers.
  3. Refrigerate 1 hour before serving.

Credit for this recipe goes to Spendwithpennies.com:

item.title: Cora's Creme Anglaise
item.description:
Also known as custard or English creme, this stuff is really good when you dip fruit it in it or put it on a waffle.

Ingredients

  • 1 small box of instant Jell-O Vanilla Instant Pudding
  • 1 cup of 35% cream
  • 2 cups of milk

Instructions

Add the Instant Pudding mix and the milk together in a medium-sized bowl. Mix together until thick.

In another bowl, thoroughly beat 1 cup of 35% cream. Add to the bowl of pudding and

item.title: Chewy Chocolate Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 1/4 cups butter, softened
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 3/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
  • 1 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 2 cups semisweet chocolate chips

Directions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
  2. In a large bowl, cream together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla. Sift together the flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt; stir into the creamed mixture. Mix in the chocolate chips. Drop dough by teaspoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheets.
  3. Bake 8 to 9 minutes in the preheated oven. Cookies will be soft. Cool slightly on cookie sheet; remove from sheet onto wire rack to cool completely.

Here is a link to the actual website. Credit to allrecipes.com:

item.title: Buffalo Wild Wing Secrets
item.description:
Buffalo Wild Wing has to be one of the best chicken wing joints going. Here are a handful of their cooking secrets and sauce recipes.

How to fry your wings:

  • Heat oil or shortening in your fryer to 350°F.
  • Fry wings for 10-12 minutes, or until golden brown.
  • Remove wings and dry on a rack or paper towels briefly.
  • Place in plastic container with a sealable lid. Add 1/4 to 1/3 cup of your preferred sauce (see below) to the container.
  • Close lid and shake well to coat wings.

Buffalo Wild Wing Sauce Recipes:

item.title: California Rolls (Sushi)
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients

  • Juice of 1/2 lemon
  • 1 medium avocado, peeled, pitted, and sliced into 1/4-inch thick pieces
  • 4 sheets nori (dried seaweed paper)
  • Sushi rice - Recipe follows (or use this great Instant Pot recipe I use)
  • 1/3 cup sesame seeds, toasted
  • 1 small cucumber, peeled, seeded, and cut into matchstick-size pieces
  • 4 crabsticks, torn into pieces
  • Pickled ginger, for serving
  • Wasabi, for serving
  • Soy sauce, for serving

Instructions

Directions

Squeeze the lemon juice over the avocado to prevent browning.

item.title: Bread Pudding II
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 slices day-old bread
  • 2 tablespoons butter, melted
  • 1/2 cup raisins (optional)
  • 4 eggs, beaten
  • 2 cups milk
  • 3/4 cup white sugar
  • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Billy’s Note:I didn’t have raisins once, so I subbed in 2 apples cut up in little pieces.  I drizzled caramel sauce on it to make a caramel apple bread pudding.  Worked great!

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
  2. Break bread into small pieces into an 8 inch square baking pan. Drizzle melted butter or margarine over bread. If desired, sprinkle with raisins.
  3. In a medium mixing bowl, combine eggs, milk, sugar, cinnamon, and vanilla. Beat until well mixed. Pour over bread, and lightly push down with a fork until bread is covered and soaking up the egg mixture.
  4. Billy’s Note:I let it soak up all that eggy milkness for around 15 mins here before putting it in the oven.
  5. Bake in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until the top springs back when lightly tapped.

Credit for this recipe goes to AllRecipes.com.  Here is the actual link to their page:

item.title: Blueberry Buttermilk Scones
item.description:
Ingredients

For the scones

  • 2 and 3/4 cupsall-purpose flour
  • 1/3 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 tablespoon baking powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
  • 3/4 teaspoon salt
  • 3/4 cup fresh blueberries
  • 1/2 cup cold unsalted butter cut into pieces
  • 1 tablespoonvanilla extract
  • 1 cup cold buttermilk
  • 1 tablespoon heavy cream or milk
  • 2 tablespoons coarse sugar

For the glaze

Ingredients

  • 1 cup confectioners’ sugar
  • 2-3 tablespoons milk
  • 1/2 teaspoonvanilla extract

Instructions

Instructions

Make the scones

item.title: Best Homemade Salsa Ever!
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 small jalapeno peppers (leave the seeds in for an extra kick)
  • 1/2 onion, chopped
  • 3 garlic cloves (I’d use 1-2 more next time)
  • 1/4 - 1/2 cup of fresh cilantro
  • 6 tomatoes of varying sizes, chopped
  • 1.5 - 2 tsp of ground cumin
  • 1 tsp of sea salt + more to taste
  • 2 tbsp of lemon or lime juice

Instructions

  1. Add all ingredients to the bowl of a food processor. I like to add in the jalapeños, onion and garlic first and then the cilantro and tomatoes, so the tomatoes do not get too liquid-y. Pulse about 10 or so times and until all ingredients are combined and diced, but be sure to stop before the salsa becomes too watery. However, you can pulse longer if you do like your salsa with more liquid.
  2. Taste the salsa and add more ingredients as desired. I would advise stirring in the extra ingredients, not pulsing in the food processor. The salsa will become too watery, quickly.
  3. Transfer to a bowl and enjoy!
  4. I prefer to refrigerate salsa for 1 day prior to serving to allow the flavors to intensify. Salsa will keep for about 1 week in an airtight container or jar in the fridge; however, it’s never lasted that long in this household.

Billy’s Notes: Make sure to “core” your tomatoes and get all the runny bits and seeds out.  Just toss em.  They make the salsa way too watery.  Also, throw everything EXCEPT the tomatoes in the blender or food processor and pulse ’em small. THEN throw your tomatoes in and pulse gently till it’s the consistency you want.  I made the mistake of putting it all in at once and, to get all the other stuff worked in, my salsa was almost gazpacho in the end.

item.title: Beef Stew
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 pounds cubed beef stew meat
  • 3 tablespoons vegetable oil
  • 4 cubes beef bouillon, crumbled
  • 4 cups water
  • 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
  • 1 teaspoon dried parsley
  • 1/2 teaspoon ground black pepper
  • 3 large potatoes, peeled and cubed
  • 4 carrots, cut into 1 inch pieces
  • 4 stalks celery, cut into 1 inch pieces
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 2 teaspoons cornstarch
  • 2 teaspoons cold water

Directions

  1. In a large pot or dutch oven, cook beef in oil over medium heat until brown. Dissolve bouillon in water and pour into pot. Stir in rosemary, parsley and pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover and simmer 1 hour.
  2. Stir potatoes, carrots, celery, and onion into the pot. Dissolve cornstarch in 2 teaspoons cold water and stir into stew. Cover and simmer 1 hour more.

Notes:

item.title: Baked Cream Cheese Chicken Taquitos
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8-10 flour or corn tortillas*
  • 1 cup shredded chicken
  • 2 oz. cream cheese softened (reduced fat will work)*
  • 1/2 cup shredded Monterey-jack cheese
  • 1 clove garlic minced (1/2 tsp.)
  • 1/4 cup salsa verde (I used normal red salsa)
  • 1/4 tsp. ground cumin (I didn’t use this)
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or foil (spray well if using foil) and set aside.
  2. In a large bowl, mix together the chicken, cream cheese, Monterey-jack cheese, garlic, salsa verde, and ground cumin until well combined. Season with salt & pepper to taste.
  3. Spoon two tablespoons of the chicken mixture into each tortilla. Roll each tortilla tightly and place seam side down on the baking sheet. You can secure the tortillas with toothpicks to hold them together a little better.
  4. Bake for 15-17 minutes, broil for the last minute or two to get them a little more crispy.
  5. Serve immediately.

Recipe Notes

If using corn tortillas, make sure to microwave them for 45 seconds-1 minute. This will make them a little easier to work with and less likely to tear.

item.title: Apple Cider Rice Krispie Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2cupunsalted butter, plus more for greasing
  • 2 cupsapple cider or unfiltered apple juice
  • 1 tablespoongranulated sugar
  • 1 teaspoonground cinnamon
  • 210-ounce bags mini marshmallows, divided (about 11 cups total)
  • 1/4teaspoonkosher salt
  • 112-ounce box rice cereal, such as Rice Krispies (about 10 cups)
  • Nonstick cooking spray

Instructions

  1. In a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven set over medium heat, melt the butter. Continue cooking, stirring often to prevent the milk solids from burning, until the butter foams and darkens into a light amber color and smells nutty, 4 to 6 minutes. Immediately add the apple cider, increase the heat to high, and bring to a boil. Let the cider boil, stirring occasionally, until it reduces into a thick, bubbling caramel-like consistency that coats the back of a spoon, 18 to 20 minutes.
  2. As the cider is reducing, grease a 9x13-inch glass or metal baking pan. In a small bowl, combine the sugar and cinnamon.
  3. Once the cider has reduced, add one bag of marshmallows and salt and continue cooking over high heat, stirring constantly, until the marshmallows melt and darken into a light caramel color, 3 to 4 minutes.
  4. Remove the pot from the heat and add the remaining bag of marshmallows. Stir just until the marshmallows are about half melted, then add the rice cereal. Gently but quickly mix everything together until the rice cereal is evenly coated in the marshmallow mixture and immediately transfer into the prepared baking pan. Gently press the mixture into an even layer using a spatula sprayed with nonstick cooking spray. Sprinkle the cinnamon sugar mixture over the top and let set for at least 1 hour before cutting.

Credit for this recipe goes to Food52.com:

item.title: Christmas Crack
item.description:
Ingredients

:

  • 1 cup of butter
  • 1 cup of brown sugar
  • 1 bag of Chipits chocolate chips
  • Toppings of choice
  • About 2 cups of pretzel crisps or saltines

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to325degrees Fahrenheitand line a medium cookie tray with parchment paper. Lay out pretzel crisps or saltines on the tray in a single layer until the tray is fully covered.
  2. In a small pot, melt butter and brown sugar together until fully combined. Bring to a boil, reduce temperature to medium-low and stir continuously for 3 minutes.
  3. After the 3 minutes, pour the mixture overtop of the pretzels or saltines on the tray, try to spread it as much as you can but it will spread in the oven!
  4. Bake in the oven for 8-10 minutes until bubbly. Remove from the oven and add your bag of chocolate chips, trying to spread them evenly.
  5. Place back in the oven for 1-2 minutes until the chocolate is melted and can be spread with a rubber spatula or spoon. Add your toppings of choice on top of the spread chocolate (I used skor bits, M&Ms, and sprinkles).
  6. Let the tray cool and then place it in the fridge for 1-2 hours until firm. Remove from the parchment paper and crack or cut into pieces.
  7. Serve and enjoy!

item.title: Chicken Bacon Ranch Pasta
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients:

  • 2 cups of shredded chicken (approximately 2 chicken breasts)
  • 2 tablespoons of dry ranch seasoning
  • 1 cup of pasta of choice (macaroni is good)
  • 3 1/2 cups of chicken or beef broth (beef gives more flavour)
  • 2 tablespoons of milk
  • 4 oz (half a brick) of cream cheese
  • 4-6 slices of bacon crumbled or sliced
  • 2 cups of shredded cheese of choice

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to450 degrees Fahrenheit and cook chicken breasts until fully cooked (about 15-20 minutes).
  2. Add shredded chicken and ranch seasoning into a bowl and combine.
  3. Add chicken, broth, and pasta into a large pot, stir, and bring to a boil.
  4. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and cover for 15-20 minutes until noodles are the preferred texture.

Note:Stir every so often to make sure noodles aren’t sticking to the bottom

item.title: Cinnamon Streusel Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

Cookie Dough:

  • 1/2 cup softened butter
  • 1/2 cup vegetable oil
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 1/2 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 tsp baking powder
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 2 and 1/2 cups flour

Streusel Topping:

  • 1/4 cup softened butter
  • 1/4 cup brown sugar
  • 1/3-1/2 cup flour (depending on the consistency once mixed)
  • Pinch of salt

Icing:

  • 1/2 cup powdered sugar
  • 1 tbsp milk
  • splash of vanilla

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
  2. Combine butter, oil, and brown sugar and mix for a few minutes until light and fluffy. Add egg and vanilla and mix again. Add remaining ingredients and mix until dough forms
  3. For streusel, combine ingredients and mix. If the mixture is too sticky and not crumbly, add more flour.
  4. Scoop 1/4 cup of dough and place on a tray with parchment paper. Make a small indent on the top of the dough ball and add streusel topping into the dent.
  5. Bake for 12-15 minutes. While baking make the icing, combine all ingredients and whisk until a runny icing/glaze forms.
  6. Add icing once cookies are cooled :)

item.title: Chicken Taquitos
item.description:
Ingredients

Chicken:

  • 3 large chicken breasts
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1/4 tsp chili powder
  • 1/4 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/4 tsp cumin
  • 1/4 tsp salt

Filling:

  • 1 block cream cheese
  • 3 tbsp sour cream
  • 1/3 cup salso
  • 2 tsp cumin
  • 1 tsp chili powder
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 and a 1/2 cups of shredded cheese
  • 16-18 small flour tortillas

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to375 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Mix together chili powder, garlic powder, cumin, and salt. Drizzle chicken with olive oil and add seasoning mix to both sides of each chicken breast
  3. Cook the chicken for 20-25 minutes or until fully cooked (internal temp of 165 Fahrenheit). Shred once cooked using 2 forks.
  4. Turn the oven up to 425 degrees Fahrenheit
  5. Mix cream cheese, sour cream, salsa, cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, salt, cheeses, and shredded chicken together in a bowl.
  6. Add a few tablespoons to each tortilla and roll them up.
  7. Place on a parchment lined baking tray or in the airfryer until golden brown (about 10-15 minutes).

item.title: Carrot Cake Cheesecake Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

Cream Cheese Filling:

  • 1 block of cream cheese
  • 1/2 cup powdered sugar
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Cookie Dough:

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 3/4 cup white sugar
  • 1/3 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
  • 4 large carrots
  • 3 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1 tbsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp nutmeg

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Mix room temp cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla together in a bowl
  3. Scoop 1 tablespoon balls of the mixture onto parchment paper and chill in freezer while preparing cookie dough
  4. Beat butter, white sugar, and brown sugar together for about 1-2 minutes. Once combined, mix in egg and vanilla
  5. Shred, pat dry, and then mix carrots in.
  6. Fold in flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
  7. Scoop 1/3 cup of dough, flatten in palm of hand and place a cream cheese ball in the middle. Roll the dough around the cream cheese ball.
  8. Roll cookie dough ball in white sugar (optional)
  9. Place 6 to a tray with lots of room for expansion
  10. Cook 10 minutes, check them, 3 more minutes, check them, 3 more minutes
  11. Allow to cool ON TRAY for 10 minutes

item.title: Boursin One Dish Pasta
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 package of garlic and fine herbs Boursin cheese
  • 2 cups uncooked pasta (bowties work well)
  • 4 cloves of garlic finely chopped
  • 1/2 cup of cubed cheddar cheese
  • 2 cups of chopped fresh broccoli
  • 2 tablespoons of olive oil
  • 2 cups of stock (vegetable or chicken)
  • 1 teaspoon of smoked paprika
  • 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper
  • 1 teaspoon of salt
  • 3/4 shredded cheese (any kind)
  • 1/2 cup of heavy cream (can add extra cheese instead to thicken)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Add Boursin to middle of baking dish, surround with uncooked pasta, broccoli, and cheese cubes
  3. Add olive oil, spices, and stock over top of everything.
  4. Cover with foil and bake for 30 minutes covered.
  5. Remove after 30 minutes. Add shredded cheese and cream, stir and place back in oven uncovered for 10 minutes or until pasta is tender.

If too runny, use the cornstarch and water method to thicken the sauce

item.title: Confetti Cheesecake Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

Cream Cheese Filling:

  • 1 block of cream cheese
  • 1/2 cup powdered sugar
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Cookie Dough:

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 1 and a 1/2 cups granulated sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 and a 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 2 and 3/4 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1/4 cup sprinkles

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Mix room temp cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla together in a bowl
  3. Scoop 1 tablespoon balls of the mixture onto parchment paper and chill in freezer while preparing cookie dough
  4. Beat butter and sugar together for about 1-2 minutes. Once combined, mix in egg and vanilla
  5. Fold in flour, baking powder, and baking soda. Mix in sprinkles.
  6. Scoop 1/3 cup of dough, flatten in palm of hand and place a cream cheese ball in the middle. Roll the dough around the cream cheese ball.
  7. Roll cookie dough ball in white sugar (optional)
  8. Place 6 to a tray with lots of room for expansion
  9. Cook 10 minutes, check them, 3 more minutes, check them, 3 more minutes
  10. Allow to cool ON TRAY for 10 minutes

item.title: Soft Pretzels
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 1/3 cups warm water
  • 1 tbsp active dry yeast
  • 2 3/4 to 3 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tbsp sugar
  • 1 tsp salt, for pretzel dough
  • 1 egg + 1 tbsp, for egg wash
  • Coarse salt, to taste, for finishing pretzels
  • Mustard, for serving (optional)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees F.
  2. Combine warm water with yeast in a small bowl. Let stand 5 minutes.
  3. Combine 2 3/4 cups flour, sugar, and salt in a large bowl.
  4. Add yeast mixture to flour mixture. Stir or mix with your hands to bring dough together, adding 1/4 more flour, as needed.
  5. Sprinkle work surface with flour and knead dough until smooth and elastic.
  6. Divide dough into 12 equal pieces. Roll into 15-inch long ropes. Shape into a pretzel knot. (You could also just make pretzel sticks, if you like.).
  7. Brush each pretzel with egg wash and sprinkle with coarse salt.
  8. Bake until golden brown, about 15 to 20 minutes. Serve with mustard, if desired.

Maddy’s Notes:This was my favourite, from a baking camp I went to! I got queso from Lone Star to dunk them in!

item.title: Zucchini Lasagna
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1½ pounds zucchini (about 3-4 medium zucchini), sliced lengthwise ¼-inch thick
  • Salt, to taste
  • 2 Tablespoons olive oil
  • 1 cup chopped onion (about ½ large onion)
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 pound ground beef (lean is OK)
  • Ground black pepper, to taste
  • 1 (28 oz) can crushed tomatoes
  • 2 Tablespoons chopped fresh basil
  • 1 Tablespoon honey
  • 1½ cups ricotta cheese
  • 1 large egg
  • ⅓ cup Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese
  • 12 ounces (3 cups) shredded mozzarella cheese

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 375°F/190°C.
  2. Using a mandoline slicer or sharp knife, slice the zucchini lengthwise into ¼-inch thick slices.
  3. Place the zucchini slices in a bowl, sprinkle them with salt, and toss to coat the slices evenly. Set them aside for 10-15 minutes. (Salting removes a lot of the excess moisture in the zucchini).
  4. While the zucchini is being salted, heat a large pan over medium heat. Add the olive oil and onion and cook, stirring often, until the onion is soft (about 3-5 minutes).
  5. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
  6. Add the ground beef, season it with salt and pepper, and cook, breaking the meat into small pieces as it browns (about 4 to 5 minutes).
  7. Add the crushed tomatoes, basil, and honey. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for about 10-15 minutes, or until sauce is very thick.
  8. In a medium bowl, mix the ricotta cheese, egg. Parmigiano Reggiano cheese, and pepper. Stir well.
  9. After the zucchini has been sitting for about 10-15 minutes, use paper towels to blot the excess moisture that the zucchini has released.
  10. In a 9 x 13-inch casserole dish, spread about ⅓ of the sauce on the bottom and layer about ⅓ of zucchini slices in a criss-cross pattern to cover the sauce.
  11. Spread ⅓ of the ricotta cheese and egg mixture over the zucchini, and then top that with 1 cup of the shredded mozzarella cheese.
  12. Repeat the layering process until all of your ingredients are used up.
  13. Bake for 30-45 minutes or until the zucchini is cooked through and the cheese is melted and starting to brown.
  14. Let the lasagna stand for about 5-10 minutes before serving.

Credit for this recipe goes to Tip Hero:

item.title: Strawberry Cheesecake Keto Fat Bombs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup = 120 ml = 2.5 oz = 70 g strawberries, fresh or frozen
  • 3/4 cup = 180 ml = 5.3 oz = 150 g cream cheese, softened
  • 1/4 cup = 60 ml = 2 oz = 60 gbutterorcoconut oil, softened
  • 2 tablespoons = 0.7 oz = 20 gpowdered erythritolOR 10–15 dropsliquid stevia
  • 1 vanilla bean OR 1/2–1 tablespoonvanilla extract

Instructions

  1. Place the cream cheese and butter (or coconut oil) cut into small pieces in to a mixing bowl. Leave at room temperature for 30–60 minutes until softened. Make sure the butter is softened, otherwise it will be difficult to mix and achieve a smooth texture. Do not microwave the butter – the mixture needs to stay firm.
  2. Meanwhile, wash the strawberries and remove the green parts. Place them into a bowl and mash using a fork or place in a blender for a smooth texture.
  3. Add the powdered erythritol (or stevia), vanilla extract and mix well. Before you mix the strawberries with the remaining ingredients, make sure they have reached room temperature.
  4. Add to the bowl with softened butter and cream cheese.
  5. Use a hand whisk or food processor and mix until well combined.
  6. Spoon the mixture into small muffin silicon molds or candy molds. Place in the freezer for about 2 hours or until set.
  7. When done, unmold the fat bombs and place into a container. Keep in the freezer and enjoy any time!

Billy’s Notes:We used salted butter and they almost seemed a bit too salty instead of cheesecakey. Maybe used unsalted butter next time. We also used raspberries instead of strawberries, which may have made them tart instead of sweet.

item.title: Sheet Pan Shrimp
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 & 1/2 Lbs Peeled Cooked Shrimp
  • 3 Bell Peppers (1 Red, 1 Yellow, 1 Orange)
  • 3/4 Medium Red Onion
  • 1/2 TBSPOlive Oil
  • 1 TSPPink Himalayan Salt
  • 1 TSPBlack Pepper
  • 2 TSPChili Powder
  • 1/2 TSPCumin
  • 1/2 TSPGranulated Garlic
  • 1/2 TSPOnion Powder
  • 1/2 TSPSmoked Paprika
  • Fresh Cilantro (Optional)
  • Fresh Lime Juice (Optional)

Instructions

  1. Pre-heat the oven to 450*F.
  2. If your Shrimp do not already have the shells peeled off of them or the tails removed, finish removing those and set the peeled shrimp in a large mixing bowl.
  3. De-seed the Bell Peppers and cut them into 1/2″ strips lengthwise, and Cut the Red Onion into 1/4″ Strips (removing the center sections to give the dish a better texture).
  4. Combine the Bell Peppers, Shrimp, and Onion into the Large Mixing Bowl, and pour the 1/2 TBSP ofOlive Oilon top to coat evenly.
  5. Add thePink Himalayan Salt,Ground Black Pepper,Chili Powder,Cumin,Granulated Garlic,Onion Powder, andSmoked Paprikaand stir to evenly season the Shrimp and veggies.
  6. Pour the mixture onto a Large Cookie Sheet and spread out in a single layer so that most of the ingredients are not overlapped.
  7. Bake in the oven for about 8 minutes, then turn the oven up to the Broil setting and broil for about a remaining 2 more minutes. You will want to broil it until you can heard the Sheet Pan Shrimp sizzling in the oven.
  8. Remove from the oven, and serve right away.
  9. If desired you can top the dish with the suggested Fresh Cilantro and some fresh squeezed Lime Juice.

Billy’s Notes: This turned out REALLY good. Even though they’re optional, I recommend the lime juice and cilantro. Was a bit bland before those.

item.title: Low Carb Cream Cheese Fudge
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup Butter (unsalted)
  • 2 oz. Bakers Unsweetened Chocolate
  • 1 Tablespoon Vanilla
  • 8 oz. package Cream Cheese softened
  • 1/2 Cup Slenda or Stevia or any sweetener or your choice to taste (adjust to your taste)

Instructions

  1. Place butter and chocolate in a small pan and melt oven low heat. When chocolate and butter is just melted add vanilla and sweetener and blend together.
