  2. Place cream cheese in a medium bowl and pour chocolate mixture over it.
  3. With a hand mixer mix about 2 minutes.
  4. Pour into a greased pan. I use a 6 x 8 pan and I freeze it and cut into squares.

Billy’s Notes:

item.title: Low Carb Broccoli Cheese Soup
item.description:
• 4 cups broccoli (cut into florets)
  • 3 cups chicken broth (or veggie broth)
  • 3 cups shredded cheddar cheese (a little extra for topping if you wish)
  • 1 cup heavy whipping cream
  • 1/2 package of cream cheese
  • 1 tbsp minced garlic

In a large pot over medium heat, sauté the garlic for about 1 minute or until fragrant (you can add a little butter if you’d like).

Add the chicken broth, heavy cream, and broccoli florets. Bring to a boil, and then reduce the heat to simmer for 10-20 minutes or until the broccoli is tender.

item.title: Keto Pesto Chicken Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 25 oz. chicken thighs or chicken breasts
  • 2 oz. butter, for frying
  • 3 oz. red pesto or green pesto
  • 1½ cups heavy whipping cream
  • ½ cup pitted olives
  • 8 oz. feta cheese, diced
  • 1 garlic clove, finely chopped
  • salt and pepper

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
  2. Cut the chicken thighs or chicken breasts into bite-sized pieces. Season with salt and pepper.
  3. Add butter to a large skillet and fry the chicken pieces, in batches, on medium high heat until golden brown.
  4. Mix pesto and heavy cream in a bowl.
  5. Place the fried chicken pieces in a baking dish together with olives, feta cheese and garlic. Add the pesto.
  6. Bake in oven for 20-30 minutes, until the dish turns bubbly and light brown around the edges.

Billy’s Notes:This was actually really good. A bit runny, but when you’re “hangry”, it doesn’t matter. I used the green pesto and it made it look more like barf than food, so might be worth trying the red pesto instead.

item.title: Keto Soup (The bomb)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb Hamburg
  • 1 block Cream Cheese
  • 1 can Hunt’s diced tomatoes
  • 1 box Beef Broth
  • 1/2 Green Pepper
  • 1/4 small onion

Instructions

Brown the hamburg, green pepper, and onion. Whip the cream cheese so it’s nice and smooth. Add the remaining ingredients until cream cheese is melted. Serve.

Billy’s Notes:Jude sent this one to me and said it’s awesome. I still haven’t tried it yet.

item.title: Keto-Friendly Pizza Dough
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 1/3 Cups shredded mozzarella cheese
  • 1/2 Cup almond flour
  • 2 Tbsp cream cheese
  • 1 egg

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F.
  2. Place thegrated mozzarella,almond mealandcream cheesein a microwave-safe bowl. Microwave on HIGH (100%) for 1 minute, stirring halfway, or until melted and combined.
  3. Add theeggand working quickly, beat vigorously with a wooden spoon until combined.
  4. Place the ‘dough’ between 2 pieces of parchment paper and roll to line a 32cm pizza tray.
  5. Remove the top piece of baking paper and slide the dough with the bottom piece of baking paper onto the pizza tray.
  6. Prick with a fork.
  7. Bake for 10 mins or until puffed and golden.

Billy’s Notes: This was excellent. MUCH better than any of that cauliflower-based rubbish you get from pizza places. Maya found this one, so hats off to her!

item.title: Keto Crackers
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup almond flour
  • 1/2 cup grated parmesan
  • 2 tsp water

Instructions

Add the almond flour and cheese to your food processor.

Pulse and combine.

Add the water one teaspoon at a time until small balls/clumps of dough start to form in the food processor.

Transfer the clumps of dough to a bowl and use your hands to form a ball of dough out of them.

Next, use a rolling pin to roll out 1/2 the dough at a time between 2 sheets of parchment paper.

item.title: Keto Cheesy Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
  • 1/2 cup shredded parmesan cheese (use 1 cup if you really like cheese)
  • 1 egg
  • 1 1/2 tsp garlic powder
  • Pinch of Italian seasoning
  • Parchment paper
  • Salt

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 F.
  2. Mix all ingredients together in a bowl and pat it out like you would pizza dough into parchment paper (not wax!)
  3. Sprinkle with some salt.
  4. Bake for approx. 15 minutes.
  5. Broil on high for 1-2 minutes to get a crisper top and brown edges.
  6. Cut with a pizza cutter and dip in some Raos marinara sauce!

Billy’s Notes:Just sent this one my way, so I haven’t tried it yet. She said it’s great though!

item.title: Keto Cheese Roll-ups
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 8 oz. cheddar cheese or provolone cheese or edam cheese, in slices
  • 2 oz. butter

Instructions

  1. Place the cheese slices on a large cutting board. Slice butter with a cheese slicer or cut really thin pieces with a knife.
  2. Cover every cheese slice with butter and roll up. Serve as a snack.

Billy’s Notes:The butter makes eating these make you feel dirty, but they’re a good way to get the fat in your diet for keto. I actually read one of the comments that made these better by laying down a slice of deli ham, then the cheese slice, butter, and then roll it around a pickle spear. I did that and it was better.

item.title: Keto Candy
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup + 2 tablespoonsbuttersalted
  • 1 cupgranulated sweetener of choiceI used allulose * See notes
  • 2/3 cups heavy cream
  • 1 teaspoonvanilla extractoptional

Instructions

Line and grease an 8 x 8-inch baking pan with tin foil and set aside.

In a deep pot or saucepan, add your butter and melt it over low heat. Once it has melted, add the sweetener, heavy cream, and vanilla extract, if using it, and bring it to a simmer. Once it begins to simmer, stir it regularly until it begins to bubble. Use a candy thermometer to check the temperature and once it hits 145C/290F, remove it from the heat.

item.title: Keto Cabrese Omelet
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 eggs
  • salt and pepper
  • 1 tbsp fresh basil or dried basil
  • 3 oz. cherry tomatoes cut in halves or tomatoes cut in slices
  • 5 oz. fresh mozzarella cheese
  • 2 tbsp olive oil

Instructions

  1. Crack the eggs into a mixing bowl, add salt and black pepper to your liking. Whisk well with a fork until fully combined. Add basil and stir.
  2. Cut the tomatoes in halves or slices. Dice or slice the cheese.
  3. Heat oil in a large frying pan. Fry the tomatoes for a few minutes.
  4. Pour the egg batter on top of the tomatoes. Wait until the batter is slightly firm before adding the mozzarella cheese.
  5. Lower the heat and let the omelet set. Serve right away and enjoy!

Billy’s Notes:This was pretty good. Heather said that the tomatoes removed the need to smother it with Ketchup, which is good.

item.title: Keto Meat Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

Pie Contents

  • ½ yellow onion, finely chopped
  • 1 garlic clove, finely chopped
  • 2 tbsp butter or olive oil
  • 20 oz. ground beef or ground lamb
  • salt and pepper
  • 1 tbsp dried oregano or dried basil
  • 4 tbsp tomato paste or ajvar relish
  • ½ cup water

Pie crust

  • ¾ cupalmond flour
  • 4 tbsp sesame seeds
  • 4 tbspcoconut flour
  • 1 tbsp ground psyllium husk powder
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 pinch salt
  • 3 tbsp olive oil or coconut oil
  • 1 egg
  • 4 tbsp water

Topping

item.title: Keto Butternut Squash Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 butternut squash peeled, seeded, cubed
  • 4 cupsbroth
  • 12 oz frozen cauliflower thawed & cooked until soft
  • ½ cup sour cream
  • ¼ cup heavy cream
  • 1 teaspoonsalt

Instant Pot:

Instructions

  1. Put the squash and broth in the instant pot.
  2. Cook on manual high for 15 minutes. You can quick release the pressure at this point or just let it release naturally if you prefer.
  3. Add the cauliflower. Puree with an immersion blender.
  4. Stir in the sour cream and heavy. Season to taste. Serve immediately or let the instant pot stay on warm.

Stovetop:

item.title: Instant Pot Lemon Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6-8 boneless chicken thighs , skinless or with skin* (Billy’s Note:I used 3 chicken breasts)
  • sea salt and pepper to taste
  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika
  • 1/2 teaspoonred chili flakesoptional or to taste
  • 2 Tablespoons olive oil
  • 3 Tablespoons butter (swap with ghee for paleo OR Whole30)
  • 1/2 small onion chopped
  • 4 garlic cloves sliced or minced
  • Juice of 1 lemon
  • 2-4 teaspoonsItalian seasoning
  • zest of half a lemon
  • 1/3 cup homemade or low sodium chicken broth **PLEASE SEE NOTES BELOW
  • 2 Tablespoons heavy cream OR coconut cream
  • Chopped fresh parsley and lemon slices for garnish , if desired

Instructions

  1. Season the chicken with salt, pepper, garlic powder, paprika, and chili flakes.

To cook in the Instant Pot:

item.title: Instant Pot Buffalo Chicken Meatballs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1.5 lb ground chicken
  • 3/4 cupalmond meal
  • 1 tsp sea salt
  • 2 garlic cloves minced
  • 2 green onions thinly sliced
  • 2 tbspghee
  • 6 tbsphot sauce
  • 4 tbspgheeor butter
  • Chopped green onions, for garnish

Instructions

  1. In a large bowl, combine chicken, almond meal, salt, minced garlic cloves, and green onions.
  2. Use your hands to combine everything together, but be careful not to overwork the meat.
  3. Grease your hands with ghee or coconut oil, then shape the meat into balls 1-2 inches wide.
  4. Set yourInstant Potto sauté setting and add 2 tbsp of ghee.
  5. ***Working in batches, gently place the chicken meatballs in the Instant Pot to brown them. Turn them every minute until all sides are brown.
  6. While the meatballs are browning, combine hot sauce and 4 tbsp of butter or ghee and heat them in the microwave or the stove top until the butter is completely melted. Use a spoon to stir. This is your buffalo sauce.
  7. Place all the browned meatballs in the Instant Pot, then pour the buffalo sauce evenly over the meatballs.
  8. Screw on the lid to the Instant Pot, make sure that the pressure valve is set to “sealing,” then set it to “Poultry.”
  9. Once the meatballs are finished cooking (about 15-20 minutes), the Instant Pot will beep. If you are eating right away, hit “Cancel” then release the pressure valve, making sure your hand is away from the opening where the steam escapes. If not, the Instant Pot will automatically switch to the “Warm” setting for the next 10 hours and the pressure will slowly lower on its own.

Billy’s Notes: I made the mistake of using Frank’s Buffalo Chicken Sauce for this when I made it. The sauce fell apart. Even though it tasted ok, it was pretty gross. Make sure to use actual Frank’s Original hot sauce. I also just used butter for where it called for (not ghee). They were good though. Both a really tasty hot sauce recipe AND a good keto meatball recipe.

item.title: Easy Keto Cordon Bleu
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Crumb Topping
  • 2 ounces pork rinds
  • 1/4 cup flaxseed meal
  • 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
  • Casserole
  • 2.3 pounds chicken breasts (about 3 pieces)
  • Salt and black pepper to taste
  • 8 slices ham (about 112 grams)
  • 8 slices Swiss cheese (about 152 grams)
  • 3 tablespoons butter, melted
  • Sauce
  • 3/4 cup mayonnaise
  • 2 tablespoons Dijon mustard
  • Optional: 2-3 tablespoons chicken broth

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
  2. Blend pork rinds, flaxseed meal, Parmesan cheese, garlic, and 1/2 teaspoon of salt in a food processor. Set aside.
  3. Take each chicken breast and halve them lengthwise so that you have six pieces.
  4. Lay the chicken into a 9x13" casserole dish. Season with salt and pepper.
  5. Place the slices of ham on top of the chicken.
  6. Layer the Swiss cheese on top of the ham.
  7. Spread the pork rind mixture over the top of the casserole. Drizzle on the melted butter and mix it into the crumb topping with a fork. The topping will clump together somewhat.
  8. Bake the casserole uncovered at 350°F for 40-45 minutes, until the topping has browned and sunk into the melty cheese. Test the chicken with a thermometer to ensure that the chicken has reached 165°F.
  9. While the casserole is baking, whisk together the mayonnaise and Dijon mustard. If you’d like for your sauce to be less thick then you can add 2-3 tablespoons of chicken broth. I like it either way.
  10. Cut the casserole into six pieces and serve with sauce.

Billy’s Notes:This was really good! I really liked the sauce and it can likely be used with other things.

item.title: Dairy-free Keto Latte
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 eggs
  • 2 tbsp coconut oil
  • 1½ cups boiling water
  • 1 pinch vanilla extract
  • 1 tsppumpkin pie spiceor ground ginger

Instructions

Blend all ingredients in a blender. Drink immediately.

Tip!

If you’re craving hot chocolate, or just want a plain latte, replace the spices with 1 tablespoon of cocoa or instant coffee. Voila!

Billy’s Notes:This wasn’t bad. Almost like a not-very-sweet, warm egg nog. That sounds gross, but it’s not bad. I think I’d try it with the cocoa next time.

item.title: Cloud Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 eggs, room temperature
  • 3 tbsp cream cheese, softened
  • 1/4 tsp cream of tartar
  • 1/4 tsp salt (Billy’s Note: Maybe cut even this in half - I found them salty)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 300℉ and line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
  2. Carefully separate egg whites from yolks. Place whites in one bowl and yolks in another.
  3. In the bowl of egg whites, add cream of tartar and salt. Using a hand mixer, mix together at high speed until stiff peaks form.
  4. In the bowl of egg yolks, add cream cheese and mix together with a hand mixer until well-combined.
  5. Pouring slowly, use a spatula or spoon to add yolk mixture to egg whites and carefully fold in until there are no white streaks.
  6. Spoon mixture onto prepared baking sheet about ½ to ¾ inches tall and about 5 inches apart.
  7. Bake in the oven on middle rack for 30 minutes, until tops are lightly golden brown.
  8. Allow to cool (they will likely be too crumbly directly out of the oven) and enjoy.

Billy’s Notes:These were decent, but they’re VERY airy and light. Good for a cold sandwich, like meat, but gets soggy with something like a fried egg. Note that there are a lot of interesting variations to these to jazz them up a bit. Some pizza ones, “everything bagel” ones, etc… Once I experiment with them, I’ll note them here.

item.title: Chunky Keto Guacamole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 Medium roughly chopped avocados
  • 1/4 cup finely chopped red onion
  • 1/4 cup roughly chopped cilantro
  • 3 heaped tablespoons lime juice
  • 1/4 cup chopped roma tomatoes
  • 1/2 tablespoon finely minced jalapeno
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/4 teaspoon pepper

Instructions

INSTRUCTIONS

  1. Add all ingredients to a large bowl and stir well to combine. Adjust seasoning if desired.

NUTRITION

Serving Size:1/4 cup

Calories:125

Fat:11g

Carbohydrates:Net Carbs: 2g

Protein:2g

Credit for this recipe goes to PerfectKeto.com:

item.title: Chaffle "Wonder" Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 12 Tbsp of egg white (this is equal to 4 Large eggs)
  • 4 heaping tsp of Mayonnaise
  • 4 Pinches of salt
  • 4 Tbsp of cream cheese (softened - can just microwave for a few seconds)
  • 2 tsp of baking powder
  • 1 tsp of sweetener (Stevia)
  • 8 Tbsps of almond flour
  • 2 tsp of psyllium husk
  • 2 tsp of water (if necessary - should be like a pancake batter consistency - I didn’t need to when I made this)

NOTE: I quadrupled this because the original recipe only makes 2 of them. The recipe measurements above will make 8 of them.

item.title: Big Mac Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 16 ounces of ground beef
  • 6 cups Romaine lettuce
  • 1/4 cup red onion, diced
  • 2 dill pickle spears, chopped
  • grate a bit of cheddar cheese and toss it in there too
  • Thousand Island Dressing
  • Salt and Pepper to taste (I didn’t use this)

Instructions

  1. Brown ground beef until cooked through.
  2. Chop Romaine lettuce into individual bowls.
  3. Chop red onion, pickles, and tomatoes and set aside.
  4. Pout Thousand Island Dressing on the lettuce as you desire.
  5. Mix the lettuce and dressing until evenly coated.
  6. Add ground beef and chopped vegetables to the lettuce and dressing and mix until evenly coated.
  7. Salt & Pepper to taste.

Billy’s Notes: I tweaked this a bit compared to the recipe linked below. Big Macs don’t have tomatoes and I grated some cheese on mine as well.

item.title: Buffalo Chicken Spaghetti Squash
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 whole spaghetti squash, about 3 pounds
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 2 cups cooked, chopped chicken
  • 1 cup shredded cheddar, divided
  • 1 cup shredded Monterey jack, divided
  • 2 ounces cream cheese, softened
  • ½ cup sour cream
  • 2 tablespoons ranch seasoning
  • ⅓ - ½ cup Frank’s buffalo sauce, to taste
  • Ranch and Buffalo sauce, for drizzling on top
  • Green onions, for sprinkling on top

To Cook The Spaghetti Squash:

Instructions

  1. To cook in the oven, preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Cut the squash in half lengthwise and scoop out the seeds with a spoon. Discard the seeds. Drizzle with olive oil and place face down on a baking sheet. Bake for 1 hour or until squash is fork tender.
  2. Alternately, makeInstant Pot Spaghetti Squash
  3. Once squash is cooked, shred the squash into long spaghetti-like strands by using a fork to scrape the squash widthwise.

For The Filling:

item.title: Baked Chicken Drumsticks
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 pounds chicken drumsticks
  • 2 tablespoons avocado oil
  • 1 teaspoon paprika
  • 1 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1 teaspoon onion powder
  • 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
  • ½ teaspoon salt
  • ½ teaspoon cracked pepper

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees. Spray a baking sheet with non-stick spray.
  2. Add all of the ingredients to a large zip top bag and seal. Smush the chicken around in the bag to coat in the seasoning.
  3. Arrange chicken on the baking sheet and bake for 40-45 minutes or until a thermometer reads 165 degrees.

Credit for this recipe goes to That Low Carb Life:

item.title: Bacon-Wrapped Broccoli-Stuff Chicken
item.description:
Bacon-Wrapped Broccoli-Stuffed Chicken

Ingredients

  • 4 chicken breasts
  • 4 oz cream cheese
  • ¾ tsp minced garlic
  • ¼ cup of parmesan cheese
  • 1 ½ cups of chopped broccoli
  • 8 slices of bacon

Instructions

  1. Mix everything but the chicken and bacon together in a bowl
  2. Cut a slit in each chicken breast and stuff them with the good stuff
  3. Wrap each breast with 2 slices of bacon
  4. Bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour

Credit for this great recipe goes to Sonya Howard, one of my camping pals!

item.title: Bacon, Cream Cheese, Cheddar Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 4 chicken breasts (use 4 thin chicken breasts, or use 2 large chicken breasts, sliced in half, horizontally)
  • salt and pepper
  • 6 oz cream cheese , cold, refrigerated, and sliced into 8 slices
  • 8 strips bacon , cooked, chopped
  • 1 cup Cheddar cheese , shredded

Instructions

  1. Preheat the oven to 400 F.
  2. Grease the bottom of the casserole dish with olive oil. I used the the oval casserole dish measured 13 inches x 9 inches x 4 inches deep.
  3. Add chicken breasts to the casserole dish. Note about chicken: use 4 thin chicken breasts, or use 2 large thick chicken breasts, sliced in half horizontally each to make 4 thin chicken breasts.
  4. Sprinkle chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
  5. Top with cream cheese. Cream cheese should be cold, right out of the fridge, sliced into 8 thin slices.
  6. Top with chopped cooked bacon (drained from fat).
  7. Top with shredded Cheddar cheese.
  8. Bake, uncovered, for about 20-30 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through. The cooking time will depend on the thickness of your chicken breasts.

Credit for this recipe goes to Julia’s Album (juliasalbum.com):

item.title: Avocado Egg Boats
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 avocados halved with pit removed
  • 4 medium eggs
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 400 F degrees.
  2. Line a baking sheet or baking pan with foil or parchment paper (for easier cleanup).
  3. Cut avocados in half and scoop an additional 1-2 tablespoons of the avocado flesh out to create a larger nest for the eggs.
  4. Position the avocado halves propped against the baking dish or touching the other avocados snugly to avoid spilling over. You can also place a small ramekin and prop them against the avocados.
  5. Gently crack one egg in each avocado half - if needed - save some of the egg whites in another bowl if the avocado well is too small.
  6. Bake for 13-18 minutes, or until the whites are set and the egg yolks are cooked to your liking.
  7. 13-14 min. - softer yolks
  8. 15-16 min. - medium yolks
  9. 17-18 min. - hard boiled yolks

Billy’s Notes: These were good. Jury’s still out on whether I like avocados straight up, but this puts a good coating of other goodness on them. I put salsa and shredded cheese on them and fired them in for another couple of minutes to melt it. Like a keto huevos rancheros!

item.title: 90-Second Keto Bread in a Mug
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tablespoon butter
  • ⅓ cup blanched almond flour
  • 1 egg
  • ½ teaspoon baking powder
  • 1 pinch salt

Instructions

Microwave butter in a microwave-safe mug until melted, about 15 seconds. Swirl the mug to coat with butter.

Add almond flour, egg, baking powder, and salt to the mug; whisk until smooth.

Microwave on high until set, about 90 seconds. Let cool for 2 minutes before slicing.

Credit for this recipe goes to AllRecipies.com:

item.title: Turkey Noodle Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 1 small onion, diced
  • 2-3 clove garlic, minced
  • 3-4 carrots, diced
  • 2 stalks celery, chopped
  • 2 Cups cooked turkey, shredded or diced
  • 7 Cups chicken broth
  • 6 oz noodles of your choice
  • 1 tsp dried basil
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Instructions

  1. In your Instant Pot, combine the onion, garlic, carrot and celery along with the butter.
  2. Push saute and let the veggies cook for 4-5 minutes.
  3. Turn off the saute function, and add the chicken, broth and noodles - place the lid on the Instant Pot and lock, ensure the valve is closed.
  4. Push manual (high) and set the timer for 4 minutes.
  5. The pot will take 8-10 minutes to come to pressure, then will count down the 4 minutes.
  6. Once it beeps, slowly release the pressure and open the lid.
  7. Add salt/pepper and basil to taste.
  8. Serve and enjoy!

This turned out pretty good. I’d give it a solid 4 out 5. I’d even try kicking in a can of diced tomatoes next time to see how that goes too.

item.title: Sushi Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups sushi rice
  • 2.5 cups hot water (the hot water will help the Instant Pot come to pressure faster)
  • 2 tbsp rice vinegar
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 1 tbsp kosher salt

NOTE: I rinsed my rice ahead of time. This particular recipe didn’t say to do so, but the rice comes out less “gummy” if you do (from what I understand, anyway!). Just put the rice in a big bowl and cover it with cool water. Swish it around with your hand a bit, then pour the now-murky water out. Do this as many times as it takes until the water drains clear.

item.title: Stuffed Cabbage Casserole
item.description:
• 1 lb ground beef or ground sausage

  • 3 1/2 c beef broth

  • 1 tbsp garlic minced

  • 1 c rice white, uncooked

  • 1 onion diced

  • 1 jar spaghetti sauce 20 oz

  • 1 can tomato soup 10 oz

  • 1 dash hot sauce optional

  • 1 tbsp worchestershire sauce

  • 1 tsp garlic salt

  • 1 tsp oregano

  • salt and pepper to taste

  • 2-3 c cabbage cut into small chunks 2x2 pcs

  • Turn Instant Pot to saute, normal. Add a bit of olive oil, ground beef and diced onion. Cook until beef is not pink and onions soften

item.title: Spinach Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 lb fresh spinach
  • 1 Tbsp olive oil
  • 1/2 C. chicken broth
  • 1/2 C.sour cream
  • 1/2 C. mayonnaise
  • 8 oz cream cheese, cubed
  • 1 C. shredded mozzarella cheese
  • 1 tsp onion powder
  • 1/2 tsp salt, or more to taste
  • 1/4 tsp pepper, or more to taste

Instructions

Instructions

  1. In your Instant Pot, push saute, and combine the minced garlic and fresh spinach. Saute until the spinach is cooked down.
  2. Drain the excess liquid.
  3. Leave the garlic and spinach in the Instant Pot and add the chicken broth,sour cream, mayonnaise, cream cheese, mozzarella, and onion powder.
  4. Give it a big ‘stir" to combine.
  5. Add the lid on the Instant Pot and close the valve. Set the timer for 4 minutes on high (manual) pressure.
  6. Once the 4 minutes is done, do a quick release and add your salt & pepper - or more as necessary.
  7. Pour the spinach dip into a bowl to serve immediately.

Notes

item.title: Ratatouille
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 small eggplant about 3 cups, cubed into 1/4-inch pieces
  • 2 tspkosher salt
  • 4 tablespoonsextra-virgin olive oildivided
  • 1 medium onion about 1 1/2 cups, diced
  • 2 zucchini about 3 cups, cubed into 1/4-inch pieces
  • 6 cloves garlic about 2 Tbsp, minced
  • 2 bell peppers about 3 cups, cubed into 1/2-inch pieces
  • 5 whole basil leaves plus 1 Tbsp thinly sliced basil leaves
  • 1/2 tspcrushed red pepperor to taste
  • 1/2 tsp freshly groundblack pepperor to taste
  • 1 Tbspcapers
  • 2 1/2 cups tomatoes chopped into 1-inch pieces
  • 1 Tbsp parsley chopped
  • 1 tsp good qualitybalsamic vinegar
  • Additional olive oil and balsamic vinegar for drizzling

Instructions

  1. Sprinkle salt on the eggplant, toss to coat and allow to drain in a colander set over a bowl.
  2. In ‘Saute’ mode, heat 2 Tbsp olive oil.
  3. Add onion, and cook until translucent, about 3 to 5 minutes. Stir occasionally.
  4. Add zucchini and garlic, saute 3 to 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
  5. Add bell pepper, saute 3 to 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
  6. Add whole basil leaves and stir.
  7. Press ‘Cancel’ and transfer contents of inner pot into a large bowl and set aside.
  8. In ‘Saute’ mode, heat 2 Tbsp olive oil.
  9. Add drained eggplant and saute until lightly browned, stirring frequently.Be sure to prevent it from burning.
  10. Add back in reserved vegetables, crushed red pepper, black pepper, and capers.
  11. Stir gently, making sure that there are no brown bits sticking to the bottom of the inner pot. *
  12. Top with tomatoes.
  13. Cook on ‘Manual’ (or ‘Pressure Cook’) mode for 10 minutes. Make sure pressure release handle is in ‘Sealing’ position.
  14. Do a quick release of pressure.
  15. Press ‘Cancel’ and open the Instant Pot.
  16. The ratatouille will have some liquid.
  17. In ‘Saute’ mode, simmer until the Instant Pot ratatouille has thickened up, about 6 to 8 minutes.
  18. Sprinkle with chopped basil and parsley, drizzle additional olive oil and balsamic vinegar over the top.
  19. Serve as a side dish, with pasta, with a medley of salads, or on a toasted baguette topped with goat cheese.

Billy’s Notes:

item.title: Popcorn
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 Tablespoons of coconut oil
  • 2 Tablespoons of butter
  • 1/2 cup popcorn kernels

Steps

Instructions

  1. Turn on SAUTE and press Adjust to raise temp to MORE.
  2. When display reads HOT, add coconut oil and butter.
  3. Wait about 1 minute then pour in corn kernels.
  4. Stir and make sure the fat is covering all the kernels. If not add more oil.
  5. Cover (I use the IP glass Iid) and in 2 minutes or so the corn will start popping.
  6. When about 2/3 of the corn has popped, turn IP off and let sit to finish popping.
  7. Add salt and any seasonings of choice.

NOTE: This turned out really good! Tasted like buttery movie theatre popcorn. Go easy on the salt though. I added a bit too much! I’d give this a 4.5 out of 5.

item.title: Faux-tisserie Chicken
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 1/2 to 3 pound whole chicken
  • 2 Tablespoons olive oil {divided}
  • sea salt & black pepper, to taste
  • 1/2 medium onion, cut into quarters
  • 5 large cloves fresh garlic, peeled and left whole
  • southwest seasoning mix {recipe below} or 2 Tablespoons of your favorite seasoning {lemon-garlic, paleo powder, etc.}
  • 1 cup chicken stock/broth, or water

Southwest Seasoning

  • 1 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1 teaspoon onion powder
  • 1 teaspoon chili powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon cumin
  • 1/2 teaspoon basil

Instructions

item.title: Mesquite Ribs
item.description:
INGREDIENTS

Ingredients

  • 1 cup water
  • 2 tablespoons cider vinegar
  • 1 tablespoon soy sauce
  • 4 pounds pork baby back ribs, cut into serving-size portions
  • 2 tablespoons mesquite seasoning
  • 3/4 cup barbecue sauce, divided

Instructions

DIRECTIONS

Combine water, vinegar and soy sauce in a 6-qt. electric pressure cooker. Rub ribs with mesquite seasoning; add to pressure cooker. Lock lid in place; make sure vent is closed. Select manual setting; adjust pressure to high, and set time to 35 minutes. When finished cooking, naturally release pressure for 10 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure according to manufacturer’s directions.Remove ribs to a foil-lined baking sheet. Preheat broiler. Brush ribs with barbecue sauce. Broil 4-6 in. from heat until glazed. Serve with additional barbecue sauce if desired.Yield:8 servings

item.title: Instant Pot White Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup long grain white rice (rinsed or unrinsed)
  • 1 1/4 cup low-sodium chicken broth
  • ½ teaspoon salt
  • ½ tablespoon butter

Instructions

  1. Add rice, chicken broth, salt, and butter to the pressure cooker pot and stir.
  2. Secure the lid and turn pressure release knob to a sealed position. Cook at high pressure for 3 minutes.
  3. When cooking is complete, use a natural release for 10 minutes and then release any remaining pressure.
  4. Fluff the rice with a fork.  Serve immediately.

Credit for this recipe goes to Insta Fresh Meals:

item.title: Instant Pot Spaghetti Squash
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 medium spaghetti squash (about 3 pounds)
  • 1 cup water

Instructions

Use a sharp knife to carefully cut the spaghetti squash in half.

Remove the seeds from the squash using a spoon or your hands.

Add the trivet to your Instant Pot and add 1 cup of water.

Place the spaghetti squash in the Instant Pot. If both halves do not fit at the same time, you’ll have to cook them separately. The lid must fully seal with the squash in the pot.

item.title: Instant Pot Rice (Basmati or Jasmine)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Cup Basmati (or Jasmine) rice
  • 1 Cup Water
  • Salt to taste (just a few shakes will do)

Instructions

  1. Put all the ingredients in your Instant Pot.  I give them a little stir for good measure.
  2. Set the IP for 6 mins and go chillax for a bit.
  3. Once those 6 mins are up, let it natural release for 10 mins.
  4. Likely good to get it out of the pot right when it’s done, as it tends to stick to the pot if you let it sit for a bit.

Cleanup is easy.  Just let your pot soak with water for 10 mins and it rinses right out.  Really good for butter chicken, stir fry, etc…  Comes out perfect.

item.title: Instant Pot Pulled Pork
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tablespoon Packed Brown Sugar
  • 2 Teaspoons Paprika
  • 2 Teaspoons Garlic Powder
  • 2 Teaspoons Onion Powder
  • 1 Teaspoon Salt
  • 1 Teaspoon Pepper
  • 1/2 Teaspoon Cumin
  • 1/4 Teaspoon Cayenne, Optional
  • 3 Pound Boneless Pork Roast, Cut into 3-4 inch cubes
  • 2 Tablespoons Olive Oil
  • 1/3 Cup Apple Cider Vinegar
  • 2 Cups Barbecue Sauce, Divided
  • 1 1/4 Cup Chicken or Beef Broth
  • 1 Teaspoon Hot Pepper Sauce, Optional

Instructions

  1. In a small bowl mix together the brown sugar, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, pepper, cumin, and cayenne.
  2. Season the cubed pork with the spice mixture and set aside.
  3. In a medium bowl whisk together the apple cider vinegar, one cup of the barbecue sauce, the hot pepper sauce if using, and one cup of the broth.
  4. Turn your instant pot to the saute setting. Add the olive oil and allow it to get hot.
  5. Add half of the cubed pork to the hot oil and saute for 5 minutes, turning to brown on all sides. Remove the browned pork to a plate and repeat with the remaining half of the cubed pork.
  6. Turn the instant pot off. Use the remaining 1/4 cup of beef broth and a wooden spoon to deglaze (scrape the brown bits) from the bottom of the pot.
  7. Add all of the meat along with the barbecue sauce mixture to the instant pot.
  8. Close the lid and set to sealing.
  9. Cook on manual high pressure for 40 minutes. Once the cooking time is up, allow the pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes, then do a quick release.
  10. Remove the meat from the instant pot and shred. Stir in the remaining cup of bbq sauce and serve on buns as desired.

Billy’s Notes: Heather made this one, and it was great! She didn’t use hot sauce and only 1/2 cup of the BBQ sauce at the end (that last step).

item.title: Instant Pot Chicken and Dumplings
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 tsp Olive Oil
  • 1 Tbsp Butter
  • 1 small Onion, diced
  • 1 Bay Leaf
  • 1 cup Carrots, chopped (about 2 or 3 carrots)
  • 1 cup Celery, chopped (2 stalks)
  • 3 large cloves Garlic, pressed or minced (about 1 Tbsp)
  • 1 tsp Salt (more to taste)
  • 1/2 tsp Pepper
  • 1/4 tsp Poultry Seasoning
  • 1/2 tsp Sage, dried
  • 4 sprigs Fresh Thyme, 2 tsp leaves (or 1/2 tsp dried)
  • 1 2" sprig Fresh Rosemary, 1 tsp leaves, stripped off and minced (or 1 tsp dried & crushed)
  • 4 cups Chicken Broth, low sodium
  • 1 large Potato, chopped
  • 1 1/2 lbs Chicken Thighs, and/or Breasts, cut in slightly larger than bite sized pieces
  • 1 cup Frozen Peas (or mixed vegetables)
  • 1/2 cup Heavy Cream
  • Box of Bisquick

Instructions

  1. Turn the pressure cooker on to the Sauté setting. When the pot is hot, add the olive oil and the butter. Then add the onion and bay leaf. Stir.
  2. Add the carrot, celery, and garlic. Cook for a few minutes, scraping the bottom of the pot to get up any brown bits (deglaze).
  3. Add the salt, pepper, poultry seasoning, sage, thyme, and rosemary. Cook for a few seconds to let the spices become fragrant.
  4. Add the chicken broth. Put a glass lid on the pot and let the contents warm up to barely a simmer.
  5. Add the potato and chicken and stir.
  6. Add the dumplings.  Make them according to the Bisquick package and then drop heaping teaspoonfulls into the broth (not too large as they need to cook through, and they expand).
  7. Cancel the Sauté function.
  8. Place the lid on the pot and lock in place. Turn the steam release knob to the sealing position.
  9. Press the Pressure Cook/Manual button (or dial) and then the + or - button (or dial) to select 7 minutes.
  10. When the cooking cycle has finished, do a Controlled Quick Release of the steam/pressure. Start by using a wooden spoon or other long handled utensil to turn the steam release knob towards Venting, and start letting the steam out gradually.
  11. Do this several times, and back off if any of the sauce starts to spew out with the steam. Then open it all the way when you feel certain only steam will be coming out. It will take a couple of minutes to fully vent.
  12. When the pin in the lid drops down, open the lid carefully, and stir in the frozen peas or mixed vegetables. You may need to push the dumplings aside.
  13. Stir in the heavy cream and put the glass lid on to let it sit and heat through for 3-5 minutes.
  14. Taste and adjust salt, if needed.
  15. Serve.

Credit for this recipe goes to Simply Happy Foodie:

item.title: Instant Pot Brussels Sprouts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 tablespoons of coconut oil
  • 1/2 cup of onion (yellow, white or even a few shallots would work best), chopped
  • 1.5 – 2 teaspoons of minced garlic
  • 3 strips of bacon, chopped
  • 1 lbs of Brussels spouts, outer leaves removed and cleaned (leave whole)
  • 1/2 cup water
  • Salt and Pepper to Taste
  • Butter, optional

Instructions

  1. Turn your Instant Pot on Sauté
  2. Add your coconut oil to the pot
  3. Add your chopped onion and minced garlic and sauté for a minute
  4. Add in your chopped bacon
  5. Sauté until your onions are translucent and bacon crisps up
  6. Add your prepared, whole Brussels Sprouts with a 1/2 of water
  7. Salt and Pepper to Taste
  8. Give it a quick stir to incorporate everything
  9. Put on your lid, making sure that your vent is closed
  10. Set on Manual for 3 minutes on LOW PRESSURE
  11. Once cooking is completed (listen for that beep) do a quick release
  12. Open your lid when ready and drop in a bit of butter and stir (optional)
  13. Drain out the liquid
  14. Plate those bad boys up and try not to eat them all folks!

Billy’s Notes: Alright, really important. Where it uses coconut oil, use something else!! These were good, but totally tasted like coconut. I love coconut. I love brussels sprouts. But not sure if the two mesh very well. I’m going to use butter or even just olive oil for that part next time.

item.title: Hard Boiled Eggs
item.description:
Ingredients

Prep Time

1 minute

Cook Time

5 minutes

Servings

1-6 eggs

INGREDIENTS

  • 1-6 XL Fresh Egg chilled (from the refrigerator)

INSTRUCTIONS

Instructions

  1. Fill the Instant Pot with one cup of cold water. Add the steamer basket, or trivet and steamer basket, egg stand (if using) and eggs.
  2. Close and lock the lid of the pressure cooker.
  3. Cook for 5 minutes at low pressure (if your pressure cooker does not have this setting, try do a test egg at the pressure cooker setting it has.
  4. When time is up, open the pressure cooker with the Normal release - release pressure through the valve.
  5. Open the pressure cooker and place the egg(s) in a container filled with cold water. Keep the water cool by bringing the container to the sink and running more cold water into the container for 1 more minute, to serve warm, or 3 minutes to cool down completely.
  6. Tap the two ends and the middle of the egg and peel delicately.

Billys Note:Tracey showed us an easy way to remember and do hard-boiled eggs in the IP. The 5-5-5 method. So just a cup of water, like the recipe above, in the Instant Pot. Then 5 mins on high. Then 5 mins natural release. Then 5 mins in cold water or an ice bath. Did them like this and they were perfect and easy to peel.

item.title: Holiday Baked Beans
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 poundDried Navy Beans

  • 1 TablespoonKosher Salt(to add to the soak water)

  • 3 cups Water for soaking

  • 6-8 slices Thick Bacon roughly chopped

  • 1 medium Onion roughly chopped

  • 3 1/4 cups Fresh Water

  • 1/4 cupMaple Syrup(the real stuff)

  • 1/3 cupMolasses

  • 1/4 cupLight Brown Sugar(packed)

  • 1/3 cupHeinz Ketchup

  • 1 TablespoonWorcestershire Sauce(secret ingredient, shhh)

  • 1 1/4 teaspoonsDry Mustard Powder

  • 1/2 teaspoonSea Salt

  • 1/4 teaspoonFreshly Ground Black Pepper

Instructions

Instructions

  1. Rinse and sort beans, removing any debris.  Add beans and salt to a bowl and cover with water, two inches above beans.  Soak for three hours.  Drain beans and rinse.
  2. Select the Sauté or Browning function on Pressure Cooker and allow to heat.
  3. Add bacon and onions to the Pressure Cooker cooking pot and cook until bacon begins to render, scraping brown bits from bottom of cooking pot. Add 1/4 cup of water and continue cooking until water has evaporated, bacon fat is rendered and onions are semi-soft.
  4. Add beans, water, Maple Syrup, Molasses, Ketchup, Brown Sugar, Worcestershire Sauce, Mustard, Pepper to the cooking pot and stir to combine.
  5. Lock on lid and close Pressure Valve.  Cook at High Pressure for 75-80 minutes.
  6. When Beep is heard, allow a 15 minute Natural Pressure Release and then open Pressure Valve to remove remaining Pressure.
  7. Discard any floating beans.
  8. Add the salt. Taste a couple of beans for tenderness and sweetness and add more Molasses, if a sweeter taste is desired.  If Beans are still hard, cook for another 15 minutes.
  9. Select the Sauté or Browning function and cook until desired consistency.

Recipe Notes

item.title: Garlic and Rosemary Mashed Potatoes
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients

  • 5-6 Large Potatoes Peeled and cubed You can use more or less with the same cooking time
  • 1 cup chicken broth
  • 3 cloves garlic
  • 1 sprig rosemary
  • 2 tbs butter
  • 1/4 cup milk

Instructions

Instructions

  1. Place cubed potatoes in the Instant pot with chicken broth whole cloves of garlic, rosemary sprig, and butter.
  2. Place IP on manual high pressure for 15 minutes.
  3. Do a quick release.
  4. Drain Potatoes if needed and add milk and butter
  5. Mash until creamy and smooth. I use my kitchen aid mixer or a handheld masher.

Here is a link to the actual website. Credit Adventures of a Nurse:

item.title: Corn on the Cob
item.description:
Ingredients

On Rack

  • 4 Ears Corn on the Cob (or more)
  • 1 cup Water

In Pot

  • 4 Ears Corn on the Cob (or more)
  • 1 cup Water
  • 1/2 cup Milk
  • 1 TablespoonSugar
  • 2 Tablespoons Butter
  • 1 teaspoonSea Salt

Instructions

On Rack

Instructions

  1. Add one cup of water to the Pressure Cooker cooking pot and place a trivet on top. Lay corn on trivet.
  2. Lock on lid and close Pressure Valve. Cook on Steam (High Pressure) for 1 minutes. If frozen, cook for 0-1 minute. After 5 minutes, do a Quick Release.

In Pot for Butter Flavor

item.title: Chicken Wings
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients

  • 1 1/3 pound chicken wings about 12 wings
  • 4 tbs taco seasoning
  • 1 1/2 tsp smoked salt

Instructions

Instructions

  1. In a shallow dish mix the spices together
  2. Roll each wing in the spices gently, the spice don’t need to cover the whole wing
  3. In your instapot- add 1 cup of water
  4. Add a steamer basket to the bottom of your instapot
  5. Add your wings to the steamer basket. They can touch but should not be on top of each other. Instead I turned my on their ends to fit the 12
  6. Close and lock the lid. Make sure the pot is set to sealing. Hit Manual and set for 10 minutes at high pressure.
  7. When the pot has cooked for 10 minutes at high pressure, carefully with a pan holder or some hand protection vent the pot.
  8. Once the pot has come back to regular pressure open the lid and remove the wings to a baking sheet. Broil wings for 10 minutes for crispy skin.
  9. Enjoy!

Here’s a link to the actual website. Credit to A Real Food Journey:

item.title: Chicken Pad Thai
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 tbspolive oil
  • 2chicken breasts, diced
  • 4 clovesgarlic minced(or 2-3 tsp of minced garlic)
  • 3 tbsplow-sodium soy sauce or tamari
  • 1/2 cupjarred pad thai sauce (I like Thai Kitchen brand)
  • 1 1/2 cupswater
  • 1/2 packagerice noodles
  • 1 cupCarrot matchsticks
  • 1/2 eachred and yellow pepper, sliced(We replaced these with a bunch of bean sprouts instead)
  • 4green onions, sliced
  • 1/3 cupchopped peanuts(We omitted these for obvious reasons - allergies aplenty!)
  • 1/3 cupfresh chopped cilantro

NOTE: We made a few changes to the original recipe.

item.title: Chicken (from frozen), Rice, and Carrots
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 and 1/4 cups long grain white rice
  • 2 cups unsalted chicken broth
  • 1 tsp. salt
  • 1/2 tsp. pepper
  • 4 (6-8 ounces each) individually-frozen, boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • 2 tsp. Italian herb blend, or your favorite dried herb blend
  • 5 -6 large carrots, peeled and cut into 1” pieces

Instructions

  1. Measure rice into bottom of the Instant Pot.
  2. Add chicken broth and salt and pepper, stir to combine.
  3. Lay the frozen chicken breasts on top of the rice. Season the chicken breasts with the Italian herb blend or your favorite herb blend.
  4. Lay carrots on top of the rice.
  5. Cover pot, lock on the lid, and set the steam release valve toSealing
  6. SelectPressure Cookand set to 10 minutes.
  7. When the cooking cycle has finished, allow the pot to sit for 5 minutes, and then do a quick release by moving the valve toVenting.

NOTES

You can make this recipe with fresh chicken breasts as well. Set the cooking time to 7 minutes. Allow the pot to sit for 5 minutes, and then do a quick release by moving the valve toVenting.

item.title: Chicken Broccoli and Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp Butter
  • 1½ - 2 Pounds Boneless Skinless Chicken Breast cut into cubes
  • 2 Cloves Garlic
  • 1 Small Onion
  • 1 Tsp Salt
  • 3/4 Tsp Pepper
  • 1 Tsp Garlic Powder
  • 1 and 1/3 Cups Long Grain Rice
  • 1 and 1/3 Cups Chicken Broth
  • ½ Cup Milk
  • 1½ Tbsp Flour
  • 1-2 Cups Fresh Broccoli cooked
  • 1 and 1/2 - 2 Cups Mild Shredded Cheddar Cheese

Instructions

Instructions

  1. Turn Instant Pot onto saute. Add butter and heat until hot. When hot, add chicken, onion, and garlic.
  2. Cook chicken mixture until onion starts to get translucent. Add rice, broth and seasonings. Stir well.
  3. Whisk together milk and flour and set aside.
  4. Cook on manual high pressure for 5 minutes. When the timer is up perform a quick release.
  5. Immediately add milk and flour mixture and mix until it is well combined.
  6. Add broccoli and cheese and stir until well combined. Serve immediately.

Here is a link to the actual website.  Credit to I Don’t Have Time for That:

item.title: Chicken Breasts
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients

  • 1 Tablespoon oil (I use avocado, but you can use coconut or canola - something with a high smoke point is best!)
  • 3 boneless, skinless chicken breasts (uncooked)
  • 1/4 teaspoon garlic salt per chicken breast (or you can use just garlic powder and regular salt)
  • dash black pepper
  • 1/8 teaspoon dried oregano
  • 1/8 teaspoon dried basil
  • 1 cup water

Instructions

Instructions

  1. Preheat the sauté function on the Instant Pot at the highest setting, and add oil to the pot.
  2. Season one side of the chicken breasts.
  3. After the display reads “hot,” carefully add the chicken breasts, seasoned side down, to the pot. I use tongs to avoid hot oil splatter.
  4. Add seasoning on the second side.
  5. Cook about 3 to 4 minutes on each side, and remove from pot with the tongs.
  6. Add 1 cup water to the pot (may need more for 8 quart pots), plus the trivet.
  7. Place the chicken on the trivet.
  8. Lock the lid, and cook on manual high for 5 minutes.
  9. Allow the chicken to naturally release for 5 minutes, and then quick release the rest.
  10. Remove the chicken from the pot, and allow to rest for about 5 minutes before serving for maximum juiciness.

Here is a link to the actual website. Credit to A Pinch of Healthy:

item.title: Beef Stew 2
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 1/2 - 2 pounds beef chuck roast , trimmed and cut into 1 - 2 inch chunks (OR beef stew meat if you can’t find chuck roast)
  • 1 -2 teaspoons sea salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
  • 1/2 teaspoon onion powder
  • 2 1/2 teaspoonItalian seasoning, divided
  • 1/3 cup flour , use gluten free flour or arrowroot starch if needed (leave out for keto)
  • cooking oil
  • 1 medium onion , cut into large chunks
  • 3-4 cloves garlic , minced
  • 6 Tablespoonstomato paste
  • 2 Tablespoons balsamic vinegar or red wine
  • 4 medium Yukon potatoes , peeled and chopped into 1 inch chunks (sub with cauliflower for low carb / keto)
  • 1 large sweet potato peeled and chopped into 1 inch chunks (swap with pumpkin for low carb / keto)
  • 3 medium carrots , peeled and cut into slices (leave out for low carb / keto)
  • 2 ribs celery chopped
  • 1-2 teaspoonsWorcestershire sauce
  • 1 bay leaf
  • 4-5 cupsbeef broth, homemade or low sodium
  • water as needed to cover the vegetables
  • 1-2 Tablespoons fresh chopped parsley for garnish
  • 1/2star anise, optional but adds SO much depth and flavor
  • salt and pepper to taste

TO THICKEN STEW (OPTIONAL):

item.title: Carrot Cake Oatmeal
item.description:
INGREDIENTS:

  • 1 tablespoon butter
  • 1 cup steel cut oats
  • 4 cups water
  • 1 cup grated carrots
  • 3 tablespoons maple syrup
  • 2 teaspoons cinnamon
  • 1 teaspoon pumpkin pie spice
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 3/4 cup raisins
  • 1/4 cup chia seeds

DIRECTIONS:

Add butter to pressure cooking pot, select Sauté. When butter is melted add the oats and toast, stirring constantly, until they smell nutty, about 3 minutes.

Add water, carrots, maple syrup, cinnamon, pumpkin pie spice, and salt. Select high pressure and set 10 minutes cook time.

item.title: Beef Stew
item.description:
Ingredients

Prep Time

10 minutes

Cook Time

60 minutes

Servings

6-8 servings

INGREDIENTS

  • 2 pounds beef stew meat
  • Stew Seasoning of your choice, amount adjusted for 2 pounds of meat, McCormick Stew seasoning was used as seasoning by original creator of this recipe, two packets were used.
  • 4 cups Water
  • 5 potatoes chopped
  • 1 cup carrot chopped
  • 1 onion chopped
  • 4 stalks celery chopped
  • 1 cup green beans raw

INSTRUCTIONS

item.title: BBQ Pork Ribs
item.description:
Ingredients

For Dry Rub

  • 1 tbsp dry mustard
  • 1 1/2 tbspsmoked paprika
  • 1 tbsp onion powder
  • 1/2 tspcayenne pepper
  • 1 tsp cumin ground
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1 tbsp brown sugar
  • 1 tsp black pepper ground
  • 2 tsp salt

For Ribs

  • 2 racks baby back pork ribs (about 3 to 4 lbs)
  • 4 cups apple juice
  • 1/4 cup apple cider vinegar
  • 2 tbspliquid smoke

For BBQ Sauce

  • 1/2 cup bbq sauce store bought
  • 1/2 cup ketchup
  • 1/4 cup whiskey
  • 1/4 cup brown sugar
  • 2 tbspWorcestershire sauce
  • 1 tbspliquid smoke

Instructions

  1. Prepare the dry rub by combining all the dry rub ingredients together in a small bowl.
  2. Remove the membrane from the back of the ribs with a paper towel.
  3. Season the baby back ribs with the dry rub generously on both sides.
  4. Place the ribs inside the Instant Pot, standing up and wrapping around theInstant Pot.
  5. Pour the apple juice, apple cider vinegar and liquid smoke inside the Instant pot, no need to stir. Close the lid (follow the manufacturer’s guide for instructions on how to close the instant pot lid). Set theInstant Potto the Meat/Stew setting and set the timer to 20 minutes.
  6. Once theInstant Potcycle is complete, wait until the natural release cycle is complete, should take about 15 minutes. Follow the manufacturer’s guide for quick release, if in a rush. Carefully unlock and remove the lid from the instant pot.
  7. While the ribs are cooking prepare the BBQ sauce. In a small saucepan add all the BBQ sauce ingredients and stir to combine. Simmer on low heat for about 20 to 25 minutes, the sauce should reduce a bit.
  8. Carefully remove the ribs from theInstant Potand place them onto a baking sheet. Brush them generously with the prepared BBQ sauce on both sides and place under the broiler for about 5 minutes. Keep the door to your oven open while broiling the ribs and keep an eye on them, because they could burn quickly.
  9. Serve with leftover BBQ sauce.

Credit to Jo Cooks for this recipe. Here is a link to their webpage:

item.title: Apple Crisp
item.description:
Ingredients

INGREDIENTS

  • 5 medium sized apples, peeled and chopped into chunks
  • 2 tspcinnamon
  • 1/2 tspnutmeg
  • 1/2 cup water
  • 1 tbspmaple syrup
  • 4 tbsp butter
  • 3/4 cup old fashioned rolled oats
  • 1/4 cup flour
  • 1/4 cupbrown sugar
  • 1/2 tsp salt

DIRECTIONS

Instructions

  1. Place apples on the bottom of yourInstant Pot. Sprinkle with cinnamon and nutmeg. Top with water and maple syrup.
  2. Melt the butter. In asmall bowl, mix together melted butter, oats, flour, brown sugar and salt. Drop by the spoonful on top of the apples.
  3. Secure the lid on the instant pot. Use the manual setting, and cook on high pressure for 8 minutes.
  4. Use a natural release. Let sit for a few minutes, the sauce will thicken.
  5. Serve warm and maybe top with vanilla ice cream.

Servings: 3-4

item.title: 5-Ingredient Mac 'n' Cheese
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb(s) elbow macaroni
  • Kosher salt
  • 1 (12-oz) can evaporated milk
  • 3 Tbsp unsalted butter
  • 3 cups shredded mild or medium Cheddar

Instructions

  1. Combine the macaroni, 4 cups water and 1 tablespoon salt in an Instant Pot® (see note). Follow the manufacturer’s guide for locking the lid and preparing to cook. Set to pressure cook on high for 4 minutes.

  2. After the pressure cook cycle is complete follow the manufacturer’s guide for quick release and wait until the quick release cycle is complete. Careful of any remaining steam, unlock and remove the lid and switch to low saute setting.

item.title: Seapie (Yan's)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pack of beef
  • 1 pack of pork
  • 1/2 turkey breast or 1 chicken breast
  • 1 can of beef consommé
  • 1 can of water
  • 1/2 medium onion
  • 2 tbsp lard
  • Bannock (double batch)  Recipe here:Bannock (Yan’s)

Instructions

  1. Lard up the sides of the cast iron dutch oven.
  2. Cut meat into bite-sized cubes and toss that shit into the pot.
  3. Chop up the stupid onions into small enough pieces so you won’t know it’s there.  Mix that devil’s veggie witht he meat chunks.
  4. Salt and pepper that bitch, but keep in mind the consommé is salty, so don’t overdo it.
  5. Throw in lard tbsps over top the meaty mix.
  6. Add in consommé and water.  Should be covering meaty mix but just barely.
  7. Mix up the bannock dough and form a flat circle the size of the pot.  It won’t shrink or grow, so make it pretty close.
  8. Slap that doughy deliciousness over to the meaty mix, then cut some air vent slits into it.  Just a few - no need to go full Dahmer on it.
  9. Splash a bit of water over the bannock so that it doesn’t burn or get drier than a bucket of sand.
  10. Put it in the oven at 350 degrees for about 4 hours or so.

item.title: Sweet Chili Heat Corn Salad (Sandra)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 an orange pepper
  • 1/2 a red pepper
  • 1/3 a yellow pepper
  • 1 can of corn
  • 1/4 cup of shredded cheese (optional)
  • 2 cups crushed Sweet Chili Heat Doritos
  • 2 Tbsp of mayonnaise

Instructions

Mix everything but the Doritos ahead of time.  When you’re ready to serve it, mix in the Doritos.

item.title: Sam's Roasted Red Pepper & Potato Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 red peppers
  • Half a red onion
  • 4 cloves of garlic
  • 2 medium sized potatoes (yellow or white)
  • A carton of vegetable broth
  • Salt & Pepper (to taste)
  • Cayenne pepper (to taste)
  • A gob of butter

Instructions:

Instructions

  1. Turn on your oven broiler to high.
  2. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spray with some cooking spray.
  3. Cut each pepper into fours and line on the baking sheet.
  4. Put in oven and let broil until skins start to turn black.
  5. Once skins have turned black, take out of oven and put into a large mixing bowl.  Place plate on top of it to trap the heat in.  This helps the skin soften so they are easy to remove.  Let the peppers stay in the bowl for 10 minutes.
  6. While you wait, mince your onion and garlic.
  7. Wash and peel potatoes and cut them into cubes.
  8. Once 10 minutes have passed, carefully peel the skins off the peppers.
  9. Once the skin has been peeled off, chop up the peppers.
  10. In a large soup pot, melt butter at the bottom over medium heat.  Combine your onion and garlic.  Sauté for about 2 minutes, until the onions become soft.
  11. Add potatoes and combine for about another minute.
  12. Add in red peppers and stir everything together for about a minute.
  13. Season with salt and pepper.
  14. Empty carton of vegetable broth into the pot, stirring occasionally.
  15. Let soup simmer on low to medium heat, until potatoes are soft.
  16. Carefully sample the broth and add cayenne pepper if needed for an extra bit of spice.
  17. Carefully remove from stovetop and use immersion blender to bend sopu to a velvety texture.

item.title: Pasta Salad (Nandini)
item.description:
Ingredients

Dressing:

  • 1/4 cup oil
  • 1/4 cup vinegar
  • 2 1/2 tbsp sugar
  • 1/4 concentrated orange juice
  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 1 tsp crushed red pepper
  • 1/2 tsp basil
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Other stuff:

  • Whatever pasta you want
  • crushed feta cheese
  • grapes (red & green)
  • red and green peppers
  • olives
  • celery

Instructions

  1. Mix all that stuff up.  That’s your dressing.
  2. Cook choice of pasta, drain, and add the dressing.
  3. Add the other stuff cut up however you want, however much you want, because I don’t have these kinds of details.  You can blame Heather if it tastes bad.  She wrote down the initial recipe.

item.title: Oregano, Sun-Dried Tomato, and Chicken Stuffed Baguette
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 (8 oz/250g) pkg cream cheese, softened
  • 2 cloves of garlic, minced
  • 2 tbsp dried oregano leaves
  • 1 tsp (or less) of black pepper
  • 2 cups cooked, diced chicken breast (about 2 5oz breasts)
  • 1 cup finely sliced strips of seeded zucchini (about 1 small)
  • 1/2 cup sun-dried tomatoes, reconstituted, thinly sliced
  • 1/4 cup mayonnaise
  • 1 1/2 tsp grated lemon peel (1/2 a lemon)
  • 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1 Parisan bread (baguette?)

Instructions

  1. Mix cream cheese, garlic, oregano, and black pepper together using an electric mixer until well blended.  Set aside.
  2. In a separate bowl, stir remaining ingredients together, except the baguette, and set aside.
  3. Slice the baguette lengthwise from the side but not all the way through.  Carefully scoop out bread from inside using a spoon.
  4. Completely coat inside of baguette with cream cheese mixture ensuring the edges are well covered, reserving 2 tablespoons of the mixture.  Fill baguette evently with chicken mixture.
  5. Close baguette, using the reserved cream cheese mixture to coat the cut edges.  Wrap very tightly with plastic wrap to keep baguette edges closed.  Chill in the refrigerator for 12 hours or overnight.
  6. Before serving, unwrap baguette and cut 1" wide slices with a serrated knife.  Transfer slices to serving platter and add garnish.

item.title: Jollof Rice (Yan's)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 bell peppers (ideally red, but I had 1 green and 1 red)
  • 3 roma tomatoes
  • 2 red onions (barf)
  • 2 scotch bonnet peppers
  • 3 garlic cloves
  • 1 tsp fresh ginger
  • Half a cup water
  • ⅔ cup vegetable oil
  • 1 can tomato paste
  • 1 tbsp curry powder
  • 2 tsp dried thyme
  • 3 chicken bouillon cubes
  • Bay leaves
  • 3 cups parboiled long grain rice
  • Salt and pepper to taste (be generous)

Instructions

  1. Roughly chop up bell peppers, tomatoes, 1 of the onions, scotch bonnet peppers, garlic and ginger. Throw that into a blender, add half a cup of water, and get it all liquidy.

item.title: Coconut Cookies (Persian-Style) (Farang's)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 1/2 cups of ground coconut
  • 1/3 cup of shredded coconut
  • 1 2/3 cups of powdered sugar
  • 2 large egg whites (approx. 1/4 cup)
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 1/3 cup neutral oil (such as sunflower or canola)

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, combine the finely ground coconut and powdered sugar.

  2. Add the egg whites directly to the mixture. There is no need to whisk them beforehand. Mix until a soft, smooth dough forms.

item.title: Cabbage Rolls (Gerry)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5 lbs of lean hamburg
  • 1 1/2 cans of tomato juice (48 oz)
  • salt pork (small) chopped
  • 3 heads of cabbage
  • 3 cups long grain rice, cooked
  • onions, salt and pepper

Instructions

  1. Boil cabbage, peel leaves off, and core.  Keep centre for top of cabbage rolls.
  2. Fry hamburg, onions, salt, and pepper in the oven at 350 degrees for a 1/2 hour untill cooked.
  3. Cut salt pork, while partially frozen, into small pieces.  Fry on low about 20 mins.
  4. Cook rice separately.  (Guess it’s up to you to figure out how!)
  5. Mix rice, hamburg, and salt pork together gently.
  6. Line a roast pan with foil.
  7. Don’t overstuff the cabbage leaves (about 1 to 1 1/2 tbsp of filling in each)
  8. Place cabbage rolls in the pan.  Put a little bit of tomato juice over the first later.  Layer 2 more times.
  9. Place leftover cabbage around outside of other rolls (so they won’t burn).
  10. Pour tomato juice over all.  Can put a few leaves on top of other rolls.
  11. Go around the edges with a knife to make sure there’s enough juice.
  12. Add tomato juice during cooking if needed.
  13. Cover with foil and put cover on the roast pan.
  14. Cook 1 hour at 350 degrees, then 2 hours at 300 degrees.

item.title: Bannock (Yan's)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 2 tsp brown sugar
  • 1 pinch of salt
  • Water, as needed

Instructions

  1. Mix ingredients into a bowl until mixed and a sticky dough forms. Can add a soupcon more flour to make it less sticky. But this is important, and I cannot stress this enough - DO NOT KNEAD. This is not bread. Do not overmix or spend too much time slapping this dough ball around, or the bannock will end up harder than Henry Cavill’s pectorals.
  2. Flatten out dough onto floured surface until its about a half-inch thick or so, then cut up into pieces. Don’t use rolling pin, use your hands like a peasant. Cut into whatever size pieces you think you’ll want. If you use larger pieces, you  might need to make holes in the middle to allow it to properly cook through.
  3. Deep fry in some oil until golden brown for best results. Can also be baked in the oven, or wrapped around a bannock stick and cooked over a fire. Feel free to throw in extra bits to your liking, like throw in some blueberries if you want more desserty bannock, or jalapenos if you are feeling spicy.

item.title: Rhubarb Strawberry Cake (Tracey)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 3 tbsp flour
  • 3 cups sliced strawberries
  • 3 cups diced rhubarb
  • 1 1/2 cups flour
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 cup margarine
  • 1 cup rolled oats

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
  2. In a large bowl, mix white sugar, 3 tbsp flour, strawberries, and rhubarb.
  3. Place mixture in a 9x13 pan.
  4. Mix 1 1/2 cups of flour, brown sugar, margarine, and oats till crumbly.
  5. Put crumble on top of rhubarb and strawberry mixture.
  6. Bake 45 mins in oven or until crisp and brown.

item.title: Pickled Eggs (Mom's)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups white vinegar
  • 1 1/2 cups of water
  • 1 1/2 tsp salt

Optional:

I like to jazz mine up a bit more so I put in:

  • A couple of cloves of garlic in each jar
  • A few sprigs of dill
  • A shake of crushed red pepper

Instructions

  1. Put all ingredients in a bot and boil for 10 minutes.
  2. Let this solution cool completely.
  3. Hard cook a dozen eggs (this solution will easily do 2 dozen eggs).
  4. Peel ’em and pack em into quart jars.
  5. Pour the cold vinegar solution in the jars, then seal.
  6. Let stand 2 days before crushing these delicious friggers.

item.title: Nougat Candy (Tracey)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 2 Bags of white chocolate chips (225g each)
  • 1 Bag white mini marshmallows (400g)
  • 1 1/2 cups chopped baking gumdrops (350g of jujubes)

Instructions

  1. Make large double boiler with pot and over proof bowl.
  2. Over simmered water melt butter, chocolate chips, and marshmallows.
  3. Stir in gumdrops.
  4. Transfer to oval baking dish lined with parchment paper.
  5. Pat down.  If too sticky use sprayed parchment.
  6. Leave in fridge overnight.  Cut next day.

item.title: French Toast Bake (Tracey)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup melted butter
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 loaf of Texas toast (no crusts)
  • 4 eggs
  • 1 1/2 cups of milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

  1. Melt butter in microwave.  Add sugar, stir and pour into a 9 x 13 dish.  Spread all over the bottom.
  2. Beat eggs, milk, and vanilla.
  3. Place layer of toast, then remaining egg mixture on top.
  4. Cover and chill overnight.
  5. Sprinkle with cinnamon.
  6. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.

item.title: Lemon Coffee Cake - Pep's Grandma
item.description:
Ingredients

Mix together all of the following:

  • 1 Lemon Cake Mix
  • 1 Pkg. Lemon Instant Pudding
  • 1 Cup of Water
  • 1/2 Cup of Oil
  • 4 Eggs

Topping:

  • 2 Tbsp of cinnamon
  • 1/2 Cup of Brown Sugar
  • 1/2 Cup of Chopped Walnuts (Optional) (Billy’s Note: They’re gross - don’t do it!)

Directions:

Instructions

  1. Grease 2 regular loaf pans.
  2. Sprinkle 1/2 of the topping mixture on bottom of the pans.
  3. Pour half of the cake mixture next.
  4. Add the rest of the topping mixture.
  5. Pour the rest of the batter on top.
  6. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 50 minutes.

item.title: Dilly Casserole Buns (J.M./Linda)
item.description:
Ingredients

Flour Ingredients (#1)

  • 1 (450g) container of cottage cheese
  • 4 Tbsp melted butter
  • 2 Tsp salt
  • 2 Eggs
  • 1 Cup water
  • 2 Tsp sugar

Wet Ingredients (#2)

  • 4 Tsp active dry yeast
  • 4 Tbsp dried onions
  • 4 Tsp dill seed
  • 5 Cups flour

Instructions

  1. Put cottage cheese in bowl.  Add eggs and hot water.  Whisk together.  Add salt and sugar.  Whisk warm mixture on stove stirring constantly until it is lukewarm.  Add the melted butter after it is done heating up.
  2. Use your hands to mix in flour ingredients (#2) to wet ingredients (#1).
  3. Dump out on floured board.  Knead dough using a little bit of flour as needed.  When you see it cracking on top, that’s enough kneading!!
  4. Grease a bowl, put the dough in it, and then turn if over (the bowl or the dough?  Not sure…lemme know if you make this and I’ll update the recipe)
  5. Cover with a tea towel and the put a black garbage bag on top of the tea towel.
  6. Let rise for about 1 hour.
  7. Roll into balls.  Cover a cookie sheet with parchment paper and put your balls on the sheet.  giggle  Cover with the tea towel and garbage bag and let them rise for about 1 hour.
  8. Bake at 400 degrees for 20 minutes.

item.title: Classic Pickled Eggs (Colette's)
item.description:
CLASSIC PICKLED EGGS

Ingredients

  • 12small eggs
  • 3 cupswhite vinegar
  • ¼ cupwhite sugar
  • 1 Tbspsalt
  • 2 clovesgarlic
  • Cut up white onions(optional)

Ingredients

  • 1 bay leaf(optional)

Instructions

Instructions

  1. Prepare the eggs:Place eggs in a saucepan and cover with water. Bring to a boil, remove from heat, and let eggs stand in hot water for 15 minutes. Remove eggs from hot water, cool under cold running water, and peel.
  2. Make the brine:Bring vinegar, sugar, and salt to a boil until sugar is dissolved. Remove from heat.
  3. Jar and refrigerate:Place peeled eggs, garlic, onions, and bay leaf in a 1 quart mason jar; top with the vinegar mixture. Seal the jar and refrigerate for at least 1 week before serving.

item.title: Air Fryer Panko Chicken Thighs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 lbs. Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs (approximately 4 thighs)
  • 2 large Eggs
  • 2/3 cup All Purpose Flour
  • 2/3 cup Cornstarch
  • 1 cup Panko Breadcrumbs
  • 1 teaspoon Paprika
  • 1 teaspoon Salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon Pepper
  • 1/2 tablespoon Garlic Powder
  • 1/2 tablespoon Onion Powder

Instructions

  1. In a large bowl, mix flour, cornstarch, and spices together.
  2. In a separate small bowl, whisk eggs.
  3. In a third bowl large enough to dip a whole chicken thigh, pour in the Panko bread crumbs.
  4. Coat a piece of chicken in flour mixture, then dredge in the egg wash, and finish coating it with the panko crumbs. Place on a clean solid surface, such as a plate or cutting board.
  5. Repeat for each piece of chicken.
  6. Pre-heat the air fryer to 350 degrees F. (1-2 minutes depending on your air fryer model).
  7. Spray your air fryer basked and chicken thighs with olive oil spray. The main reason for nonstick cooking spray is it will help the chicken thighs crisp up more and keep them from sticking to the basket.
  8. Working in batches, place the thighs in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
  9. Air fry the chicken on 350F for 12 minutes. Use tongs to flip chicken and air fry for another 3-5 minutes to make sure all sides of the chicken are nice and crisp. Check the chicken with a meat thermometer to make sure the internal temperature reaches 165F.

Credit for this recipe goes to TopRockin.com:

item.title: Air Fryer Chicken Thighs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5-6 boneless skinless chicken thighs patted dry
  • 1 teaspoongarlic powder
  • 1 teaspoonsmoked paprika
  • ½ teaspoonoregano
  • ½ teaspoonsalt
  • ¼ teaspoonblack pepper

Instructions

  1. Pat chicken thighs dry with a paper towel, then transfer to a bowl.
  2. Combine all the seasonings together in a bowl, then pour them into the bowl with the chicken and use your hands to coat the chicken with the spices.
  3. Preheat your air fryer to 380° (mine took 3 minutes to preheat). Then, place chicken smooth side down into the air fryer, spray with nonstick spray/olive oil spray, and cook at 380° for 12 minutes. NOTE: every air fryer is different, so cook times may vary.
  4. Once the air fryer beeps, use kitchen tongs to flip the chicken over, and cook for an additional 6-8 minutes on the second side, or until chicken is cooked through.  (Billy’s note:  I only put them in for 4 additional mins instead of 6-8.  I think they’d have been too dry with that, so gauge them when you flip them.)

Credit for this recipe to goes to The Almond Eater:

item.title: Air Fryer Mashed Potato Balls
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups mashed potatoes (preferably made with butter and cream)
  • 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 cup panko breadcrumbs
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder (optional)
  • 1/2 teaspoon onion powder (optional)
  • Olive oil spray (or any cooking spray)

Instructions

  1. Prepare the Mashed Potatoes:If you haven’t already, prepare mashed potatoes. Ensure they are smooth and slightly on the thicker side (not too runny).
  2. Mix the Ingredients:In a large bowl, combine the mashed potatoes, shredded cheddar cheese, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder (if using). Stir until the cheese is evenly distributed.
  3. Form the Balls:Scoop about 1 tablespoon of the mashed potato mixture and roll it into a ball. Continue until all the mixture is used. You should be able to form 12–16 balls, depending on their size.
  4. Coat the Balls:Set up a breading station:In one shallow dish, place the flour.In another dish, beat the egg.In a third dish, add the panko breadcrumbs.Roll each mashed potato ball first in the flour, then dip in the egg, and finally coat it in the panko breadcrumbs. Press gently to ensure the breadcrumbs stick well.
  5. Preheat the Air Fryer:Preheat your air fryer to 375°F (190°C) for about 3-5 minutes.
  6. Air Fry the Mashed Potato Balls:Lightly spray the air fryer basket with olive oil.Place the potato balls in the air fryer basket, leaving some space between them for air circulation.Lightly spray the tops of the balls with more olive oil.Air fry at 375°F for 8-10 minutes or until they are golden brown and crispy, flipping them halfway through the cooking time for even crispiness.
  7. Serve:Once golden and crispy, remove the mashed potato balls from the air fryer. Let them cool slightly before serving.You can serve these with a dipping sauce, such as sour cream, ranch, or ketchup!

Tips:
